chapters.title: Chapter 401: Craniotomy
chapters.body:
Chapter 401: Craniotomy

Chapter 401: Craniotomy

A month later.

In the ICU ward on the first floor of the central hospital of Eco Science City.

A thin old man with a gray complexion and bones that were poking through his skin was carted out of the ICU ward and into the operation room.

Inside the operation room, apart from the three nurses, there were also four family members of the patient observing worriedly. One of them was an elderly in his sixties while the accompanying members appeared to be this man’s family members.

They watched on with solemn expression as the three nurses pushed the patient further into the operation room. They quickly trailed after the nurses until they arrived at the entrance of a sterile operation room.

“I’m afraid you folks cannot enter.”

It was then when one of the nurses stopped them. “The environment inside is sterilized, any one of you entering may risk having it contaminated.”

“But it’s my grandfather inside there.”

One of the family members in his thirties with a large mohawk on top of his head protested. He wore tight jeans and had a septum piercing that was impossible to not notice. He turned around and noticed another door beside the operation room. When one of the nurses entered the room, he caught a glimpse of an observation window that granted a view into the operating room. He immediately yelled, “Hey, what are you guys trying to do?”

“What is it, Bruno?”

The elderly man turned to the shouting man.

“Dad, they have an observation room right next to the operation room, I saw one of the nurses entering!”

The man with the septum piercing started making a commotion and tried to force his way into the observation room. He was promptly stopped by two security guards that looked at him with an icy, blank stare.

“Hey buddy, it’s my grandfather inside the operation room, what right do you have to stop me from entering?”

The septum-piercing man pushed off the security guards’ hands and continued protesting.

However, the two security guards did not offer any explanation.

“Is this how it’s going to be?”

The elderly man turned to the nurses that stopped them from entering. “Are we not allowed to observe the operation of my father, Watson?”

“This...”

The nurse stuttered for a moment and became visibly troubled by the elderly man’s pleads. “Mr. Qian will be watching the operation today as well, never mind... I’ll head inside and ask for his input.”

After that, the nurse stepped inside the observation room.

Within half a minute, the nurse stepped outside again, this time nodding to the group. “Mr. Qian has permitted your entry. I’ll have to ask you all to stay silent.”

“Mr. Qian my butt...”

The man with the septum piercing called Bruno complained under his breath. His insolence was immediately interrupted by the elderly man. “Bruno, this isn’t your Chicago third-rate biker gang gathering, you better watch your language.”

With that, the group was escorted by the nurse into the observation room.

The first thing that greeted the elderly man upon entering was a large and speckless floor-length window to his left. On the other end of the window was none other than the operation room. The window gave them a completely unobstructed view into the operation room.

The observation room was furnished with two long sofas and a coffee table. There were already three people seated separately on the sofa. They appeared to be discussing something among themselves.

The elderly man’s gaze quickly focused on one of the men in his mid-forties on the sofa. His eyes became widened with shock when he registered who this was. “Uncle Felix... Is that you?”

This immediately caught the attention of the man sitting on the sofa. When he looked up and saw this graying old man in front of him, he could not help but find the humor in the situation. “Is that Watson Jr? It must have been two decades since we last met, hasn’t it?”

“It is you...”

The elderly man became extremely sentimental. He immediately dragged Bruno by the arm and approached closer. “Thank you for your guardianship, if not for you, the chances of my father coming to Eco Science City is next to nil. The medical fees here are too high... This is my son, Bruno.”

“I remember Bruno was barely a teenager when I last met him, I can’t believe he’s a fully grown man now.”

Felix nodded and greeted, his expression was full of kindness.

There was a bizarre scene rarely witnessed happening inside the observation room — an elderly man with a head full of white hair was addressing someone in his mid-forties with such dignity and respect despite being the senior. Despite that, the people witnessing this were hardly thrown off by the strangeness of this interaction as if this was nothing out of the ordinary.

There was a major shift in social norms ever since the birth of the “age reversal treatment” project. Appearances were no longer the deciding factor of a person’s age.

“Mr. Felix...”

Bruno had finally wisened up at this point as well. Despite being an uneducated punk, he was at least familiar with the fabled Munster family. Despite being a relatively new family, they were on equal standing as the other old guards in the business world. Word on the street was that the most reputable biker gangs in Chicago had many ties with this family as well.

“Hello, Bruno.”

Felix nodded politely before it suddenly occurred to him to introduce the young man sitting next to him to this new group of visitors. However, the young man quickly shrugged, signaling that he did not want to be introduced.

Felix decided to introduce the man seated on the opposite sofa instead, “This is the man in charge of Eco Science City, he’s also the CEO of Blackwatch’s African branch, Mr. Qian Wenhuan.”

“Your reputation precedes you, I’ve seen you on television.”

The elderly man was surprised by this sudden introduction. He quickly stepped forward and shook hands with Qian Wenhuan, barely bothered by the fact that this person was incredibly young and could not be anywhere close to his forties. While he went for the handshake, he inadvertently glanced at the young man seated next to Felix.

The young man seemed to be the youngest of the trio of men sitting on the sofa. Despite that, the acute elderly man could tell that this young man held the highest rank among the trio.

“Could this man be none other than...”

The elderly man quickly stopped his stream of thoughts as he said this, his heart clenching. Since this man motioned for Felix to not introduce himself, the elderly man knew better than to expose his identity either. After shaking hands with Qian Wenhuan, the elderly man only nodded courteously at the young man’s direction.

The young man returned his gesture with a subtle smile.

After that, Felix enthusiastically invited the group to sit with them on the sofa and started making small talk with everyone. It was only after the preparations in the operation room were complete when everyone directed their attention to the ongoings behind the observation window.

Dozens of medical staff filed into the operation room one after another. They connected James Watson who had been in a coma for a month at this point to electrocardiogram and BIS machines. After that, they injected James Watson with an appropriate dosage of tranquilizers to put him into a deep slumber.

After injecting the tranquilizer, one of the nurses stepped forward with a shaver in hand. The nurse was extremely proficient with using the shaver and quickly shaved a bald patch on James Watson’s fractured skull. After that, they disinfected the shaved region appropriately again.

After shaving, several other nurses stepped forward and began making adjustments to the operation table. With moments, the operation table was craned upward like a chair and put James Watson in a position where he can sit upright.

Two more nurses proceeded to bring in the operation scaffold and set it over the top of James Watson’s head. It was set in a way that only the top part of his head that was riddled with operation scars was exposed through a ring section of the scaffolding. After that, they threw a sheet of cloth over the scaffolding.

With that, the top of James Watson’s skull was completely exposed to the doctors in the operation room as well as the gathered crowd inside the observation room.

The previous wound had already been stitched up during the previous brain trauma operation, which meant that this was technically the second craniotomy operation James Watson would be going under. The doctor sliced across the guided lines on his scalp left behind from the previous stitching while another doctor used electrocoagulation forceps to stop the bleeding. After that, they lifted the scalp and drooped it over his face.

The next part was a horrifying sight to behold. James Watson’s scalp was laid over his face casually, revealing the bloody skull underneath with plenty of cracks, fractures, and many other operation scars...

“Prepare for craniotomy. Get the hemostatic forceps and norepinephrine at the ready!

“Pay attention to the BIS machine and make sure the patient remains unconscious throughout this procedure...”

The audience gathered in the observation room stopped chattering when they heard the attending doctor started issuing a sequence of orders. Among them, Qian Wenhuan and Felix got up on their feet and approached the observation window, determined to not miss out on any detail.

With James Watson’s skull layer under his skin exposed, the doctor started using a drill to unscrew the previously installed skull clamp and lifted off a large chunk of the skull as if it was a mere building block.

Underneath the skull was a layer of cloudy dura mater membrane. The doctor slowly and carefully sliced through the slightly wiggling membrane layer to finally reveal the naked damaged brain for everyone to see.

With the mangled brain finally presented, not only the family of James Watson, even Qian Wenhuan and Felix could not help but hold their breath at this sight.

Despite having gone through an operation previously, the brain still looked as if it had been torn apart by a rabid dog. Its general structure was left in an extremely disorderly state.

The missing parts of the brain had been destroyed by the bullet shell and had to be removed during the previous operation. James Watson’s brain looked like a case of tofu left unattended in a wildly swerving vehicle. It did not have what should be a regular round shape expected of a brain.

To still be alive despite all this, such staggering life force...

Everyone was amazed at the tenacity of his life force.

However, the crew of doctors was unfazed by this since they had been informed of James Watson’s current state from the CT scan images provided. “The brain structure is exposed, no signs of infection or deterioration detected and we’re ready for implantation ... NSR implantation equipment is ready!”

Following the chief surgeon’s orders, a piece of equipment shaped like a helmet placed in the corner of the operation room was carted to the middle.

After the device was wheeled in, a doctor oversaw the process and placed the device over James Watson’s head. A row of laser beams immediately appeared from the device and rapidly scanned through James Watson’s brain before disappearing again.

What was this, laser mapping?

That was what everyone thought as they glanced at Qian Wenhuan curiously. To their surprise, even Qian Wenhuan was unsure of what this was since this was the first time he saw this device as well. Surely enough, his boss had personally mobilized the top engineers of the industrial park to design this device. This device was known as “NSR Implanter”...

Before everyone could get a closer look at this device, something resembling a sewing machine slowly descended from above, slowly inching toward James Watson’s brain.

From a certain angle, one could see tiny little chips the size of fingernails attached to the ports on the sewing machine.

After that, the device that looked like a sewing machine began to operate at a rapid pace on James Watson’s brain in a manner very similar to tailoring clothes. The movements it made were extremely subtle and could only be noted upon very close inspection.

“What’s it doing?”

Bruno turned to his father with a puzzled look.

“If I had to wager a guess, fixing James Watson’s brain.”

Felix shrugged as he turned to the Chinese young man also watching over the operation. “Am I right, Sir Chen Chen?”

The Chinese young man who had been silent all this time was none other than Chen Chen. He turned to the confused crowd and nodded. “Yes, this technology utilizes a specially designed AI chip to replace the defective region of the brain. It connects part of the brain’s nerves through nano-scale electrodes, so the chip integrates itself as a part of the brain, which helps repair the damaged function of the brain.”

“How miraculous!”

Watson’s son — the elderly man was amused by this technology. Just like his father, he was an expert in the biological science field. Although he was not as renowned as his father, he could still hold his own when it came to general biological knowledge.

Of all people present at the scene, he was more aware than the rest of them what this piece of technology could imply...

This technology would revolutionize the neurology field!

“Alright, it’ll keep doing this for more than ten hours to come.”

Chen Chen headed for the exit. “If I were you guys, I’d wait outside until the chip has been fully implanted into Watson’s brain.”

“Why does it take this long?”

Bruno asked instinctively. However, Chen Chen could not be bothered to give him an answer and left immediately after.

“Bruno, Sir Chen Chen has explained everything clearly.”

The elderly man looked at his good-for-nothing son and frowned. To prevent this insolent son of his from causing any further trouble, he had to explain, “Do you know how many neurons are there in the brain? Even saying there are hundreds of millions of them would be an understatement. Sir Chen Chen mentioned that they were nano-electrodes, this means there may be tens of thousands of nano-scale electrodes on a single piece of chip the size of a fingernail.

“With each maneuver, the device inserts one electrode into the brain and connects it to a specific nerve point in the brain. This is a very delicate practice and considering it implants a single electrode approximately ten times per second, do you now understand why the entire process would take more than ten hours due to the sheer volume of electrodes?”

Bruno still wanted to say something, but was shot down by his father’s dismissive glare.

Felix smiled. “Alright, at any rate, let’s wait outside. Let’s see if Eco Science City can give James Watson and us a surprise after ten hours.”

“Sir Felix, this way, please. The rest of you can come with me as well. I’ll arrange a room for you to rest.”

After hearing Felix’s remark, Qian Wenhuan immediately took action and escorted the guests out of the observation room.

Inside the operation room, the crew of doctors and nurses slowly shuffled out of the room as well. All that remained were two nurses stationed in the room to monitor the operation just in case something went wrong.

After everyone had left, the lights in the operation room were turned off as well. The original brightly lit operation room where not a single spot of shadow could be seen was now drowned in darkness. The machine resting on top of James Watsons’ head continued operating restlessly...

chapters.title: Chapter 402: Setup
chapters.body:
Chapter 402: Setup

Chapter 402: Setup

The practical implantation of nano-scale electrodes took far longer than expected.

Felix was extremely patient. He was the founder of the Munster Consortium and although his son Adrian was the one acting as the core founder of the group and he had long since retreated to the sidelines, many people in North America still widely regarded Felix as the true founder of the Munster Consortium.

Only two years ago, Felix, who was in his eighties at that point, had been diagnosed with Alzheimer’s. His son Adrian began searching everywhere for a cure for his father and ended up investing in Osmond corporation that seemed to have high potential in coming up with a cure. This was how he stumbled into Blackwatch who was trying to buy Osmond’s shares at the time.

It was around that time when Chen Chen directly approached Adrian. After some dialogue between the two of them, not only did Chen Chen agree to yield his shares in Osmond, he also recruited the Munsters into God’s Forbidden Zone on one condition. That was that he would cure Felix of his Alzheimer’s.

The workings of fate were always so unpredictable.

Two days later.

With Qian Wenhuan accompanying him, Felix brought James Watsons’ family on a tour around Eco Science City. In addition to visiting the major parks and playgrounds in the city, they also visited the vast industrial park and the space launch base that was still in the midst of construction.

In the end, the group of six arrived at the Sandwich Harbor, which was where most of the sanatoriums of the Eco Science City were located in.

As he saw the rows after rows of sanatoriums erected across the horizon as far as the eye could see, James Watson’s son, Watson Jr., found himself inexplicably impressed while also filled with envy.

It was his greatest desire to come here for its fabled treatment. The only thing stopping him was the medical fees totaling up to millions of dollars.

“Watson Jr, have you not considered undergoing one of our treatments available at our sanatoriums?”

As if sensing the desire in his gaze, Qian Wenhuan who was walking at the back of the group suddenly commented, “You’re at the ideal age for the age-reversal treatment. Don’t forget about our stem cell therapy as well. On top of restoring your body to its prime condition and getting rid of all the scars, we can also grant you a youthful appearance.

“We also equip the world’s most advanced stem cell facial surgery technology. With the stem cell facial surgery, you’ll no longer experience any side effects of aging and you can say goodbye to any surgical facelift for the rest of your life.

“Then there’s also our biological enhancement program. It doesn’t matter what your current physique is, we can transform your body and turn you into the next American male model within a week...”

Watson Jr. could only afford to muster a strained smile as he listened to Qian Wenhuan’s enthusiastic introduction. “I’m very sorry Mr. Qian. If I could, I’d come to your city to indulge in the most sophisticated and advanced medical treatment program in the world. Unfortunately, I can hardly afford any of the treatments available here.”

When Qian Wenhuan heard this, his eyes instinctively darted at the absent-minded Bruno nearby.

From Qian Wenhuan’s perspective, this elderly man standing in front of him was unmistakably the heir of a world-renowned scientist. James Watson himself once served as the chairman of Cold Spring Laboratory, boasting a salary of 500,000 US dollars per year. Factoring in his many accumulated accolades in his youth, his net worth should still easily range above millions even after he was expelled from his position.

As things may seem now, the current state of the family was a shadow of its former self. There was the issue of James Watson running his mouth with bold and out-of-touch claims, but the true downfall of this family probably stemmed from none other than this hippie-looking fellow over here...

Qian Wenhuan smiled and made no further comments despite having noted this. Just as he was about to divert the conversation to something else, he suddenly heard Felix speaking, “Mr. Qian, I’d be more than willing to pay for Watson Jr.’s medical fees.”

“Uncle Felix...”

Watson Jr. was taken aback by this sudden suggestion, he immediately became overwhelmed with emotions. “You’ve already paid for my dad’s medical fees, how could I...”

“No problem. Compared to your father’s medical fees, yours will only be a drop in the bucket.”

Felix shrugged. “Besides, if your father agrees to join Blackwatch after he has recovered, I doubt I’ll even have to foot the bill at all.”

“So that’s how it is.”

Watson Jr. suddenly got a better grasp on the situation. He quickly turned to Qian Wenhuan and asked, “Mr. Qian Wenhuan, could I know if your company is still hiring? I was also in the biology field.”

“Of course, Blackwatch welcomes anyone to join our ranks.”

Qian Wenhuan smiled amiably and nodded. “But researchers will have to pass a certain set of tests to prove themselves worthy. Since you’re the son of a Nobel Prize winner, I’d say these tests will be a breeze for you.”

As the group was discussing this, Qian Wenhuan’s secretary suddenly approached him and whispered something in his ears. Shortly after, Qian Wenhuan nodded before turning to the rest of the group. “Sir Felix, everyone, I have good news for you all. James Watson’s operation has been completed.”

Everyone was visibly pleased by this announcement. Felix quickly added. “Let’s head back right away then. I’m sure Watson Jr. and the others are eager to know how Watson is doing now.”

“Yes, I look forward to seeing the results.” Watson Jr. agreed.

“Hm, I believe there might be some slight misunderstanding here.”

Qian Wenhuan quickly waved his hand side to side. “This is only the preliminary stages. It’s still largely ambiguous whether James Watson’s operation could be deemed a success and how he would recover. It will still take some time until Sir James Watsons wakes up.”

“I see.”

Felix commented, not without slight disappointment in his voice, “I’d say it’s still worth seeing for ourselves, let’s head back.”

“Alright.”

Qian Wenhuan nodded and gave a signal. Several black cars quickly approached the group.

While everyone was getting into their respective vehicles, Bruno, who had not been paying attention all this while, suddenly approached Qian Wenhuan with a sly smile. He poked fun at Qian Wenhuan in a hushed tone. “Buddy, I never thought that you’d hire a male secretary. Don’t tell me that you’re... You know?”

Qian Wenhuan’s expression immediately turned dark. Bruno’s remark seemed to have triggered a traumatic memory in him.

“Bruno, get over here now!”

Watson Jr. who had just entered his vehicle rebuked Bruno immediately.

Bruno twitched his lips to the side like a brat before entering the vehicle.

Half an hour later, when the group returned to the central hospital of Eco Science City, they saw James Watson who already had his wounds restitched together.

His appearance was largely the same as it looked previously. His complexion was still uncannily pale and green, large arrays of machines were positioned next to him and he did not seem to be anywhere near conscious yet.

“The operation was a great success.”

One of the main surgeons announced enthusiastically to them, “We’ve inserted a total of six AI chips into the patient’s brain. These chips will do an excellent job at substituting the damaged region of the patient’s brain and allow for advanced computing abilities, rest easy.”

“Really?”

Watson Jr. was exhilarated by this revelation. “Will my father wake up?”

“It’s very optimistic by the current looks of it.”

The doctor responded and nodded before turning around and left.

“Congratulations, it seems your dad will recover very soon.”

Felix congratulated and joked a little. “I’ve been here long enough and I suppose it’s about time I take my leave. When your father wakes up, remember to tell him to visit me otherwise I won’t ever acknowledge knowing him as a friend.”

“You have my word, Uncle Felix.”

Watson Jr. thanked him earnestly. “I’ll make sure to pay you a visit along with my father.”

Felix nodded and turned to Qian Wenhuan. “Mr. Qian, thank you for your hospitality these past few days. Please help me inform Sir Chen Chen that I’ll be leaving today. Also, make sure to send me the bill for Watson Jr.’s treatment.”

“Take care, Sir Felix.”

Qian Wenhuan nodded in response.

Everyone watched in silence as Felix departed. The silver-haired Watson Jr. was especially emotional.

“Alright, then I shall be saying goodbye as well.”

Qian Wenhuan said suddenly while still hanging a courteous smile on his lips. “If you want to begin the age-reversal treatment, you can call this number whenever you’re ready and someone will have everything arranged for you. If you’d like to enlist in our company’s scientific research institute, you can call this number instead...”

Qian Wenhuan gave Watson Jr. two contact numbers before reminding them, “Meanwhile, we’d appreciate it if you guys can keep this particular instance of us curing James Watson a secret. Since this particular technology involves our company’s latest scientific advent, it’s extremely confidential and we’d prefer for it to not be exposed to the outside world, you understand?”

“Of course, we understand.”

Watson Jr. nodded and promised. “Don’t worry, we’ll never leak any information even if we’re approached by reporters.”

“You have my gratitude.”

Qian Wenhuan bid his farewell one last time before leaving them.

Surely enough, without having Felix here, the Watson Jr. family was not important enough to warrant Qian Wenhuan staying with them.

Watson Jr. was well aware of this but was not any less gracious with Qian Wenhuan for it. He had the good sense to escort Qian Wenhuan to the hospital entrance before bidding farewell.

After Qian Wenhuan had exited the hospital and went into his designated vehicle, he took his phone out and called the only pinned contact number in his phonebook.

“Boss, everything has been arranged. Sir Felix just left as well.

“I already informed them to not leak any information regarding our brain damage treatment technology. I think that Bruno isn’t very reliable and there’s a chance that he might spill the beans...

“Yes, understand.”

With that, Qian Wenhuan hung up and quickly turned to the secretary in the driver seat. “Back to the headquarters.”

So, they exited the parking lot of the hospital and headed for the city...

...

Meanwhile, Chen Chen was drenched in sweat in the middle of the training grounds. With a very subtle flick in his eyes, a phone levitated in the air and charged into a locker.

The locker was shut with an abrupt clank as if there was someone invisible residing in the same space as Chen Chen.

“Are you sure we won’t need to take any actions to prevent this information from being leaked?”

Little X’s tiny figure could be seen in the corner of the room, several explosion-proof shield plating was arranged in front of her. She asked curiously, “We just released the V.1 series, you don’t think it’s too fast for us to reveal that we have brain damage restoration technology?”

“There’s no point hiding it anymore anyway.”

Chen Chen replied blankly. As he did this, he fixed his gaze and immediately sent several daggers on the opposite end of the room soaring toward him, slicing apart the air as they came at him. Before these daggers could get anywhere close to Chen Chen, they were stopped in their tracks by an invisible force.

It looked as if these daggers had run into an invisible sponge several meters away from Chen Chen. Their momentum was immediately halted as they clattered onto the floor lifelessly.

After cutting these high-velocity dagger’s momentum, Chen Chen added. “North America is already plotting against me. They already have a confirmed date and location at this point. Whatever happens now won’t change their mind in the slightest, so there’s no need for us to hide anymore.”

As he said this, yet another wave of daggers came sweeping toward him from a dark corner. Chen Chen glared fiercely in their general vicinity and shot his right palm outward!

The moment he did that, another round of metal clanking onto cold hard concrete echoed across the room again. The daggers were sent flying away in the opposite direction and smashed into the walls.

“In that case, does that mean that Sir Godfather has made all the preparations to go to war with them?”

Little X pouted, a hint of worry appeared on her complexion. “We have to face the Earth Federation all by ourselves, what if we lose...”

“Who says that we’re going to face the Earth Federation head-on?”

Chen Chen wiped the blood off of his nose and remarked icily, “In a few days, the Nobel Prize winners will be announced. They will be sending invitation letters to invite me to the awards ceremony in December. I can pretend to accept the invitation for now and reject at the last minute come December. This way, we can buy two months’ time.

“By that point, they should realize that they have no hope of arresting me in North America, so that leaves them with only one option, which is to take the direct public approach. Either they will begin imposing strict sanctions against our company or apply pressure on the Namibian government to have them arrest me.”

Chen Chen continued elaborating. “Which means that we only need to focus on taking certain approaches come December to limit their options against me.”

“What sort of approach?”

Little X asked curiously.

“X, could you use the Prophet System to determine which company was it that tried to use quantum computers to design quantum viruses to be used against us?”

Instead of providing a direct answer, Chen Chen returned with another question.

“I’m afraid I can’t. I can’t get any output that has anything to do with the quantum computer.”

Little X answered dejectedly, “It’s pretty clear at this point that the Prophet System is completely ineffective when it comes to anything dealing with the quantum computer or anything related to it.”

“I figured.”

Chen Chen nodded, not surprised by this information. He sighed and expressed, “This quantum computer that appears to have something to do with the USB drive has stumped me every step of the way. At this point, I even have to be cautious to not harbor any ill intentions against it. It appears that in addition to being able to predict future catastrophic events, it also has a built-in self-defense mechanism. The moment I plot something against it, its computation is able to successfully detect it.

“But judging by this piece of information that you just provided me, I noticed that this quantum computer has its blind spot as well...”

A mischievous smirk that was impossible to decipher appeared on Chen Chen’s lips as he said this, “In this case, I know exactly what to do next...”

chapters.title: Chapter 403: Recovery
chapters.body:
Chapter 403: Recovery

Chapter 403: Recovery

When James Watson faintly opened his eyes, what he saw was a blank white room.

Outside the window, there was the chirping of birds and the natural sunshine piercing into the room. The room itself was filled with the strong scent of disinfectant. This scent made James Watson’s head feel cloudy. He struggled to register where he was.

He slowly glanced to his side and saw an array of life-preserving machines next to the bed. He saw bundles of cables connecting these intricate machines to his own body.

“You’re awake.”

It was at this moment when James Watson suddenly heard a calm voice from in front of him. James Watson weakly turned to the direction of the sound to see several doctors standing at the foot of the bed.

James Watson opened his mouth and tried to say something. To his dismay, he discovered that he could not force the words out of his mouth. Before he could spiral into a panic, one of the doctors stopped him.

“Your brain sustained major damages previously and you’ve been in a coma for the last two months. It’s normal to not be able to perform certain cognitive functions as your brain is still trying to adapt.”

After the doctor informed the patient of this, he whispered something into his walkie-talkie. Shortly after, hectic rounds of approaching footsteps came from outside the room.

James Watson turned to the door and saw his closest family members charging into the room.

“My God...”

“Father!”

Watson Jr. was the first one to step forward. He held James Watson’s hand tightly in his palms. “Father, how are you feeling?”

“Where... Am... I...”

Watson asked, surprised that the words managed to escape his lips this time.

“You were shot previously and we took you to the hospital. We’re now in the Eco Science City in Namibia, Africa.”

Watson Jr. sounded overwhelmed as he informed his father, “It was Uncle Felix that saved us.”

“So, it’s him...”

Watson’s voice was unimaginably hoarse. “But, why don’t I... I can’t remember the incident...”

“Doctor?”

Watson Jr. first thought was to turn to the doctor for directions.

“This is normal.”

The doctor gave it to him straight. “We used the chip to restore the functionalities of the damaged region of his brain. However, there’s only so much we can do when it comes to his memory. It’s considered fairly normal to lose a portion of one’s memory after sustaining a major brain injury.”

“Alright, then.”

Watson Jr. turned to his father again and tried to reassure him. “It’s alright Father, we can help you jog your memory later on.”

Watson nodded at the suggestion. It was then when he suddenly realized something and reached for his right eye.

He felt his hands brushing against a layer of bandages.

“My eye...”

“Your eye sustained severe damage and cannot be salvaged. Don’t worry, after you have recovered, we can install a bionic eye for you. It’s a part of the design of the Xingtian Project.”

The main surgeon explained.

“Bionic eye?”

Watson Jr. was baffled by this. “I’ve been paying attention to this project as well, but I recall that the Xingtian Project only included bionic limbs and there was no such thing as bionic eyes.”

“Bionic eyes are part of the latest developed bionic organs of the Xingtian Project. It’s currently in its developmental stage and thus not announced to the public yet.”

The main surgeon elaborated, “Its underlying principle holds resemblance to the current brain restoration technology. Surgical machines connect the patient’s visual nerve with the electrodes in the bionic eye to achieve the effect of granting vision.”

“Unbelievable...”

Watson Jr. was astounded by the prospect of this technology.

Before any of this had happened, if someone told him that current technology allowed the restoration of brain damage and regaining sight, he would flat out call that person a liar.

What he was witnessing now had shattered his sense of reality. Not only did his father wake up from his coma, but he also awoke in a healthy state. He now realized that the Earth Federation’s technology.... No, Eco Science City’s technology had reached the realms of divinity...

Several hours later, the doctors brought a stack of cards with figures printed on them to conduct a basic recognition test on Watson who was lying in bed. The results were astounding. The elderly man who had sustained significant brain damage did not display any difficulty with recognizing basic objects and patterns. There was also no symptom of an altered personality caused by the damage, thus proving that he still had regular cognitive functions and personality.

A day later, Watson had progressed to a point where he could sit up on his own and eat without any assistance.

Watson first got down from his bed three days after waking up. Barring his slow movements, he displayed the physicality expected of an average person.

It was by this point when everyone fully comprehended that James Watson had recovered. From someone who was on the brink of death and suffered major damage in more than ten percent of his brain region, he was brought back to the state of a regular person. It was a scientific marvel.

“Mr. James Watson.”

While the Watson family was celebrating merrily, several doctors arrived in Watson’s intensive care unit room. Among the group of doctors was a familiar figure — Qian Wenhuan.

“Is there anything I can help you with, Doctor Johnson?”

Watson had been here for some time and familiarized himself with the doctors. He quickly greeted the head surgeon.

“This gentleman would like to have a short conversation with you.”

Doctor Johnson announced as he turned to Qian Wenhuan.

“Father, allow me to introduce you.”

Watson Jr. thought to himself. “About time” as he introduced the guest, “This young gentleman here is the CEO of Blackwatch, Mr. Qian Wenhuan.”

“Oh, I’m afraid I’m not.”

Qian Wenhuan waved his hands. “I’m just the CEO of the African branch.” He shook hands with James Watson as he said this and set a sheet of a document on the table. “Mr. Watson, I’m sure you know why I’m here. This might not be the best time to discuss this since you haven’t fully recovered, but I figured I’d bring the letter of intent ahead of time. If you’d like to further negotiate, feel free to contact me.”

“Is this about me joining Blackwatch?”

James Watson nodded. “I’m no longer the chairman of Cold Spring Harbor. As long as you can excuse my old age, I’d be more than willing to step up to the position you ask of me.”

“Excellent.”

Qian Wenhuan nodded before producing a thin golden card and handing it to Watson. “Since you’ve agreed to join, then this card shall be our company’s introductory gift to you. The director himself instructed me to give this to you.”

Watson accepted the card, slightly dumbfounded by this gracious gesture. After inspecting the gift, he was shocked to see that this golden card was a membership card to the sanatoriums.

“What the h*ll, this is a golden membership card!”

Bruno exclaimed, “A premium membership card only below diamond and platinum cards!”

“That’s right, looks like Mr. Bruno is well informed.”

Qian Wenhuan smiled. “This is the hospitality that our professor-level researchers are entitled to. Once Mr. Watson makes further contributions to our company, this may be upgraded to diamond or platinum.”

The doctors that came in with Qian Wenhuan were filled with jealousy when they heard this remark. The golden membership card was extremely precious but it was nothing compared to the diamond and platinum cards, the true symbol of luxury.

The benefit that came with the platinum membership card was completely unrestricted access to the services in Eco Science City sanatoriums at any given time!

James Watson’s treatment was certainly what a world-class scientist rightfully deserved.

After leaving the hospital, Qian Wenhuan turned to the secretary behind him. “Inform our department to file James Watson’s records into our company’s Beta grade confidentiality. Don’t forget about the medical records as well.”

“Understood.”

The stockily-built secretary replied with a firm nod before writing down the message to the relevant department. Just as he tapped the send button on his phone, there was an unexplainable sudden blink.

The secretary thought it was some sort of strange bug. He clapped his phone against his hand and inspected again. It was displayed that the message was sent successfully.

Seeing that his task was complete, the secretary put away his phone and continued following Qian Wenhuan out of the hospital.

On the other side, the director of the Human Resources department received Qian Wenhuan’s secretary’s message. However, only the first half of the message was visible. The second half of the message had completely vanished, perhaps caused by either a program error or signal congestion...

chapters.title: Chapter 404: Kidnap
chapters.body:
Chapter 404: Kidnap

Chapter 404: Kidnap

“I’ve intercepted the message coming from Qian Wenhuan’s end, just as you instructed.”

A little girl in a white dress and barefoot sat cross-legged on a sofa and said with her eyes shut, “The Human Resources department are doing as they were instructed. They are filing James Watson’s document into Beta grade confidentiality along with his associating medical records.”

“Stop him.”

Chen Chen instructed.

“Understood.”

The little girl answered swiftly. Meanwhile, in an office in the Human Resource department located in Eco Science City’s headquarter, there was a Chinese man in his mid-thirties. This charming man was the director of the department, recently relegated here from another headquarter.

Just as he was about to tap the mouse and enter the encryption confirmation password, his phone suddenly rang.

“Tunak tunak Tun... Da Da Da! Dholna, vaje tumbe val taar, Soode dil de pukar, Aaja karle ye pyar...”[1]

The Chinese man frowned and looked at his phone. He saw the display of his phone set on the table repeatedly flashing. There seemed to be something wrong with it.

His first instinct was to pick it up to inspect what the problem was. The moment he picked it up, he felt a scorching sensation burning across his palms!

“Hnghh...!”

The Chinese man dropped his phone as if he had been electrocuted. The moment his phone clapped against the ground, the display shattered all across the floor with a sudden crack!

“The h*ll?”

The Chinese man was jolted by this. He could only watch helplessly as the smashed pieces of the phone became engulfed in flame. The hungry fire slowly devoured the SAMSUNG logo etched on the back of the phone...

It was only until the phone had become completely smoldered and the awful stench of burnt plastic filled the room when the Chinese man came back to his senses. When he glanced at the computer again, he saw that the encryption had been completed.

Without paying any further attention to the encryption page, his first thought was to open a new webpage and head to QQ and posted a short update on his feed. “Just had the living crap scared out of me, I was just working and minding my business when my phone exploded...”

...

Half a day later.

“Bruno, are you sure you’re going to return to Chicago all by yourself?”

Watson Jr. expressed helplessly. “I really can’t leave here right now, if you insist on leaving, you have to head back by yourself.”

“Come on, I’m not a toddler okay?”

Bruno responded impatiently, throwing his shoulders out as he said with a bewildered expression, “Come on, I know how to take care of myself.”

“You’re a grown-up now, I hope you don’t cause any further trouble out there.”

Watson Jr. looked at his father Watson who was sleeping soundly in the other room. His voice still filled with concern as he asked, “Are you not going to wait until your grandfather wakes up to say goodbye?”

“No need, he’s not dying anytime soon anyway. Besides, this entire thing turned out to be a blessing since both you and he are going to receive the age-reversal treatment.”

Bruno commented with a slight hint of sarcasm in his voice and quickly left without much hesitation.

Immediately after turning around with his back facing his father, a coy smile appeared on Bruno’s lips. He tapped on his shoulder, the cunning smile still hanging on the corner of his lips.

When Bruno arrived on the ground floor, he immediately went to the receptionist. He produced a patient certification from his pocket and presented it to the nurse on duty. “I’m a family member of James Watson, I’d like to look up some information regarding his medical records.”

“Alright, this way please...”

The nurse at the reception took the patient certification and nodded after ensuring it was the original document. She got right up and led Bruno to an office.

There was nothing out of the ordinary inside the office, only several laptops set up across a table. The nurse opened one of the laptops and looked up one of the files on the server.

“What is this, where’s the medical record?”

Bruno asked, perplexed.

“We use digital medical records here. If you want the medical record in print, you’ll have to ask the head surgeon.”

The nurse explained, “The documents are with the head surgeon.”

“Alright then.”

Bruno shrugged at the nurse’s suggestion and seated himself in front of the laptop. He started flipping through the digital medical record while taking out his phone, appearing as if he was going to take pictures.

The nurse did not comment on Bruno’s actions, she only promptly turned around and left.

After ensuring the only witness was gone, Bruno grew bolder. He selected and opened James Watson’s medical record, then held his phone up to the screen and tapped on the capture button.

“Click!”

With an abrupt click, Bruno had recorded the first page of the medical record.

“Ehh...”

It was only after taking a picture of the first page when Bruno noticed there was a small mark printed onto his grandfather’s medical record.

It was a symmetrical pattern that reminded him of an illustration of a starry night sky. The image was printed in black and white and sparsely splattered across the section below the text on the first page of the medical record. It looked like some sort of watermark or perhaps it was some sort of pattern printed in the document background.

“Whatever, who cares.”

Although the appearance of these patterns was rather outlandish and seemed to come out of nowhere, Bruno knew he did not have time to care about minor details such as these. He selected the second page and took a picture with his phone again.

Then on to the third page and the fourth page...

The medical record was certainly extremely comprehensive. The operation report of the head surgeon alone took up more than 30 pages. By the time Bruno was done with documenting everything, twenty minutes had passed.

Bruno massaged his slightly sore wrist and pulled himself up from his seat before heading out of the office.

“Sir, have you found what you needed?”

The nurse on shift asked when she saw Bruno stepping out of the office. Bruno completely ignored the nurse’s question and sped up his pace, quickly stepping out of the nurse’s line of sight and out of the hospital.

After leaving the hospital, Bruno hurriedly signaled for a taxi as if he was being pursued by somebody. After entering the taxi, he took out his phone and sent the first few pages of James Watson’s medical records to another email.

Within half a minute, Bruno’s phone started ringing. Bruno found himself slightly surprised by this near-immediate callback and proceeded to answer it without a second thought.

“D*mn, Bruno. Are what you just showed me real? James Watson receiving treatment in Eco Science City? What’s this about some brain restoration technology in Eco Science City?” the caller spoke urgently.

“Of course, Ackerman.”

Bruno answered smugly, “How’s that, some pretty shocking news, I know. How much for this info?”

“You’re not trying to scam me, are you?”

The broker still asked in disbelief, “Bruno, you know what happens if you try to play any tricks on me.”

“I won’t try to scam you, I swear.”

Bruno explained quickly, “I know you guys won’t believe me, so I went out of my way to sneak footage of my grandfather’s operation and footage of him after recovery.”

“Oh my God, so it’s the real deal?”

The voice on the other end of the phone was growing more excited. “Bruno, I swear you must be my lucky star if this is real. I’ll offer ten thousand for the medical records and the footage. Don’t sell it to anyone else!”

“Ten thousand is nowhere near enough.”

Bruno frowned. “This isn’t your everyday local flash news. I want a hundred thousand or else I’ll look for other buyers!”

“Impossible!”

The broker exclaimed, “There’s no way this should cost me a hundred thousand, who are you trying to take advantage of here? Where’s your honor? How about this buddy, fifty thousand... How’s fifty thousand? We can meet each other halfway?”

“Sixty thousand!”

Bruno called again.

“Deal!”

The person on the other end of the phone had to grit his teeth and agree to this price.

Bruno could not help but break into a smile when he heard the magic word. “Perfect, Ackerman. We can meet tomorrow when I get back to Chicago and finish the transaction there.”

...

The taxi headed for the Walvis Bay International Airport. By the next day, a plane arriving from Namibia landed at the Chicago O’Hare International Airport.

After the plane had landed and everything was sorted, Bruno let out a lazy yawn and shuffled out of the plane with the rest of the passengers.

Before he could even step foot out of the airport, several men in suits and wearing sunglasses approached him and cut him off.

“Who are you guys?”

Bruno quickly wiped the drowsy, jet-lagged expression off his face and perked up in alert. “Where’s Ackerman?”

“Ackerman is waiting for you outside the airport, Mr. Bruno.”

One of the men took off his sunglasses and addressed Bruno sternly. “Please come with us.”

There was a hint of doubt in Bruno’s eyes. Before he could decide on his next move, two of the men with sunglasses had approached him from both sides. They pincered him in the middle and held some sort of hard object against his waist.

Bruno was certain that the concealed object was a gun used to threaten him.

“Hey buddies, I’ll come with you guys alright. Just don’t point that thing at me, I’m not a big fan...”

Bruno quickly yielded to them and left without the airport with his gracious escorts.

After exiting the airport, the men in black took Bruno to a black multi-purpose vehicle. When they pulled the door open, Bruno saw someone lying inside who had his arms and legs bound together with duct tape. Even this person’s mouth was muffled. This person was none other than Ackerman who was on the phone with him just yesterday.

“Oh d*mn!”

All Bruno could do was curse out loud before he was tossed into the back of the car within seconds. The engine was promptly started and the car drove out of the parking lot.

“Guys, what are you trying to do here?”

Bruno was about to break out in hysterics. He gestured wildly in the back of the car. “Is there a need to go this far just for sixty thousand?”

“Sixty thousand? Ahem, ahem...”

It was at this moment when Bruno suddenly heard bouts of coughing coming from further behind in the backseat. He turned around and saw another silver-haired old man speaking to him, “This means that you’re the one who fed the news to Ackerman yesterday?”

“What news?”

Bruno glanced at the tightly bound Ackerman right next to him.

The old man did not answer Bruno. Instead, he took out his phone and showed Bruno an online article.

Bruno quickly investigated it and saw the title of the article — [Breaking news! Racist idealist James Watson receives brain restoration surgery in Eco Science City.]

The main contents of this article were none other than the medical records stolen by Bruno.

[1] A popular Marathi song

chapters.title: Chapter 405: Astral Catalyst
chapters.body:
Chapter 405: Astral Catalyst

Chapter 405: Astral Catalyst

“Ackerman already publicized it?”

Bruno felt his heart drop when he saw this and he quickly pleaded for mercy, “This only has to do with Ackerman All I did was take several pictures of the medical records, I don’t have anything else. You can look at my phone if you don’t believe me.”

He presented his phone to the old man when he said this, “You can take a look for yourself if you don’t believe me. Ackerman has these exact pictures as well, there’s no need to come for me, believe me!”

“Ahem ahem, hack.. Hack hack!”

The old man went into another coughing fit, he quickly produced a handkerchief and placed it over his mouth for him to cough loudly into.

Bruno felt what may be a sense of pity when he saw this old man in such a sickly state.

“It’s alright... Ahem.”

After what seemed like forever, the old man took the phone from Bruno’s hand and turned it off right away. “It doesn’t matter whether you have it saved in the cloud or some drive somewhere. I’m sure you’ll remember where you put it after we put you through a round of torture.”

“This...”

Bruno’s heart sank when he heard this. His body collapsed weightlessly under him.

Half an hour later, they parked at the entrance of a large private estate.

Bruno stepped out of the car with a depressing gloom cast over his head. Two more men in black quickly trailed after him.

“Hold on, what about Ackerman?”

When he saw how the group of men in black seemed to have the slightest intention of untying Ackerman or carrying him out of the car, he turned to look at the car suspiciously.

Bruno turned around just in time to see a gun mounted with a silencer pointed at Ackerman’s temple.

“Poof!”

There was a muffled sound of a gunshot, immediately followed by Ackerman’s head bursting like a flower dyed red. Bruno’s eyes became so widened it felt like his eyeballs were going to roll out of their sockets any second. He thought he was going to collapse onto the ground because his feet could no longer support his weight.

Ackerman was dead.

Bruno’s legs trembled endlessly, his mind was blank.

Bruno could hardly remember how he managed to walk into the estate on his two feet, nor could he remember when he told them the password to his phone’s storage. When he came to his senses, he found himself in a room built with metal, completely isolated from mobile signals.

The room was drowned in darkness but he could still make out the outline of a large collection of tools meant for torturing. He already spilled everything he knew before any of the tools could be put to use.

The pictures in his phone had been exported and printed, all put together in an album.

It was around this point when a middle-aged man with blond hair, blue eyes, and sporting a beard with tied-back ponytail stepped into the room.

The old man in charge of supervising immediately stepped forward. “Ahem... Young master, we’ve obtained all of James Watson’s medical records. Should we delete the online digital copy?”

“Delete them. This medical record is extremely invaluable and may very well contain the blueprint for mankind’s future biological sciences development, we cannot risk having it leaked.”

The middle-aged man ordered before turning to Bruno. “Have you searched him for anything resembling a tracker or wire? Is there anything suspicious in his phone?”

“We inspected everything thoroughly, he’s clean.”

The old man answered confidently, “Don’t worry, young master. It doesn’t matter how broad Blackwatch’s online presence is, they still can’t bypass a physical Faraday cage principle.”

“Very well then.”

The middle-aged man nodded contently before he took a look at the medical records. “Use the government’s emergency encrypted transportation network to deliver this to Yellowstone Park and into the hands of my father. It’s up to him to decide where we go from here.”

Before leaving, the man they called young master turned to look at Bruno and grinned, revealing a row of pearly-white teeth.

After he left, the old man coughed several times again before giving a signal and exited the room as well.

After that, the only thing that could be heard from inside the room was the muffled wailing of Bruno followed by faint gunshots.

...

“Bruno is dead.”

In the Spire Laboratory, a huge, semi-transparent map of North America appeared in front of Little X. The map that hung mid-air was a projection created by the holographic environment simulator.

On top of the map were densely packed tiny white dots numbering up to tens of thousands. These white dots took up nearly the entirety of the North American region. Among this collection of white dots, three yellow dots distinctly stood out.

The yellow dots were at least ten times larger than the white dots and were more noticeable.

Just as Little X finished saying this, one of the three yellow dots packed closely to one another suddenly vanished as if a spark of flame had been put out.

“Fascinating indeed.”

When he saw the phenomenon observed on the holographic projection, Chen Chen rubbed his Chen and remarked, “To be able to detect a target’s position at all times, even updated accordingly in the event of death.”

“Yes, this is the latest product created from my recent study of E Language.”

Little X stated, “The Astro Catalyst made into watermark is also a Basilisk pattern and belongs to a class of meme catalysts. Unlike the previous ‘Eye’ and ‘Filth’ Basilisk patterns, it’s not dangerous and only serves to ‘mark’ its target.

“Although its functionalities are considered very basic as far as memes go, its usage is also considered the most practical. No matter where the target escapes to, whether the target vacates to the Moon or Mars, the effect of the meme remains undisrupted by spatial factors. Anyone infected by the ‘Astro’ meme can be easily detected by me.”

Chen Chen nodded and turned to the holographic projection again.

The white dots on the holographic projection represented the first type of “Astro”. When Bruno sent the first few pages of the medical records to a media firm, that media firm immediately distributed them to gain traction to their network.

This was how hundreds of thousands of people in North America came to be infected by the “Astro” meme.

Chen Chen specifically considered this factor and made it so that there were three variants of this Basilisk pattern in the medical record. The first tint of the infection was white, the next tint was yellow while the ones in the last few pages came in red color.

Most common civilians would only get a glimpse of the first few pages, therefore displayed as white color. The crucial information was located in the middle to ending sections of the document. The ones infected by this variant of “Astro” were marked as either yellow or red.

This meant that only the ones who got a glimpse of the important, central information were labeled as yellow or red color, also making them the higher ranking officials among Blacklight Biotechnology’s rivals.

“But Sir Godfather...”

There was a hint of hesitation in Little X’s voice. “Will this approach of ours allow us to maneuver around the quantum machine’s predictive abilities?”

“Of course.”

Chen Chen nodded and started to explain, “Keep in mind that our target isn’t the quantum machine itself but the higher-ups that make direct contact with the quantum machine.

“The federation’s quantum machine is set up to protect humanity from any upcoming disasters. It’s extremely invaluable, but apart from being able to foresee upcoming disasters, it’s only built with self-defensive mechanisms. How was it supposed to do anything about the high-ranking officials?

“As long as the quantum machine itself isn’t our direct target, we shouldn’t have to be concerned about being discovered by its predictive calculations.”

Chen Chen smirked coyly as he said this, “Now that we’ve intentionally set off this heavy-weight cannon that’s the brain restoration technique, chances are our enemies who have eyes everywhere will be notified of it almost immediately. If they realize this is a trap set up by us, then what? Are they going to choose to not go through this crucial medical record anyway?

“Even if they pull all of their tricks out their pockets, would they ever expect our method of tracking them is something that surpasses common laws of physics?”

“I see.”

Little X nodded. “That’s true, our actions are next to impossible to be traced as long as we play around the quantum computer. But then... Why are you downloading this movie?”

The display of the computer in front of Chen Chen suddenly turned on. A movie was placed on the desktop in MP4 format.

The title of the movie was — Spectral.

chapters.title: Chapter 406: The Federation’S Quantum Lab
chapters.body:
Chapter 406: The Federation’S Quantum Lab

Chapter 406: The Federation’s Quantum Lab

The news that Blacklight Biotechnology had cured James Watson quickly made headlines all over the world.

Not many people knew the name James Watson and some of them only knew him for his racially discriminatory remarks. Nonetheless, the news that James Watson had been assassinated some time ago exploded all over the Internet and became the most viral headline concerning James Watson...

Now it had only been two months. Suddenly there was news that James Watson was cured. At once, this news shook up the entire Internet.

Hidden forces were already stirring beneath the surface as countless parties tried their best to get a firsthand account from Blacklight Biotechnology.

In response to this news, Chen Chen did not let Blacklight Biotechnology provide any explanation. He neither admitted the authenticity of this news nor clarified that it was a rumor, simply allowing it to go viral on the Internet.

Soon, countless experts and professors began to speak out, saying that this news was just a rumor. After all, everyone who had paid attention to James Watson’s brain injury two months ago knew that it was not a problem that modern medicine could solve.

Now, all of a sudden several second-rate media claimed that James Watson was cured in Eco Science City. Although it was plausible and there were even medical records as evidence, most people still did not believe it.

As more people followed this news, more people naturally paid attention to other aspects of this case. Soon, groups of black people gathered in several cities across North America began to protest against Blacklight Biotechnology in the form of demonstrations.

The reason for this was very simple. As James Watson was a racist, if Blacklight Biotechnology saved him, naturally, it was also racist...

Still, this view was rather extreme and did not trigger a greater chain reaction. What was more frequently discussed was the veracity of this news.

At the same time, in the Yellowstone Park of North America, a square building was hidden deep in the large forest within the nature reserve.

This was a large building with ten floors and a width of one hundred meters. It was like a huge shopping mall, yet it was not built in a bustling city but a primitive no man’s land instead.

The walls of this building seemed to be extremely sturdy. The entire building was encased in ten meters of concrete, so it was gray all over.

In front of the building’s gate, there were dozens of massive golden strips inserted in the ground, forming a special arrangement structure.

If someone scraped off the gold paint on the surface, they would find that these long strips were made of lead. Dozens of lead strips surrounded the gate of the building, forming a kind of impenetrable barrier.

The combination of all this formed an area that could isolate all electromagnetic radiation and remain unaffected by any radiation.

At this time, this building that looked so silent and still was filled with busy activity within — hundreds of staff members and researchers from all over the world continuously streamed through the hall and laboratories inside the building. Everyone moved at an urgent pace, wearing solemn expressions.

In the highest-level conference room deep in the hall, dozens of representatives from various continents sat around the conference table, discussing something fervently.

“Delegates, please settle down. Today’s ‘Quantum Deity Conference’ shall officially begin.”

A gray-haired old white man walked to the front of the conference table. He first bowed in greeting and then said in English, “According to the order of our initials, the position of chairperson for this week’s Quantum Deity Conference will be held by me, Buckle Murphy, for one week.”

With that said, the projector displayed a data map on the screen behind the old man. The data map was composed of curves and various incomprehensible symbols. The average person would not understand it at all, but everyone present appeared to be highly invested and would occasionally exchange a whispered comment.

“Alright, this is the forecast report of ‘Adam’ yesterday.”

The old man turned around and slowly said to the screen behind him, “As you can see, in this forecast chart, the Earth Federation has generally maintained a stable index in recent years. This shows that in the next month, there won’t be any event that endangers human civilization. The probability of a crisis in the next year is 0.1% and the probability of a civilization crisis in the next ten years is 12%, which is still in the normal curve...

“However, the curves in the fourth index and the seventh index show a small decrease and the curve in the eighth index has undergone a 15% increase. These curve disturbances also mean that in the next year, something that will have an impact on the structure of the world is likely to happen...”

“Oh, d*mn it...”

“Surely that can’t be?”

Many people in the conference room instantly grumbled and became vigilant. Obviously, to these people, a stable world structure was preferred.

“We should be thankful. It’s better than having a ‘crisis disturbance’.”

The old man chuckled and consoled everyone. “You should’ve noticed that in the past ten years, the crisis outbreak index has been showing higher and higher numbers over this period, and it’s even diverging drastically from the previously predicted results. According to the prediction, there would be no crisis in 2023, which was last year, but in March 2023, Adam suddenly overturned its previous prediction, predicting that the onslaught of the African virus will cause an Ekhi-level social disturbance.

“Subsequently, at the end of 2023, an Amida-level disturbance, which is one level higher, was predicted.”

The old man said in a low voice, “This is the first time we’ve come across an Amida-level disturbance. The disturbance index exceeded 1,000%. The probability of human civilization’s extinction exceeded 300%. Fortunately, after we performed many calculations and ran through countless quantum collisions of the interference wave, the general region of the crisis’s source was finally determined.”

Everyone fell silent upon hearing this.

Of course, they knew what the old man was talking about. At the end of 2023, the quantum computer “Adam” suddenly rescinded its previous predictions and issued an epic crisis alert — human civilization was already on the verge of destruction.

It was just that the predicted interference this time was far stronger than usual, so strong that they could not even predict the source of the crisis. In desperation, all the continents emptied their pockets together and purchased electricity for a short time to overclock Adam’s forecasting ability. In the end, they obtained two key phrases —

“North America” and “statue”.

In the face of this crisis, the quantum computer that had always been so definitive was unable to give any specific clues. This cast a shadow of despair in the hearts of countless people.

Fortunately, this crisis was finally solved when remedial measures were taken although the Earth Federation had not yet figured out who or what had taken the three statues.

In the end, North America had used up their precious, once-a-year forecast.

However, it was the same as the previous prediction. When it came to predicting the three statues this time, “Adam” completely lost its effectiveness and could not predict any valuable clues.

The whereabouts of the three statues remained a total mystery.

The meeting ended quickly. Everyone left the conference room swiftly. The old man ordered the assistant to clean up the room, saved and archived all the valuable materials, and then left the conference room as well.

Just as he was leaving, a female employee suddenly ran over and stood in front of the old man, panting. “Mr. Murphy, someone is looking for you outside, requesting that you leave the Quantum Deity Lab.”

chapters.title: Chapter 407: As Buddha Says, It Can’T Be Spoken
chapters.body:
Chapter 407: As Buddha Says, It Can’T Be Spoken

Chapter 407: As Buddha Says, It Can’t Be Spoken

When Buckle Murphy had passed numerous checks and walked out of the laboratory, on the tarmac outside, a small helicopter had been ordered to wait for a long time.

Buckle Murphy stepped onto the helicopter. The helicopter started up quickly and flew out of the forest along a narrow winding path.

After flying for a distance, the winding roads under his feet had completely disappeared and there was only a dense endless jungle in front of him. They even had to cross over dozens of mountains. The helicopter traveled for half an hour before it left the forest, coming to the end of the trees.

Here, a large-scale military base popped into Buckle Murphy’s field of vision.

It was not until the helicopter landed that Buckle Murphy jumped out of the vehicle with the help of a few soldiers. At the same time, four or five people in the barracks before him came up to him.

“Father.”

A middle-aged man with a ponytail walked to the forefront. When he saw Murphy, he immediately looked attentive. “I brought you something. I believe you’ll be shocked after seeing it.”

“Stop hyping it up.”

Buckle Murphy waved his hand and walked toward the barracks in the squall of the helicopter. As he walked, he said, “As a ‘shield bearer’, my time is very limited and this week I’m also the chairman of the Quantum Deity Conference. So, if you have something, just hurry and show it to me.”

“Yes.”

The middle-aged man walked into the barracks, took out a document, and handed it to Buckle Murphy.

“What’s this?”

Buckle Murphy took the document, gently turned to the first page, and raised his eyebrows suddenly. “James Watson’s hospital records?”

“Please keep reading, Father.”

The middle-aged man somberly said, “This case isn’t simple. You should’ve heard that James Watson was assassinated two months ago. He should’ve died, but Blackwatch Corporation developed a new type of technology and healed him when he was on the brink of death.”

“Oh?”

Murphy’s expression turned completely serious when he heard these words. He walked to the table in a few steps, sat on the chair, and pored through the document carefully.

After he did so, the surprise in his eyes could no longer be concealed and he even gradually frowned.

Time turned from afternoon to night. The shining stars slowly rose from the end of the forest. The starry sky was extremely bright. This was a magnificent star-studded sky that could only be seen in Yellowstone Park.

It was only when the ground outside the barracks was lit up by starlight that Murphy rubbed his dry eyes and put down the case sheet in his hand.

“Father?”

The middle-aged man immediately walked up from one side.

“Heh heh heh...”

Murphy laughed softly. “An AI chip and nano-scale electrodes that can replace the brain, along with NSR implants... They can even repair brain injury?”

“Father, do you think this is fake?”

The middle-aged man was surprised. “We’re not sure either. If this technology shows up in any research institution, I’d assert that it’s fake, but if it’s Blackwatch...”

“Whether the records are real, I don’t know. I only want to know — what are these patterns?”

However, Murphy just shook his head, picked up one of the papers, and stood in front of his son. “I noticed earlier that there are these patterns on every page and they aren’t fully identical, for there are three types of patterns.”

“It’s probably a watermark.”

The middle-aged man was a little uncertain. “It may also be an anti-counterfeit mark that Blackwatch uses. Plus, it doesn’t get in the way of reading.”

“Is it really just a watermark?”

Murphy frowned, then put the three patterns together and kept comparing them. “This pattern has no symmetrical structure. It cannot be a logo or a watermark. In my opinion, it’s more like a password or a code word...”

“What?”

The middle-aged man was startled. “Do you think there’s other information hidden in these patterns?”

“I’m just guessing. When you head back, ask a few professional linguists to analyze these watermarks and see what they mean.”

“Yes.”

The middle-aged man could only assent.

“If these records are forged, we can forget the whole thing. But if the contents of this case are true, then we’ll speed up our destruction of Blackwatch.”

Murphy grew sterner. “This kind of technology has the potential to control human thoughts. The brain is the forbidden area of the human body. Once our enemies master this technology, the consequences will be unimaginable. Therefore, this kind of technology must never be allowed to appear in the hands of forces other than ourselves!”

“That said, this company is truly something else.”

The middle-aged man suddenly realized. “It’s no wonder that the spies we sent not only didn’t get much news but were killed in the Eco Science City. We don’t even know how they died...”

Murphy did not answer but gave the middle-aged man a few more orders, then walked out of the barracks, and returned to the helicopter.

Even as the helicopter lifted off again, Murphy still maintained a solemn expression. He did not know why but after reading the document, he had the feeling he was being spied on.

This feeling was very subtle and indistinct, somewhere between something and nothing.

“My mind is playing tricks? Or am I being watched?”

Murphy murmured. At this moment, he again recalled what happened five years ago.

On that day five years ago, when he was elected as a “shield bearer” and became a representative of North America, he was monitored by “Adam”.

“Adam” was a quantum computer. No one knew its origin. According to the earliest group of people, it was as if this quantum computer had emerged from the earth itself. At the beginning of the Federation’s founding, the quantum computer “Adam” had appeared naturally and spontaneously.

The program of the quantum computer had also been set up. It was running at all times. From that moment to ten years later, all possibilities were calculated. Once any disaster that endangered human civilization was discovered, a crisis alert would be issued.

How great was the computing speed of “Adam”?

One could measure this by the Summit supercomputer, which had the fastest computing speed in the Earth Federation. The floating-point computing speed of Summit was 200 PFLOPS, which was 200 thousand trillion operations per second. In that case, “Adam” had 300 billion times the computing speed of Summit.

In other words, the calculations “Adam” performed in 1 second would take the Summit 10,000 years...

It was due to this kind of perverse computing speed that “Adam” could bear the responsibility of receiving input from all the variables of the entire planet, thereby predicting all the crises of the Earth Federation one month or even ten years into the future.

Therefore, the quantum computer Adam was also called the “Holy Shield” and Murphy’s current position was the “shield bearer.”

Although the “shield bearers” had little power, they represented the interests of the various continents in the Federation. Every six-year cycle, each continent would choose three members to act as the “shield bearers” of its own continent.

On the day he became a shield bearer, the quantum computer detected everything about him, including his birth and death. The quantum computer “Adam” thus reconstructed his past and predicted his future.

At that time, Murphy felt as if he was being watched...

Murphy’s expression darkened. He knew that this feeling could not have come from “Adam”. Although “Adam” could calculate extremely swiftly, its resources were still extremely precious. It was said that only about three hours of calculation time could be squeezed out per year. The remaining time must be used to forecast the crises of the Earth Federation.

In this way, the accuracy of Adam’s calculations was still barely enough. The forecast spanning ten years could only provide them with a general trend. If they wanted to get a more accurate forecast, they must perform calculations on a monthly basis.

So, if it were not Adam, there were only two possibilities — one was that all of this was just a trick of the mind and the other was that a quantum computer similar to Adam was evaluating Murphy.

At this instant, Murphy thought of Blackwatch Corporation.

Since he started targeting this company two years ago, he had only watched the company grow bigger and bigger. Moreover, the dozens of spies he sent, as well as the resistance organization White Embers he had indirectly established, were all wiped out. Even those members of the plutocratic families whom he had roped in were beginning to experience various mishaps.

Many of these spies who had encountered unexpected fates were all well concealed and the only channel by which they might be exposed was the Internet.

As a result, Murphy gradually came up with a bold conjecture that Blackwatch Corporation was likely to have a quantum computer similar to that of the Earth Federation. It was only by relying on that computer’s monitoring of the Internet that this company could keep growing stronger and uncovering all of its hidden enemies so accurately.

Therefore, he simply set up a bureau and ordered an agent to bring a USB flash drive containing Adam’s quantum virus to the Eco Science City. The agent was to insert the USB flash drive into their opponent’s intranet.

This quantum virus could be regarded as a major achievement of the Earth Federation’s many-year research into “Adam”. Its function was also very simple. Namely, it would copy all the data in the other party’s computer and without requiring any connection, those data would be copied to Adam’s side.

In that way, the existence of another quantum computer would inevitably be detected by the Earth Federation.

Even if the agent failed, the quantum USB drive would still fall into the hands of Blackwatch Corporation, which would still have the opportunity to insert the USB drive, causing the same result.

It was just that nothing happened in the end.

The agent was indeed exposed but the USB flash drive belonging to North America had vanished without a trace. It was like throwing a meat bun to hit the dog. The dog was fine but the meat bun had been swallowed by the dog.

Therefore, even now he did not know whether his conjecture was right or wrong.

After learning this, the North American officials finally became anxious. Since they could not do anything about the other party even after a secret attack, they had to tear off their masks and lay out a plan to arrest the head of the Blackwatch Corporation.

Still, this was all done in secret. The Earth Federation headquarters and the officials of other continents did not know about all this. Otherwise, it would become another conflict of interest between all parties.

All these thoughts flashed through Murphy’s mind. This sensation of being spied on caused Murphy to ponder extensively.

“Could it be that they’ve discovered my existence?”

Murphy felt a chill for no reason, but then he realized how absurd this sounded. As the shield bearer of the Earth Federation, would he be afraid of a private company?

Still, he had to admit that the company was indeed a bit too mysterious. It had an endless stream of biotechnology, which just so happened to solve the crisis of life and death. Once this happened too many times, people would naturally become somewhat apprehensive...

Thus, was this all just his mind playing tricks?

Murphy had no idea and there was nothing he could do now. He could only wait for the genius named Chen Chen to leave the Eco Science City in December and arrest him when he attended the Nobel Prize ceremony.

These thoughts echoed back and forth in Murphy’s mind. About an hour later, he returned to the Quantum Deity Laboratory in the depths of the forest and returned to his post.

What Murphy did not know was that the moment he saw those watermarks, he became watched by a pair of eyes, tens of thousands of kilometers away...

...
