Chen Chen said in a deep tone, “The reason people believe these tales isn’t because they’re inherently gullible but because deep inside, they choose to believe these stories. They don’t care what the truth is. They only wish that these stories were true. Even if these stories were debunked tens of thousands of times, they simply refuse to look further into it.

“This is the nature of man. Therefore, when a real supernatural event occurs to the masses, there’s not much else that we need to do since they will come up with theories of their own. Some would claim that it’s God, the Devil, and stir up fierce social debates and turmoil...”

Chen Chen snapped his fingers and smirked. “I’m willing to bet that it won’t take more than a month before Northern America plunges into chaos...”

Little X nodded in a way that seemed like she had a rough idea of what Chen Chen was talking about.

“I’ll modify it right away. Give me ten minutes.”

With that, Little X sat down on the sofa and shut her eyes again.

In just nine minutes, Little X opened her eyes again. The printer simultaneously produced two patterns, which Chen Chen promptly inspected. He saw that the patterns were largely the same as their previous versions, only there were several strokes missing here and there.

“Excellent.”

Chen Chen crumpled them up and tossed them into a shredder. “X, your task is to ensure that everyone in the continents other than Northern America and Europe sees the immunity pattern. This way, only the population from our targeted continents will be vulnerable to this infection, can you do that?”

“It won’t be an easy task, but it won’t be anything compared to the task of producing the Basilisk images.”

Little X smiled slyly, bobbing her head to the side.

Chen Chen nodded contently, confident in his assistant.

...

The Secret of Dreams was a well-known forum in Northern America that had been operating for thirteen years, accumulating millions of members and millions of posts discussing their dreams.

The forum admins even went out of their way to hire dozens of masters at dream analysis to answer questions of the netizens visiting the forum.

On this day, a new post seeking advice suddenly appeared on the forum.

[Hello fellow dreamers and teachers, my name is Lilith, I’m a psychologist. Recently, I’ve been having some strange dreams that I’m not sure if should be attributed to the stress I’ve been feeling. I always see the same face in the dream. Is there an explanation for this?]

Within moments, a fellow forum member replied:

[It must have been the face of someone you’ve met in real life but forgot about. What happens is the face of this person remains deep in your conscience so that you would see this person in your dreams since dreams are inherently the product of our underlying memories. You won’t see someone you haven’t met before in your dreams.]

The comment was well-received and saw many likes quickly. A lot of comments followed after this comment.

[Of course, it’s Sanimo, you’re reaching the level of the teachers!]

[All hail Sanimo!]

[I’m new here, is this Sanimo popular here? Is his analysis reliable?]

[...]

Meanwhile, in the suburbs of New York City, Sanimo was looking at the chain of replies under his comments, a prideful smile hung by his lips. He proceeded to swallow a gulp of coke.

He had just finished class and was browsing the Internet inside his dormitory. He just happened to see this new post and decided to write a comment since it was convenient for him to do so.

This person was a senior member of the forum and usually very rarely replied to new members.

chapters.title: Chapter 421: Sanimo
chapters.body:
Chapter 421: Sanimo

Chapter 421: Sanimo

As he saw how the crowd of netizens flattered and praised him, Sanimo’s vanity was greatly satisfied. He nodded with a haughty smile, waiting for the other party to reply to his answer.

Sure enough, half an hour later, this netizen named Lilith replied.

[My God, is that right? But the stranger I dreamed of was very scary. There shouldn’t be such a scary person in reality. He was like a demon. If I had met him in reality, I’d remember...]

Seeing this reply, Sanimo could not help frowning, but he was in a good mood today, so he did not quibble over the fact that Lilith was doubting his assessment, but patiently explained:

[Here’s the thing, if you’ve seen something terrifying, that means you’ve mixed up his face with a monster in a horror movie. This is also a normal phenomenon. The stranger’s face may have come from any horror movie or even a poster that you glimpsed when you passed the movie theater.]

As Sanimo replied, the popularity of this post began to rise slowly, and it soon entered the homepage. After it gained popularity, all netizens underneath started fervently discussing with each other.

[TIL. No wonder I have nightmares every time I watch a horror movie. So this is the reason...]

[Sanimo is right, is this related to the specialized branch of dreams?]

[I see. Post bookmarked.]

With the appearance of a group of netizens, the popularity of this post increased even more, to the point that even Sanimo was a little surprised.

However, at this moment, an unfamiliar account suddenly commented underneath:

[I’m curious, what kind of face did you see?]

This sentence immediately led the discussion of the post to the topic of what the face looked like in the dream. Everyone also inquired. Obviously, to many netizens, this topic was more interesting than anything that came before.

Sanimo refreshed the page twice and saw that Lilith had not replied to him, so he checked the time.

It was already 9:30 at night, but at this time, his roommate’s bed in the dormitory was still empty. His roommate was a Mexican. His parents were in jail for drug dealing and he only managed to survive by the money his parents had saved up and entered this school.

Nonetheless, although this roommate was the child of a drug dealer, he studied very seriously, otherwise, he would not have been admitted to Stanford University and become a member of this world-renowned school.

About two minutes later, just when Sanimo was a little tired and was about to turn off the computer to go to sleep, the forum finally chimed with a notification.

Sanimo clicked on the notification, and sure enough, it was the original poster Lilith who responded:

[Big thanks to the answer from the veteran Sanimo and the enthusiastic help of all netizens. The reason I’m replying to you now is that someone asked me what kind of face I saw, so I drew it out from memory and gave him a name, “This Man”. If you’re interested, you can click to open it, but please be careful, because this picture is very scary... [Picture]]

She had even drawn it out?

Seeing this, Sanimo smiled subconsciously and moved the mouse to the picture, but just when he was about to click on it, his hand suddenly paused...

He did not know why, but when he moved the mouse over this picture, Sanimo suddenly felt an inexplicable pounding in his heart.

As it got closer to night, the dormitory had become completely dark. Since he returned to the dormitory after class, he had never gotten out of bed. Therefore, the lights in the dormitory were not turned on. Only the lights from other dormitories came in from outside the window, illuminating part of the space in the dormitory, but most of the corners were still pitch black.

At this time, Sanimo stiffened his neck and turned his head to look into the dark corners of the dormitory.

There was nothing there.

Sanimo exhaled. He did not know why he suddenly had a horrible feeling, let alone in the dormitory that he was very familiar with. Still, he did not think much about it and he moved the mouse over the picture.

As a veteran who had trawled through major websites and forums for many years, it was not like he had never seen frightening images. At this point, the other party had already aroused his curiosity and his curiosity had overcome his fear.

“Click.”

With a soft click from the mouse, the picture entered the loading state and then an image with a white background was loaded from top to bottom...

The Internet speed at this time became extremely slow for some reason, so the picture that could have been loaded in an instant had to emerge from top to bottom, but Sanimo was already staring at the screen.

First, he saw a tuft of sparse hair, and then, a pair of thick and comical eyebrows appeared, and after that, a pair of large, hollow eyes...

Sanimo held his breath subconsciously.

The picture kept on loading. Behind the hollow eyes, a weird nose gradually appeared, and finally, a slightly upturned mouth.

The most important thing was that this mouth was too big...

After seeing this picture, the corners of Sanimo’s mouth twitched, and at the same time, a chill shot up his spine and surged over his scalp in ripples. It felt as if the top of his head was about to explode.

Was this the “This Man” that Lilith said?

Sanimo took a deep breath and subconsciously moved away from the screen. At this moment, he even regretted his reckless behavior.

The visual effect of this picture called “This Man” was scary, but it was not enough to frighten people who were mentally prepared. Even so, for some reason, after seeing this picture, Sanimo felt an inexplicable burst of heart palpitations.

He could not tell the source of such palpitations. Even he felt that it was just an illusion.

Just when Sanimo’s nerves were on edge, outside the dark dormitory’s door, there was a sudden sound of scattered footsteps.

Sanimo hurriedly closed the picture while he looked up at the door, only to hear the footsteps coming to the door. Following that, there was a sound of a key turning in the lock.

“Phew...”

After hearing this sound, Sanimo finally breathed a sigh of relief. Since his roommate was back, he would feel much braver. At least, he would no longer feel so nervous.

“Click!”

After the clattering noises, the door of the dormitory opened subsequently and Sanimo suddenly saw a figure walking in from outside.

Due to the lights in the corridor, the person who came in was faced away from the light, and Sanimo could not see the other person’s appearance. Nonetheless, he could judge that this was indeed his roommate based on his body shape, so he immediately shouted:

“Danny, why are you only back now?”

“Uh, I ran into some trouble outside...”

His roommate’s voice came over, with a touch of helplessness. “Sanimo, since you’re in the dormitory, why didn’t you turn on the lights?”

“I’m too lazy. You can turn it on.”

Sanimo spread his hands. “What trouble did you have outside?”

“Nothing much, a bee happened to sting my face and my face swelled up.”

The roommate walked under the switch of the fluorescent light. “Will you come take a look for me?”

“Of course, no problem.”

Sanimo laughed and went directly to the side of the bed. At this moment, the roommate pressed the switch at the same time.

At once, the entire dormitory lit up and the dazzling light made Sanimo close his eyes promptly. When he opened his eyes again in the next second, his roommate was already standing in front of him.

“Sanimo, look at my face, is it swollen?”

His roommate’s voice still rang out in the dormitory, but at this time, Sanimo’s eyes bulged. His pupils shrank and incomparable panic flared in his eyes ——

In front of him, there was a man with thick eyebrows, vacant eyes, and a mouth that was stretched to the base of his ears. The man was wearing his roommate’s clothing while tilting his head with an eerie smile as he stared unwaveringly at Sanimo...

This Man...

“Ahh!”

Sanimo burst out in a scream and sat up from the bed. A torrent of cold sweat had soaked his pajamas. His entire body was heaving heavily and he looked around wildly at the same time.

At this time, the window was slightly brighter. Sanimo quickly looked at the clock but found that it was already the next morning.

“Phew... It was just a dream.”

Sanimo wiped the cold sweat from his forehead and slowly recovered.

At this moment he also remembered. After seeing the spine-tingling portrait of the man last night, he kept getting the creeps. He did not fall asleep until his roommate came back, but he did not expect he would dream of that man...

How very strange.

Sanimo murmured but at this time, he found that his roommate’s bed nearby was empty. The blanket was turned over messily and his roommate’s slippers were gone.

In the washroom nearby, there was the sound of water.

“D*mn, this guy takes a long time to wash up every time...”

Sanimo cursed in a low voice. He felt the urge to pee and quickly got out of bed, walked to the bathroom door, and knocked on the door vigorously. “Danny, can you come out first and let me go to the bathroom?”

“Soon!”

His roommate mumbled back from the washroom.

“Please!”

Sanimo continued to knock on the door. “You’re brushing your teeth now. If I use the toilet, it won’t delay your brushing. My bladder’s bursting, please!”

“I’m coming out now!”

His roommate’s voice still sounded muffled and then the bathroom door slowly opened from the inside.

“Thanks, buddy!”

Sanimo was finally relieved. He smiled gratefully but in the next second, the smile on his face completely froze...

That previous man with the strange face was standing in the bathroom, looking at himself quietly through the palm-wide crack of the door.

...

“Wo-oah!”

Sanimo only felt his blood run cold. He suddenly sat up in bed, panting in big gulps, his eyes widened. He could not recover for a long while.

It was a dream in a dream!

Thinking back to the previous dream, Sanimo felt that everything in the dream was so clear. So clear that it did not even seem like a dream, but a real memory!

Thinking of this, Sanimo quickly turned sideways, turned on the computer again, and logged into the forum which he was most familiar with without thinking about it.

Sure enough, the post of the netizen named Lilith remained in the forum.

Sanimo took a deep breath, slowly opened the post, and scrolled down. Sure enough, he saw the face of the man in the dream again.

Curvy and thick eyebrows, hollow eyes, a cracked and smiling mouth...

The man was staring at him with an unfathomable smile. Although it was just a picture, Sanimo’s heart trembled with apprehension as he stared back.

At the same time, under this picture, a lot of new replies had appeared and the number of replies even exceeded one hundred!

[F*ck! It gave me a fright!]

[WTF is this monster?]

[Why did I have such itchy fingers!]

[F*ck U!]

[...]

As if looking for something, Sanimo continued to scroll down and soon, he found a message he wanted to see —

[D*mn, it’s all thanks to this f*cking picture. I dreamed after seeing this picture. I had a total of three dreams, two ordinary dreams and one nightmare. This guy was in all my dreams, truly evil!]

Sanimo looked at the time of this message and it turned out to be 7:25 in the morning, which was five minutes ago!

After reaching the bottom of the post, Sanimo instinctively pressed F5. The entire webpage was automatically refreshed and a new comment appeared below this comment!

[Did you dream of him? No way, me too! I also dreamed of this guy!]

This message was sent exactly one minute ago.

At this moment, Sanimo faintly thought of something as if he had found a channel to unburden himself and he quickly typed a paragraph:

[I also dreamt about him. I had two dreams and they were all nightmares! In the dream, this guy became my roommate. My God, I can’t believe all this!]

After sending this comment, Sanimo got out of bed hurriedly. He glanced at the bed next to him nervously, but just so happened to see his roommate Danny roll over in a sleepy daze.

Fortunately, Danny still maintained the appearance of a normal person...

Seeing this, Sanimo finally relaxed. He quickly walked into the bathroom to wash up and then without waking up his roommate, he walked out with his laptop on his back.

Today is Sunday and there were no classes for his course. Where Sanimo was heading were where his club was located.

Due to his love for dream research, he joined the school’s dream research club in his freshman year. This club focused on analyzing human dreams and the president was a high-achieving psychology student, specializing in the subconscious aspects of humans.

Sanimo’s knowledge of dreams also came from the hands-on teaching of this president who was a senior student.

When he came to the club’s room, no members had arrived yet so Sanimo sent a message to all the members of the club with his mobile phone. After that, he directly connected the computer to the printer to print out the black and white picture from the forum.

As he watched the printer spit out that weird face little by little, there was no fear in Sanimo’s heart. Rather, he was feeling a faint excitement because he suspected that this picture might have the ability to hypnotize people!

Anyone who had seen this picture would easily dream of its existence in a dream. This strange phenomenon must be related to the subconscious!

This kind of hypnotic pattern that could affect the human subconscious was very rare and its existence was even once doubted by many people. After all, everyone’s minds were different and most of these pictures were only effective for a few people. There had never been a picture that worked for everyone.

Now, Sanimo was vaguely aware that he had likely found a picture that could hypnotize most people and the name of this picture was called...

This Man!

chapters.title: Chapter 422: Marcus
chapters.body:
Chapter 422: Marcus

Chapter 422: Marcus

Half an hour later, after Sanimo had notified everyone, energetic young men and women arrived, and the once desolate place suddenly became lively.

These people got together in small groups as soon as they came, laughing and bantering with each other. The club was filled with a cheerful spirit.

Every time these people asked Sanimo what great discovery he had made, Sanimo did not answer directly but said he would wait until everyone came.

“Sanimo, you said you had a huge discovery, so what is it?”

At this point, the president, Marcus, arrived. He calmly looked at Sanimo who remained mysterious and then asked.

As the president, he had to always display a calm character and persuasive abilities and his hard work had indeed been rewarded. Many people in the club harbored a deep respect for him, something that was very difficult to achieve among peers.

Hearing the question from President Marcus, Sanimo obediently said, “Marcus, I’ve made this huge discovery inadvertently. I suspect that I’ve found a hypnotic image. This image can affect the human subconscious and cause people to have consecutive nightmares.”

“Oh?”

Marcus took a look at Sanimo when he heard this and saw that Sanimo did indeed hold a sheet of A4 paper in his hand, with something printed on it. Marcus then smiled. “Surely you don’t mean the picture you’re holding right now.”

Sanimo nodded.

Seeing this, Marcus continued. “Sanimo, I’ve said many times, the amount of information carried by pictures isn’t large and it’s far from sufficient to be used as a tool for hypnosis. In psychology, at best, it can only be used as a tool for a suggestion. If used for hypnosis, songs on the auditory level are much more effective than pictures on the visual level.”

Sanimo shook his head and said firmly, “Marcus, if you had said this in the past, I’d have agreed with you, but now it’s different. The picture in my hand is unusual. I could tell the moment I first laid eyes on it!”

“Let me see, then.”

Seeing this, Marcus did not refute again, but reached out and put his open palm in front of Sanimo.

Sanimo hesitated and finally put the picture in Marcus’s hands.

Marcus smiled and spread out the image in his hands. At the same time, the group of club members gathered around quickly.

Following that, a strange-looking man’s face appeared in front of everyone...

The moment they saw this picture, everyone present was taken aback. In an instant, the entire club fell silent, but this scene did not last for two seconds before it was swallowed up by the eager discussions in the club.

“Is this the picture?”

“Except for being quite strange, there’s nothing special about it.”

“Maybe the language of higher psychology is involved or its origin is a hint in itself?”

“He looks so ugly...”

“Hahahahaha!”

At once, everyone was discussing this loudly. They all commented on the picture in front of them to express that they were not afraid.

Among the crowd, only Marcus and a few people wore a pensive expression.

“Where did you come across this picture?”

Marcus suddenly turned his head and asked Sanimo.

“I drew it unintentionally.”

Sanimo’s possessive nature made him reluctant to name the forum, so he said vaguely, “I named it This Man. The important thing now is that we must understand whether it’s a hypnotic picture, right? I believe that the moment you saw him, you felt something unique in your hearts, right?”

“Maybe it’s because he’s so weird.”

A guy immediately explained, “I did feel peculiar the moment I saw the picture, but I think this is a form of suggestion. First, you planted a suggestion to make us think it will be mysterious. By the time you showed it, we were all stunned because we were caught off guard. This is why we felt an unusual emotion in our hearts.”

This opinion immediately attracted everyone’s support.

“In the final analysis, whoever strikes first will have the upper hand. This picture also seems related to the phenomenon of the ‘uncanny valley’, which is the reason we felt so weirdly about it.”

Another girl followed up.

The “uncanny valley” was a theory about how humans felt toward robots and non-human objects — depicting the emotional response of humans toward certain anthropomorphic things (robots, movies, anime characters, and so on) as a graph. As anthropomorphic things were similar to humans in appearance and behavior, humans would have positive emotions towards robots. This emotional value would increase as the similarity between the anthropomorphic object and humans increased.

However, when the degree of similarity between anthropomorphic objects and humans reached a certain value, humans’ reactions to them would suddenly become extremely negative. Even if anthropomorphic objects were only a little different from humans, these tiny distinctions would become very conspicuous, leading to disgust and even fear. At this point, the curve of the emotional value would plummet straight down.

As the similarity between anthropomorphic objects and humans continued to rise, achieving the same similarity that existed between ordinary people, humans’ emotions toward them would return to a positive scale again. This resulted in a human-to-human empathy level, which meant that the emotional value curved right up again and reached a level similar to the previous peak.

Therefore, this pattern would appear: the curve of human emotions toward anthropomorphic things slowly rose from the beginning to a rapid drop in the middle, and then rose again at the end. A “Great Rift Valley” appeared in the middle of the curve.

This Great Rift Valley was called the “uncanny valley” effect. It referred to the fact that some anthropomorphic things were too human-like, but they were still different from human beings. Therefore, to the human senses, the appearances of these objects would seem creepy, unsightly, and so on...

Therefore, this club member was now attributing their responses to the uncanny valley effect.

“I admit, it does look ugly, but I swear, this isn’t the uncanny valley.”

Sanimo shook his head, extremely disappointed from the bottom of his heart. He did not expect that everyone would not believe him, so he retrieved the image from Marcus, then turned and walked out of the club.

“The only way to test this is, you only need to wait until tonight to know.”

After saying this, Sanimo walked straight out of the club and ignored the shouts and cries of everyone behind him.

Only Marcus quietly lingered upon the creepy feeling from before and gradually, a thoughtful look appeared on his face.

...

When it was time for lunch, everyone in the club gradually dispersed. As the last person who left closed the door of the club, the entire venue fell into silence.

Still, this silence lasted only a few minutes. Soon, as the bathroom door creaked, the door slowly opened, and a blonde girl walked out of the door.

This girl was the one who put forward the uncanny valley theory earlier. She first looked around and confirmed that there was no one in the club, then quickly walked to the computer and began to retrieve the previously printed documents.

Soon, a thankful look shone in the girl’s eyes. She briefly pressed the print button and immediately the printer made a squeaking sound again. At the same time, a pattern was printed out.

It was the same weird face as before.

Seeing this image, the girl picked up this picture in satisfaction and was just about to leave. However, the moment she turned around, she suddenly discovered that a tall person was standing behind her without her realizing it!

“Ahh!”

The girl sat down on the ground in shock. Only then did she get a clear look. It was President Marcus who was standing behind her.

“Marcus, what are you doing sneaking around behind me?”

The girl stood up, huffing angrily, and at the same time subconsciously hid the paper sheet she was holding behind her back.

“No need to hide, I’ve seen it.”

Marcus stretched out his hand and grabbed the picture from the girl’s hand. At the same time, he calmly said, “It’s very unethical to print someone else’s unauthorized material privately.”

“I was just curious.”

The girl wanted to snatch it back quickly, but she could not reach it, so she stomped her feet. Then her eyes gleamed and she suddenly smiled. “Mr. President, why haven’t you gone back?”

“I was just going to clean up.”

Marcus replied while putting away the picture in his hand. He moved to pick up the broom on the side.

“Marcus, isn’t the reason you’re still here also because of that weird picture of Sanimo’s?”

The girl made a request and at the same time persuaded, “How about this — I’ll print another one. We’ll each take our own and keep our mouths shut. What do you say?”

Marcus hesitated for a while, then nodded in agreement and then left the club.

When he walked out of the club and came to the campus path, Marcus took out the pattern again. He looked at the peculiar face in the pattern and his eyes glimmered in deep thought.

The reason he stayed behind at the club and even bumped into the girl who had also lingered on for a long time was that he had a special feeling about this picture.

It was an emotion filled with coldness and abnormality. The moment he saw the picture, he seemed to see the human face talking to him and at the same time, there seemed to be a deep whisper by his ear.

This was a feeling that no movie, book or even painting could induce. Perhaps, as Sanimo said, it was a pattern that could hypnotize the human spirit.

Therefore, he was the last one to leave the club just now.

However, now it appeared that in the entire club, apart from Sanimo, only he and Molly had sensed the extraordinary nature of this pattern while the others seemed unable to feel it at all. Otherwise, they would not have dismissed the mysterious pattern so readily...

Thinking of this, Marcus returned to the dormitory. He recalled what Sanimo had said before, so he simply put the pattern away and lay directly on his bed.

Then, he slowly closed his eyes, preparing to use his sleep to verify whether all this was true or whether it was just Sanimo spouting nonsense.

Gradually, Marcus fell asleep.

...

“Who are you?”

Marcus looked around. At this moment, he was on a desolate road. He was walking alone and there was an impenetrable mist around him.

At the very head of the road, a black figure stood quietly at a close distance.

The black figure’s limbs were extremely slender as if they were a bag of bones, but unlike the limbs, the head was huge. This head, placed upon this body, created an extreme sense of incongruity.

After seeing this, the weirdness of the scene caused Marcus to stop warily. He stood still and said to the shadow in front of him, “Who are you?”

“Rustle rustle...”

There was the sound of someone shifting in the mist and at the same time, the figure began to move its feet slowly, approaching Marcus step by step.

Marcus wanted to run but at this moment, he felt as if he was standing in a pool of asphalt. No matter how he moved his legs, he was firmly adhered to the ground, unable to move at all.

Thus, he could only helplessly watch the dark shadow slowly approaching...

Ten meters, nine meters, eight meters...

As the figure got closer, Marcus became increasingly terrified. He seemed to understand instinctively that this person approaching him was not a good person!

When the figure was only three or four meters away from him, finally, the shadow revealed its face. The instant Marcus saw this face, he could not refrain from gasping sharply!

This Man!

This name popped out in Marcus’s mind for some reason. He screamed and the entire world quaked rapidly, like a landslide!

His dream was collapsing!

A flash of understanding appeared in his heart. At this moment, Marcus suddenly regained consciousness in his dream. He looked bewildered at This Man who was still standing in front of him and said suddenly, “Who are you really!”

This inquiry was completely different from the previous question...

“I’m no one, I’m me, I’m you, I’m everyone in the world...”

However, a surprising thing occurred. The weird man in front of him did not do him any harm but directly answered, “And now, I’m the embodiment of your dreams and unknown fears.”

After hearing these words, Marcus wore a flabbergasted expression. For some reason, his emotions in his dream were more adaptable and abundant than in reality. He suddenly raised his head and asked again, “Why did you appear?”

“Weren’t you the one who brought me here?”

The strange man replied solemnly, “Because deep down, you wanted me to appear. That’s why I appeared. It’s that simple.”

Marcus took a deep breath after hearing this.

chapters.title: Chapter 423: Spread
chapters.body:
Chapter 423: Spread

Chapter 423: Spread

The afterglow of the evening shone in from outside the window and the entire room was covered in a dusky amber hue.

There was an inexplicable buzzing sound in Marcus’ ear. The sound seemed to hover between reality and illusion. It sounded like tinnitus, but also like a mysterious call from the distant horizon.

Marcus sat on the edge of the bed blankly, his eyes dull and vacant, his hair disheveled. He looked as though he had not woken up yet.

However, at this time, his mind was already turned upside down...

“This Man, does it exist?”

Marcus muttered in a low voice as he kept going over that indescribable realization he had in the dream. It was only when this feeling began to fade that he reluctantly opened the drawer on the side and recorded everything in the dream.

As a fanatic of dream research, he had this special hobby. He recorded every interesting dream he had so that he could recall them quickly in the future.

This method was also done by those who loved to lucid dream.

According to scientific theories, dreams were the product of the subconscious. As an example, if one dreamed they were looking everywhere for a toilet, it was because one had drunk too much water before going to bed, which led to the urge to urinate. If one encountered a situation in reality that made one feel at a loss, this might be expressed in a dream in the form of non-stop running.

When people said dreams came from the heart, it was due to this principle.

Lucid dreaming was a special physiological concept of dreams.

When dreaming, most people were incapable of rational thinking. Their ideas and thoughts diverged along with the flow of the dream, just like a mindless zombie.

However, in the lucid dream state, dreamers had the ability to think and recall as if they were awake in the dream. They would ponder upon and observe this dream state as if this were reality, and some people could even actively control the dream.

This was an exchange between the subconscious and the surface consciousness, an exchange between the ego and the id.

“To think that lucid dreaming only happens once in a while. Yet it occurred again this time and it’s in such a situation... Sure enough, This Man has magical powers.”

The words of the man in the dream still echoed in Marcus’ ears, but there was a mystified glint in Marcus’s eyes. He had a feeling that maybe the man in the dream was the meaning of dreams he had been searching for.

“Because I wanted you to appear, so you appeared. What’s the difference between This Man and God, then?”

He looked at the printed pattern of the weird face on the table again and a zealous look gradually emerged in his eyes...

...

Nighttime quickly arrived.

Sanimo had finished an entire day of class. He returned to the dormitory but found that his roommate was still not there.

“What is that guy doing these days?”

Sanimo muttered, a little puzzled. He opened the forum again, wanting to see what was new in the comments that day, but just after logging onto the website, he saw that there was an enlarged and bolded title pinned to the top of the forum’s homepage. It was none other than the one he saw last night.

At this time, the post did not just have dozens of comments like before. It had soared to tens of thousands of replies and the popularity of the post had surged through the roof.

“What the...”

Sanimo’s heart clenched. He hurriedly clicked on the post, only to see that countless people had posted underneath, yet their content was shockingly consistent!

[I shouldn’t read this post before going to bed. Can you believe it? After seeing this man’s photo, I saw him in a dream. The dream was very strange and the man seemed to have told me something!]

[What a coincidence, I also dreamt about him. The man said he was called This Man and he said a lot of reasonable things. Surely it can’t be...]

[That dream is disgusting. I don’t know if you guys are just trolling here, but I really dreamed of him. It was a nightmare and the point is I’m a person who rarely dreams!]

[How weird, this guy also appeared in my dream...]

[Did you all dream about him? No way, he also appeared in my dream!]

[...]

Sanimo kept scrolling down, comment by comment, page by page... He read surprisingly similar remarks. These comments were all saying the same thing, namely, after reading this post last night, the posters had all seen the man in their dreams!

Seeing this, Sanimo could not help frowning. It seemed that he could no longer cover up the source of this picture.

Quashing his intention to claim this picture as his own, Sanimo felt a little regretful. He then turned off the computer, leaned against his bed, and started swiping on his phone.

As he swiped, a wave of sleepiness overcame him silently. Sanimo yawned and then his head slowly dropped...

...

When Molly regained consciousness, she found herself standing in a dark and cramped room.

The fluorescent lamp above her head flickered with a buzz, but at its brightest, it could only illuminate a range of two or three meters while the rest of the room remained in a dim state.

This room was empty. The only thing there was an old wooden door directly in front of her and a sink next to the wooden door.

There was a steady sound of dripping water from the sink. On the old door, there was a frosted glass window and a dusky light shone in through this window, adding a new light source to the room.

There was only silence in the room.

However, in this silence, there was the faint sound of footsteps approaching.

“Clomp!”

“Clomp!”

“Clomp...”

“Who’s there...”

Molly, who was standing in the dark, felt a little uneasy. She slowly stepped back, keeping herself buried in the dark.

The footsteps were getting closer and the air in the room became more oppressive. Molly retreated to the corner of the room and backed into the wall completely.

At this moment, a dark shadow moved through the dim yellow light of the corridor and was reflected on the door window.

The sound of footsteps stopped.

“Who is it, who is there?...”

Molly asked again in a low voice, but her voice was already full of terror.

“Click...”

Suddenly, there was a sharp sound from the doorknob, which rang like thunder in Molly’s ears, causing her body to convulse. The next second, the wooden door suddenly emitted a long squeak that was extremely harsh and piercing, like an echo from hell.

As the wooden door slowly opened, a hand came in from outside the door. Molly covered her mouth tightly. From her perspective, she could see that the owner of that hand was wearing a dark red string on the wrist...

“Ahh!”

Molly sat up abruptly, clutching her chest in horror and constantly gasping for breath.

“Molly, what’s wrong?”

Her roommate’s voice came from the bed next to her, with a trace of concern. “Did you have a nightmare?”

“Seems like it...”

Molly nodded. She got out of bed and walked to the bathroom, and splashed plenty of cold water onto her face, which made her feel better.

She walked out of the bathroom again. She did not go back to sleep. Rather, after thinking about it, she took out from the drawer the pattern that she had retrieved from the club during the day and looked at it again.

Sure enough, the man in the dream was the same as the one in the pattern...

“What are you looking at?”

The roommate did not blame Molly for having disturbed her sleep. Instead, she ran to Molly’s side barefooted and suddenly saw the pattern in Molly’s hand.

“It’s so scary! What is this?”

Her roommate asked curiously.

“This is...”

Molly opened her mouth and then recalled the name Sanimo had said when she was in the club during the day, so she promptly said, “He’s called... This Man.”

“This Man?”

Her roommate let out a whisper.

Ignoring the words of her roommate on the side, Molly began to recall her dream earlier, but at this time, she found that her dream had begun to turn blurry. The only thing she remembered was the scene in which the man opened the door and walked toward her step by step.

The strangest thing was that, while walking, the man kept shaking his head until Molly had started to see double...

He seemed to have said something or perhaps he had not said anything. Nonetheless, Molly had forgotten after waking up from the dream.

Molly scratched her head in distress, anxious to remember what he had said, but the more she tried, the less she could remember.

Then she spotted her wrist from the corner of her eye and suddenly, she had a flash of realization!

He seemed to have worn a red string on his wrist.

The moment she thought of this, a faint impulse appeared in her heart. As if possessed, she slowly opened the drawer, found a piece of red string, and wore it around her wrist.

The moment she put it on, a feeling of peace gently washed over her heart. It felt as if this red string could protect her from harm.

Then, she climbed into the bed again and slowly fell asleep...

...

When Sanimo woke up again, it was early in the morning.

He stretched lazily, having slept very well last night. His fatigue was erased.

There was still the picture of This Man on the table, but at this moment, in his eyes, this picture was not as scary as before. This was because in the dream last night, he and This Man had sat side by side and carried on a long conversation about many things.

“Unexpectedly, I dreamed of him not only on the first day but also last night...”

Sanimo shook his head and was just about to get up for class, but at this moment, he heard a loud commotion coming from the window.

“What’s going on?”

Sanimo stood up curiously, opened the curtains, and looked out. He saw a large crowd of people gathered at the bottom of the building. These people had assembled in front of the bulletin board and were discussing something heatedly. Some were exclaiming softly while others were laughing constantly.

In the crowd, Sanimo even saw Marcus.

Maybe there was a new announcement or something?

Seeing this, Sanimo was not too bothered. He quickly went to the bathroom to finish washing up, then put on clean clothes and walked downstairs.

He went all the way to the bottom of the building, only to realize that the group of people had not dispersed yet. Dozens of people had gathered here, looking at the huge bulletin board in front of the dormitory.

However, when Sanimo followed everyone’s gaze and looked at the bulletin board, he was stunned.

A huge white sheet of paper was posted on the bulletin board, which was the size of a blackboard. On the paper, there was a strange face with large, thick eyebrows, huge hollow eyes, and that wide mouth.

Who else could it be other than This Man?

Seeing this, Sanimo gasped sharply.

Just then, Marcus walked out of the crowd. While talking loudly to the people, he was about to turn around and leave.

Seeing this, Sanimo quickly went up and grabbed Marcus. “Hey, why had this pattern appeared here?”

“I enlarged it and printed it out.”

Marcus replied calmly, “And I also know that this picture wasn’t created by you. Instead, you found it on the Internet. Am I right?”

“Uh, yes.”

Sanimo nodded in embarrassment and then he said, “But this picture has the power to make people have nightmares. Won’t you cause trouble by doing this?”

“Who said you’ll have nightmares?”

Hearing this, Marcus stopped walking. He slowly turned his head and looked directly at Sanimo. “As long as you’re willing to accept This Man, he’ll drop the act and talk to you in an easygoing manner, right?”

Looking into Marcus’s eyes, Sanimo suddenly felt a chill for some reason. He nodded. “Well, that’s my fault. I didn’t have a nightmare last night... But...”

“There’s no but!”

Marcus squinted and put his hands on Sanimo’s shoulders solemnly. “Have you ever thought that This Man is probably evidence of God’s existence that we’ve been looking for?”

“God... What do you mean?”

Sanimo was even more confused. He subconsciously wanted to retreat, but his shoulders were so tightly gripped that he could not escape.

“A dream is a symbol of the human subconscious and the subconscious is a symbol of all thoughts and desires deep in the human heart...”

Marcus said with a serious expression, “It’s precisely because humans desire the appearance of God that countless people’s subconscious minds display a high degree of unanimity. When these unanimous forces are condensed in one place, they will affect reality sooner or later. Therefore, This Man appeared...

“This is what This Man told me personally. Because we want him to appear deep in our hearts, so he appears. Everything is that simple!”

“What are you talking about?”

Sanimo was completely dumbfounded. He looked at Marcus, whose expression gradually changed from calm to fanatical. At this moment, he seemed to be looking at a religious fanatic.

Staring at the somewhat crazy Marcus, he hurriedly broke free of the other guy’s grasp, only to notice that Marcus’ right wrist was tied with a thick red string.

“This is...”

Sanimo felt his scalp turn numb because he remembered that there was a piece of red string like this in the hands of This Man in his dream!

“Ah, you’ve noticed this.”

Seeing that Sanimo was staring at his wrist, Marcus laughed darkly as he raised his hand, revealing the dark red string on his wrist. “This is proof of belief in This Man. Sanimo, you’re my evangelizer, so you must wear this red string too, got it?”

“Marcus, you’ve lost your mind!”

Sanimo shouted in agitation. After that, he swiftly turned around and ran away, disregarding everyone’s surprised gaze. When he ran into the teaching building and when he could no longer see Marcus, he leaned against the wall and gasped heavily.

He managed to flee...

He had never thought that a mere pattern would have such a massive influence. Even Marcus was bewitched.

Thinking of this, Sanimo seemed to have thought of something. He quickly took out his mobile phone and logged into the dream forum again.

However, what made him shudder in fear was that it was not just at the top of the forum’s homepage. At this time, in the entire forum, almost every post on each page had someone talking about This Man and the indicator on the original pinned post also showed that it had been reposted more than 100,000 times.

In other words, This Man had broken free from this forum and had begun to spread on the Internet...

At this time, Sanimo, who had recovered his senses, felt a faint pang of remorse. Perhaps he should not have exposed this picture on campus.

The power of this picture was far greater and scarier than he had imagined.

chapters.title: Chapter 424: Prediction
chapters.body:
Chapter 424: Prediction

Chapter 424: Prediction

As more people saw This Man’s pattern and more reports about This Man surfaced, this strange unfamiliar face had gradually entered the public’s view.

A day later, on the streets of Los Angeles.

“I’m your reporter, Julia. Behind me now is a huge parade. We can see that more than ten thousand people have gathered here. These people claim that they have been called by God. They’ve spontaneously walked into the streets, propagating this God to passers-by. However, this god may not be God, but someone known simply as This Man...

“Among them, there’s no lack of former devout believers and even pastors, but at this moment, they’ve abandoned the Bible and their previous beliefs...”

As the camera zoomed in, one could hear in the video slogans such as “This Man is our Savior from heaven, This Man is supreme”, and so on. Behind Julia, there was an endless crowd of people. These people kept shouting while waving logos made by This Man, following the flow of the masses in the street.

“What is This Man? Is he a person? Or is he a god... This is Julia from CNN reporting to you.”

Sanimo looked at the news report on the TV in a daze. He shook his head and picked up the remote control to change the channel.

“This Man — according to legend, everyone who sees his face will dream of his existence after going to sleep. This phenomenon cannot be explained by scientists yet.”

After he skipped CNN, Sanimo found the next channel, NBC, also discussing the topic of This Man.

The host Alan looked serious. “Some people think that This Man is an omen of the imminent death of humankind because before living things die, their thoughts will converge as one. Others think that This Man is the manifestation of fragments from humanity’s collective consciousness. It was born in the collective consciousness of human beings, so it has the ability to travel through dreams.

“There are also some who think that This Man is none other than a god... So, what is This Man? What are the benefits and harms that he will bring to mankind?”

Hearing this, Sanimo could not refrain from changing to another channel.

This time, he landed on ABC TV.

“This Man is a demon from hell. I must judge him!”

Two men in suits stood on the stage. One was shorter and the other was taller. At this time, the shorter man suddenly roared, “This Man, you’re a demon from hell! I shall judge you right now!”

With that, he shut his eyes tightly and pointed his finger at the camera. “I’ll judge you in the name of God!”

The taller man on the side immediately closed his eyes when he heard these words. He clasped his hands together in a moved and profound manner, appearing to be praying.

“You’re a saboteur! You’re a killer, you’re a demon!”

The shorter man yelled passionately, “Get out of here. Your power has been destroyed by me. Get out of here, because I shall put you on trial!

“I demand, I demand, I demand that you disappear immediately! I cast you out of your kingdom!”

As he spoke, he pointed his finger to the ground and chanted, “You’ve fallen and crawled on the ground, just as God commanded you when he put his foot on your head in the Garden of Eden! This Man, you’ll be vanquished, no longer able to run amuck!”

Hearing this, the taller man hurriedly shouted, “Never again! Never again!”

“The catastrophe has ended and Northern America has recovered its former tranquility and peace... Hallelujah!”

Seeing this, Sanimo could not help but twitch his mouth and he simply turned off the TV in the dormitory.

As This Man was everywhere on the Internet, Sanimo did not go online at all but had turned on the TV. Who would have thought that the programs on TV were completely taken over by This Man as well?

What he did not know was that at this time, the entire Earth Federation was already assessing this matter seriously...

Currently, more than a hundred representatives had gathered in the conference room of the Earth Federation headquarters.

“Everything that is happening now is very abnormal.”

The representative from Northern America wore a somber expression. “After our preliminary summary, we’ve ascertained that all humans who have seen the picture of This Man will dream of him that night and there has never been an exception.”

“Is that so?”

The representatives of the other continents looked at each other when they heard these words. Among them, the representative of the European Continent said immediately, “But there’s no such situation on our side...”

“Could it be because This Man hasn’t spread that far yet?”

The representative of Northern America could not help but say bitterly, “Maybe in a few days, a similar situation will happen on your side.”

“The picture of This Man has indeed spread to where we are.”

The representative of Oceania shrugged. “But unfortunately, except for you guys, the entire world has not come across any similar phenomenon. Perhaps the riots and demonstrations on your side are due to other factors...”

The representative of the Northern American Continent naturally would not believe these words. He turned his head and looked at the representatives of other continents. “What about the rest of you?”

“We haven’t come across that yet.”

The representative of Mainland China replied with a calm expression, “Moreover, everything about This Man, including his name and the pattern, have been blocked by our entire network. Although some people claimed that they dreamed of This Man, after we summoned them to local police stations, we discovered that they were all rumors to gain attention.”

Upon hearing this, the representative of Northern America was astounded.

After asking around, there was no similar situation in other parts of the earth except Northern America. At this moment, there was a sudden knock on the door of the conference room and an assistant walked in. She leaned down and said something in the ear of the Northern American representative.

“Are you serious?”

There was a burst of shock on the face of the Northern American representative. He suddenly raised his head and stared at the assistant.

“It has been confirmed.”

The assistant nodded.

“I see. You may leave...”

The representative of the Northern American Continent looked solemn and he waved his hand to send the assistant away.

“What happened?”

The representative of the European Continent asked immediately.

“Our Ministry of Defense verified one thing just now.”

He took a deep breath, wearing a glum expression. “There are some Northern American citizens who have fled abroad due to the riots. They found that after they left Northern America, they no longer dreamed of This Man...”

After hearing this, the representatives of the other continents had varying reactions. It was hard to tell what they were thinking.

“It seems that we’ve found the reason the This Man phenomenon hasn’t occurred in other continents.”

The representative of the European Continent pushed up his glasses after hearing this. “This is a regional phenomenon, only within Northern America?”

“If this speculation is true, it’s even more frightening...”

The representative of Mainland China immediately said, “Because this represents two possibilities. First, This Man’s influence is limited in scope; second, the other party only wants to attack the Northern American Continent.

“But no matter which explanation it is, it means that This Man or the phenomenon of This Man, is likely to be man-made...”

“Man-made?”

Everyone looked surprised. They were all elected as the official representatives of every continent in the Earth Federation, so their IQ was higher than average. Therefore, when this was said, the implications of this possibility occurred to them at once.

“Oh no!”

The representative of Northern America seemed to have thought of something. He stood up abruptly, then reached into his collar and took out a pendant shaped like a cross. “Everyone, if everything you’ve hypothesized just now is true, it means that the other party is attacking Northern America. I’m going to use this year’s emergency quota for the Quantum Deity system. There should be no objections from you, right?”

As he spoke, he glanced at the representative of Mainland China.

“Of course. The Earth Federation is intricately connected, allies through thick and thin. We in Mainland China also don’t wish to see Northern America being defeated by unknown forces.”

The representative of Mainland China smiled and took out the pendant under his collar, only his pendant was in the shape of a twisting dragon.

“What about the representatives of the other continents?”

The representative of the Northern America Continent looked at the others.

“No objections.”

“Seconded!”

“Seconded!”

Everyone took out their pendants and voiced their agreement.

The congress of the Earth Federation was extremely efficient. After all authorized representatives had agreed to Northern America’s request, everyone immediately walked toward the channel where the quantum computer Adam was housed.

After intensive inspections, everyone was even irradiated with X-rays in a sealed room. After confirming that there were no other objects on everyone’s body, they got onto the electromagnetic levitation elevator.

The case of Buckle Murphy, the representative of Northern America, more than a month ago, was still vivid in their minds. He had hidden an object behind his eyes and brought it into the quantum laboratory in an attempt to destroy the quantum computer, Adam.

Fortunately, the quantum computer was physically isolated in super bulletproof glass and he finally failed to open it.

Still, after this incident, the security for the quantum computer Adam had been taken to a higher level and no person was allowed to enter the quantum core area. If one wanted to enter, one must be accompanied by at least three people.

At this time, the many representatives stepped into the quantum core area one by one. After that, the representatives of Northern America, Mainland China, the North Sea, and Europe stood on all sides of the quantum computer and at the same time, they raised their pendants.

In the next second, the quantum computer suddenly emitted four rays of light, shining on the four pendants through the glass.

A wondrous scene took place. These four pendants directly rose from the palms of everyone’s hands and were suspended in mid-air!

At the same time, this quantum computer suddenly trembled slightly, making clanking sounds like the collision of glazed glass. At the same time, it composed speech that seemed to be formed by the collision of jade objects —

“Identity confirmed!”

As this voice spoke, the representative of Northern America immediately took a step forward and said eagerly, “Adam, enter the directional prediction mode and predict all information about This Man!”

“Please confirm that the Northern America Continent will consume this year’s prediction quota and the prediction is related to the occurrence of the ‘This Man’ incident in Northern America?”

The quantum computer verified again.

“Confirmed!”

With the order from the representative of Northern America, the quantum computer suddenly broke out into a harsh hum while it vibrated slowly at a frequency visible to the naked eye...

If one had not witnessed all this, who would have imagined that the quantum computer, which was far beyond the scope of human technology, was operating in such a manner that was unheard of...

Seeing this, everyone took back their pendants again, put them in their pockets, and then exited the quantum core area in an orderly manner.

After leaving the quantum core zone, the representative of the Northern America Continent secretly heaved a sigh of relief. He looked around at everyone and said sincerely, “No matter what the final prediction is, I hope that if we can’t solve the problem, you can convince your governments to help those of us in Northern America.”

“Of course, we will not stand idly by.”

Everyone nodded and agreed.

However, before they had even finished agreeing, the laboratory’s broadcast made a sudden announcement. “Emergency meeting, emergency meeting, representatives from all continents, please go to the conference room quickly! Repeat, emergency meeting...”

“It’s out so soon?”

The representatives of all the continents were taken aback for a moment and hurried to the conference room. In less than two minutes, everyone had returned to the conference room again.

Even so, after coming to the conference room, the expression of the Northern American representative sank.

On the computer screen of a supercomputer in the conference room, a large number of disturbance curves had emerged.

There were a lot of complicated values marked on it. These values were so complicated that only this group of representatives could understand them. At this time, seeing the data on the screen, the representatives of the continents could not help inhaling sharply.

“This is a crisis disturbance curve!”

The representative of the European Continent looked anxious with a hint of anger. “This kind of curve means that it’s a matter of life and death for the Northern American Continent, but why did Adam not warn us before?”

“Perhaps because this is a disaster in Northern America, but it isn’t a disaster to humans in the rest of the world?”

The representative of the Indian Continent immediately guessed. “After all, it’s just a computer which hasn’t been programmed with human emotions and thoughts?”

“No, Adam’s ability is known to everyone here. A quantum computer with this kind of computing power can simulate the evolution of everything in the world. It’s impossible that it wouldn’t understand what the demise of Northern America means to the entire human civilization!”

The representative of Oceania immediately explained, “It’s smarter than you and me here!”

“This isn’t the time to talk about this, gentlemen!”

The representative of Northern America was already like a cat on a hot tin roof. He walked to the computer in two or three steps and began to retrieve other information. He had to know — what This Man was, who created it, and how they should solve it.

However, as he looked over the information sent by Adam, he was rooted to the ground in shock.

This was because he found that, besides the possibility of Northern America’s destruction, there was nothing else in the computer.

In regards to all predictions regarding This Man, the quantum computer was like a blank slate...

chapters.title: Chapter 425: Investigation And Showdown
chapters.body:
Chapter 425: Investigation And Showdown

Chapter 425: Investigation and Showdown

The fifth day, in the evening.

The Blacklight Biotechnology Headquarters Building in the Eco Science City, the highest 62nd floor.

Chen Chen was sitting before a huge red sandalwood desk. In front of him, a barbell loaded with a large number of plates was suspended in the air as if a giant invisible hand was holding it and even twirling it flexibly.

While this huge barbell was spinning at high speed, it even dragged the air and made a whirring sound. Chen Chen believed that if anyone was hit by the barbell now, they would immediately be shattered into pieces.

It was only ten minutes later when Chen Chen felt a slight warmth in his nose. He subconsciously touched it but noticed his hand was stained with blood. With a glance from him, the huge barbell flew backward.

With a “clang”, the barbell parked itself steadily in the corner of the office, becoming still.

“The weight is 300 kilograms, the time is 13 minutes and 25 seconds, so my Field can exert more than 9,000N.”

Immediately, Little X walked up from one side and said, “Sir Godfather, in three months, your Field energy has shot up by six times. You have seen a significant improvement almost every day!”

“That’s right, now I have the confidence to physically fly.”

Chen Chen nodded. With his current power of Field, he was already able to fly at high altitudes. Due to his light bodyweight, he was able to hold on for much longer than thirteen minutes. Nonetheless, this force was not enough to make him fly at supersonic speed.

After he inwardly summed up today’s training experience, Chen Chen took the tissue paper Little X handed him, wiped the blood off his face, and then waved his hand and opened the international website with the environmental holographic simulator.

At once, all recent information about This Man appeared on the major web pages.

“In just five days, Northern America has almost spiraled into chaos.”

Seeing the web pages floating in thin air in front of him, Chen Chen was deeply moved. “It seems that in the Internet era of big data, the transmission ability of memes is greater than I thought. Faced with such memes, humans could do nothing but be toyed to death.”

Little X held her chin like a tiny adult and nodded. “Well, this is the scary thing about memes. Not only can it easily control life and death, but it can even easily control people’s hearts?”

“At present, this seems to be the case.”

Chen Chen nodded. He suddenly seemed to think of something and asked again, “Has the work in other continents been completed?”

“It’s not finished yet, but we can guarantee that This Man’s influence will be suppressed to a very low level.”

Little X naturally understood what Chen Chen was asking and immediately replied with confidence, “I only need to allocate a small part of my computing power to monitor the world. Nowadays, except for Northern America, I’ve planted back doors in the TV stations of all continents. As long as these people watch TV, I’ll show a frame of ‘This Man’s Immunity Pattern’ on their screens every ten minutes.

“Because there’s only one frame, the pattern would disappear before they even notice it, but the effect of this one frame is enough for them to become immune to the This Man meme.

“On the Internet, I have also specially created several pop-up advertisements. These will be disguised as advertisements of the website and inadvertently pop up when netizens go online. The advertisements also contain a frame of the immunity pattern. As long as their IP address isn’t in Northern America, I can get these ads to be delivered accurately...

“With these two methods, I’ve probably immunized four-fifths of the world’s population and the remaining people are those who rarely have access to computers, TVs, and mobile phones. If even my large-scale immunity delivery can’t reach them, the chances of them being infected by This Man is zero, so we don’t need to think about that.”

After hearing Little X’s report, Chen Chen nodded appreciatively. “Good job, thank you for your hard work.”

“I don’t find it hard at all.”

Little X shook her head vigorously, with a serious expression.

At this moment, Chen Chen suddenly walked to the floor-to-ceiling window in a few steps, frowning and looking down at the masses of sentient beings under his feet. “I have to say that it’s a miracle we have developed to this point. There are so many things in the universe that we still cannot understand. If a supernova explodes dozens of light-years away, it can still kill the people on Earth.”

“It’s no wonder that Zhao Shanhe said that, although since World War II there haven’t been any major disasters on the earth, it’s just a coincidence. Human civilization is like a sandcastle built on the beach. Perhaps the next moment, a wave would crash in and the sandcastle would disappear completely...”

“That’s why they need us.”

Little X hopped up from behind, raising her white tender hands to knead Chen Chen’s forehead. “Human civilization needs quantum computers to overcome difficulties for them and you also need me to help you remove all obstacles that stand in the way of your dreams!”

Chen Chen smiled and did not speak but at this moment, Little X looked serious again and said, “Sir Godfather, I’ve detected that the shield bearer in Northern America has used the year’s only quota for the quantum computer, Adam, to predict everything about This Man. However, in Adam’s prediction, only Northern America has a curve disturbance that indicates a high probability of extinction.”

“Looks like they can’t sit still anymore.”

Chen Chen nodded. “What about the original data? If we didn’t implant a back door into the quantum computer, would it predict that we did all of this?”

“Let me see.”

Little X closed her eyes, connected to “Adam” via the quantum USB flash drive, and then opened her eyes again within a few seconds. “No! Just like the three statues in the past, it can only predict some key information at most, but we wouldn’t be discovered.”

Upon hearing this, Chen Chen immediately asked, “What is the reason?”

“One factor is my existence.”

Little X sounded triumphant. “Similar to how my ‘Prophet System’ can’t uncover it, it can’t uncover me as well. The bigger reason is that the meme’s behavior has exceeded its framework of cognition. The only thing ‘Adam’ can do is derive clues about the meme by colliding the quantum possibilities with its own predictions.”

“That’s already impressive.”

Chen Chen said ruefully, “That is to say, a quantum computer has the ability to make predictions based on nothing. Even without any clues, it can also predict using quantum space-time effects and this ability is something you don’t have.”

“I’m indeed a little lacking in the ability to predict the future, but I’ll kick its *ss in all other aspects!”

Little X puffed up her cheeks and said.

“That was not what I meant...”

Chen Chen hurriedly smiled and assured her. “I mean, if its quantum power is combined with your Prophet System, would it be possible to predict the past and the future more precisely and with a greater range?”

“Hmm, that’s true...”

Little X then made a thoughtful face.

“Alright, don’t be angry. I worded it wrongly.”

Chen Chen ruffled Little X’s hair. He had just stood up when Little X spoke again, “Sir Godfather, Cheng Cao has something to report to you.”

“Connect him.”

Chen Chen said decisively and at the same time, Cheng Cao’s voice rang out directly in the office.

“Boss...”

Cheng Cao’s voice still sounded as stoic as always. “As per your request, I’ve found Wang Luo. She’s the deputy director of the political department in the Shangdu Branch of the Chinese Academy of Sciences. However, according to my investigation, she hasn’t contacted Wang Xi in all her life and there’s no such name as Wang Xi in all of her information.

“But the weird thing is that when I tracked down her mother Zhou Yun’s address, I found that Zhou Yun only included herself in the household register, but the page of the head of the household was left empty, completely blank...”

Chen Chen sighed secretly when he heard this. Of course, he knew why the main page of Zhou Yun’s register was blank.

“Not only that...”

Cheng Cao continued. “There’s a lot of men’s clothes in Zhou Yun’s house, but Zhou Yun is unfazed by these clothes and can even be said to be ignoring them. So I explicitly ordered a subordinate to pretend to be friends with Zhou Yun and find out what she thinks. Only then did we discover Zhou Yun’s impression was that she has always had those men’s clothes and she doesn’t know whom those clothes belonged to.

“And when my subordinate asked whom her husband was, Zhou Yun herself also displayed a moment of confusion, but this confusion quickly disappeared, and her subconscious seemed to automatically avoid these topics...”

“I see. Cao, continue to investigate and find out everything that you can.”

Chen Chen gave this order and then asked Little X to cut the call.

“Sir Godfather, are you still worried about Wang Xi?”

Little X asked apprehensively.

“Indeed. All the information about Wang Xi no longer exists in this world and even you will forget him. This kind of antimeme is really horrifying...”

Chen Chen looked solemn. “I don’t know if Wang Xi is dead or if he had gone into hiding intentionally, let alone what the existence of the USB drive has to do with him, but there must be a terrible conspiracy in all this...

“A conspiracy that spans more than 30 years and involves me, Zhao Shanhe, and even the owner of the USB flash drive before Zhao Shanhe...”

Chen Chen continued. “Moreover, I have a bigger question — if the USB flash drive had existed in the world as early as thirty years ago, that is, at the time of Zhao Shanhe, has it always been in the form of a USB flash drive?”

“Huh?”

Little X was stupefied when she heard Chen Chen’s words. After hearing this, she immediately realized that the earliest USB flash drives had only appeared in 1998 and the USB port only began to appear in 1996. In that case, before 1996, during the time of the last USB drive owner Zhao Shanhe, what kind of form did the USB flash drive take?

“Could it be that the USB flash drive can still change its form?”

Little X asked.

“That’s undetermined.”

Chen Chen shook his head. “But haven’t you noticed? In Zhao Shanhe’s video, he didn’t call the USB drive a ‘USB drive”, but used a more intuitive name, ‘dimension key’, right? Does this mean that when he was holding the USB flash drive, it didn’t exist in the form of a USB drive?

“Besides Professor Wang Xi’s disappearance, there’s almost no evidence that he’s related to the USB flash drive, but the more this is the case, the more it means that there’s a deep secret in all this, and this secret is probably extremely dangerous...”

Having said this, Chen Chen suddenly thought of something. He said abruptly, “By the way, I keep forgetting to check one thing. The file about Zhao Shanhe that you searched for, where is the server storing this file?”

“Let me check.”

Little X closed her eyes and then opened them again. “I’ve found it. That server containing the file related to Zhao Shanhe is stored in the school server room of Shangdu Jiao Tong University. There are a total of six servers in this server room and the server with the file is No. 1, which is also the oldest server. The server was set up in 2003.”

“The timing matches...”

Chen Chen narrowed his eyes when he heard these words. “Tell Cheng Cao to get that server and ship it over.”

“Okay, I’ll give the order now.”

Little X replied.

“Remember to tell Cheng Cao to be careful.”

Chen Chen continued. “As far as I know, it’s likely to be the first thing that can withstand antimemetic erosion. If that’s the case, the server itself is likely to have buried clues about Zhao Shanhe and it’s even more likely to house unknown dangers.”

After giving this warning, Chen Chen returned to his seat and fell into deep contemplation.

...

While Northern America was abuzz due to This Man incident, Blacklight Biotechnology began to release an even more shocking piece of news in the entire Earth Federation.

This news was outside Northern America and instantly suppressed the popularity of This Man, becoming headline news in all continents —

Blacklight Biotechnology announced to the world: The department of Biological Research Center in the Eco Science City, on 16 December 2024, had deciphered 99% of the cancer genetic chain and could now use genetically modified cell technology to cure all known human cancers!

Once the news was announced, the entire world was in an uproar.

If there were history books in this world, then in future history books, 2024 would be the first year that human beings formally eliminated cancer...

Following that, another update was also released — due to political oppression by the Northern American Continent, Blacklight Biotechnology was determined, from this day onward, to withdraw their FDA applications for three cancer-targeted drugs namely, the targeted drugs for “lung squamous cell carcinoma”, “triple-negative advanced breast cancer”, and “poorly differentiated advanced combined liver cancer”. Phase III clinical trials in Los Angeles, Northern America, would also be suspended indefinitely.

chapters.title: Chapter 426: Cards On The Table
chapters.body:
Chapter 426: Cards On The Table

Chapter 426: Cards on the Table

FDA is the abbreviation for the “Food and Drug Administration” in Northern America. Any drugs that wanted to be officially approved in the Northern American market had to go through this application process. Most continents also had similar institutions such as Europe’s EDQM or China’s NMPA.

Blacklight Biotechnology had canceled the FDA application of three targeted drugs in Northern America, which meant that these three types of drugs were not about to enter the Northern American market.

If these were ordinary drugs, no one would care about such an announcement. After all, if one drug was gone, there were always other drugs to substitute it and the FDA would not simply approve any Tom, Dick or Harry. The review process could take several years and the standards were extremely strict.

Nevertheless, Blacklight Biotechnology was different. The drugs it had applied for were unique in the market. They were highly effective with a cancer cure rate of over 90%. Once these era-defining drugs were withdrawn from the Northern American market, the one who lost out would be Northern America.

Therefore, after learning that the three targeted drugs would not hit the market in Northern America, the Northern American Department of State promptly protested, claiming that this was a monopoly, a sanction, and denied any intention of sanctioning Blacklight Biotechnology. The Northern American Continent, which had always been powerful, began to play the part of the victim and voiced a firm protest against Blacklight Biotechnology.

[Blackwatch Corporation Refuses to Launch Their Highly Effective Drugs in Northern America. Following This Man Incident, the Northern American Authorities Suffer Another Heavy Blow!] — Wall Street Journal.

[Imposing Sanctions on the World’s Foremost Economy With Just the Power of a Corporation, Domineering Side Exposed!] — Tencent News.

[Blackwatch Corporation’s Latest Announcement Claims That It Can Solve 136 Types of Cancers Known to All Humanity. Truth or Hogwash?] — BBC Express.

[After the Official Website of Blacklight Biotechnology Issued Two Announcements, the Entire Earth Federation Was Shocked!] — UC Subscription.

Furthermore, netizens all over the world were having a field day because of this incident. Whether it was on YouTube, Twitter, Facebook or the major forums, one could see discussions about this matter everywhere.

[Has Blackwatch Corporation eliminated cancer? I don’t believe it. If they say they have solved several of these cancers, I can still kinda believe it. Solved them all? It’s too exaggerated.]

[OP, didn’t you see the announcement? They directly deciphered the cancer heredity chain at the genetic level. Got it? They cracked cancer at the source, it doesn’t matter what kind of cancer it is.]

[Person from China here. I’ve finally realized what monopoly and dictatorship mean. Surely they’re being too harsh!]

[Hehe, no matter how powerful this company is, it doesn’t even have the fundamental principle that the whole world should work as one and openly discriminates against Northern Americans. This kind of company deserves to be sanctioned. It’s best if they go bankrupt as soon as possible and then share the technology with all humankind. Chinese cyber-troopers, don’t even bother wielding your pitchforks.]

[I’ve already blacklisted the idiot above me. They are using Chinese, yet even the sh*t they spew comes out the wrong way. Thanks for enriching my blacklist.]

[Blacklight Biotechnology has recently been making headlines in the world many times. From a private company that nobody cares about to a pharmaceutical giant today, what has this company gone through?]

[Rather than quarrel, why don’t we just disperse. What does a battle between gods have to do with us peasants, let’s just eat our popcorn and watch this unprecedented showdown.]

As the two announcements continued to go viral, there were supporters and critics and the group of people suffering from cancer paid more attention to the first announcement. They urgently needed to know whether this news was true or false or a half-truth that Blackwatch Corporation deliberately exaggerated.

Due to this, as the outcry became stronger, in the afternoon of the same day, the official website of Blacklight Biotechnology had issued a new announcement. This genetically modified cancer treatment technology would be reviewed and clinically tested in various countries within a week. Blacklight Biotechnology would not charge any fees, but would instead provide free medical treatments for participants in the clinical trials, striving to bring this new type of treatment to the market as efficiently as possible.

As soon as this news came out, the officials of the Earth Federation Headquarters, who had been a little displeased with Blacklight Biotechnology’s monopolistic decision, had nothing left to say.

At this time, the uproar was not just happening internationally. Things were in chaos on Chen Chen’s side as well. Calls were blowing up his phone. The callers were all from the helms of major financial groups and chairman-level personnel of giant international corporations.

“Sir Godfather, the Blanc Financial Group has also called. Will you take the call?”

“No.”

“Sir Godfather, the Edwards family and the Alexander family are calling at the same time. Take the call?”

“No.”

“Sir Godfather, the President of the Earth Federation Representative Association...”

“No.”

Chen Chen lay back serenely in his chair, unwilling to answer any calls.

He opened the secret network of God’s Forbidden Zone and saw that on the forum of God’s Forbidden Zone, several families were already cursing:

sigma08Philip: [What is wrong with you? I’m still going to such lengths to lobby the White House over here. How nice of you guys to drop an announcement like that! Yeah, just undo all the f*cking effort we’ve put in!]

sigma03John: [Why didn’t you notify us in advance? My stocks, f*ck!]

sigma01SimonPeter: [Chen Chen, why don’t you answer the phone? F*ck you!]

[System notification: User sigma01 has been banned by the system for 10 minutes due to using strong language. Please pay attention to your words and behavior.]

Seeing this, Chen Chen stood up languidly. He thought for a while and said, “Little X, inform all the members of the Disciple Council that we’ll have a holographic conference. I’ll give them an hour to prepare.”

“Very well.”
