However, since there was no press conference of any form, news of the agreement was not publicized.

After receiving word of the result of the negotiation, Chen Chen felt the weight lifted off his heart as well. With that, the conflict with Northern America had come to an end without the need to escalate the situation to an unrepairable state.

“I can’t believe the Northern American Continent with some of the most respected scientists and sophisticated technology were so easily convinced by the theory I simply strung together.”

Little X was especially baffled by how things have turned out. All the squabble about neutral quantum and psychological hypnotic patterns were made up by her on the spot.

The scientist Matthew Fischer’s research was real, but that was all there was to it. Besides, his research was mainly based on theory-crafting and conjecture and was largely unconfirmed speculation.

Which was why Chen Chen had Little X use this research thesis as a baseline to string together a stack of complete research and analytical report. Extra effort was put into adding experiment footage to manufacture a convincing research report.

Little X was concerned with how foolproof this tactic was since the Northern America Continent was unlike the Africa Continent. There was no chance of them not seeing through the lies. To her surprise, Northern America immediately perceived it as the gospel.

“Which is why they say that the most convincing lies in the world aren’t made out of thin air, but are sprinkled with bits of truth instead.”

Chen Chen shrugged and explained, “Don’t forget, this is the true nature of men. Confronted with This Man incident and this immunity pattern that can alleviate the memetic effect, even the best scientists will find themselves at a loss for what to do. Even if some scientists somehow stumbled into the questionable gaps in your theory, they’ll choose to question their judgment instead since the truth is evident and laid out before them that the immunity patterns are effective.”

As Chen Chen said this, he suddenly altered his tone, “Of course, this can only last in the short term. After some time has passed, they’ll notice that these documents are falsified, but what does it matter at that point?

“Since This Man incident is solved and the agreement has already been signed, there’s little else the Northern American government can do other than keep their head down and relaunch a research on This Man incident.”

Little X became enlightened and began to nod. “But what chances do they have at understanding memetic technology in the first place? I’d say it’d take a decade before they barely manage to scrape its surface?”

“A decade is being too generous, I wouldn’t bet on their success even if they had two decades.”

Chen Chen shook his head. “Of course, the context being that memetic effects don’t occur in the real world just yet. From what I understand, as society becomes more digitized and information-oriented, memetic effects will emerge sooner or later. It’s just like how everyone has a certain chance of contracting cancer. It’s merely a matter of probability.

“When the earliest form of memetic effect begins to emerge, the official parties will be the first to notice and begin to conduct research. As they start to understand the nature of memetic science, they’ll eventually realize that not only messages can be used to kill but it can also change the very essence of the world...”

As Chen Chen said this, he closed his eyes and wondered, what form of memetic effect would be the first to emerge in this world which he resided in?

“Sir Godfather, we received news from the Earth Federation.”

Little X suddenly informed, “Our list of technology has passed the review process of the Earth Federation. They have concluded that we don’t have implementations of any nuclear-form or weapons of mass destruction and we’re now cleared to conduct commercial exchange with the listed items.

“Currently, several continents and regional countries including the Central Continent have submitted applications to send someone over to take a look at our products.”

chapters.title: Chapter 432: Delta Grade Research Staff
chapters.body:
Chapter 432: Delta Grade Research Staff

Chapter 432: Delta Grade Research Staff

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

As the dust began to settle, the Northern American government started to regain a handle on the spiraling situation in the continent.

Although there were still many lingering problems that had to be addressed, at least the root of the problem had been dealt with. In time, the social order was starting to restore to a desirable state.

Meanwhile, things were progressing as usual in Eco Science City. The comings and goings remained the same as they had always been.

This city located on the western coast of Namibia, Africa played a major role in altering the trajectory of human civilization. It had gradually become a symbol of Africa and the veritable core capital of the region.

Little did they know, this city that had already achieved countless accomplishments that had left its mark in the history books would once again bring forth another miracle of unprecedented proportions...

...

Luis Pinar slowly opened his eyes in a dark and damp room.

He was startled to suddenly find himself here. It felt like it was only mere seconds ago when he was still having the time of his life in his mistress’ home. In the blink of an eye, he found himself in a damp cell hidden away from the warmth of the sunlight.

“Where in the world am I...”

Pinar pulled himself on his feet, his voice felt scratchy in his ears. It was only then when his eyes adjusted to the environment.

The small room was less than five square meters large. There were no windows or light sources installed, only four sturdy walls surrounding a tiny space with a black iron door on one side of the wall.

There was a palm-wide gap under the iron door that allowed a glimmer of dim light to seep into the room, offering just enough light for him to view his surroundings.

“How did I get here, d*mn, what’s going on here!”

Pinar started to panic when he finally made sense of his current predicament. He dashed to the door, got down on all fours, and stuck his head against the floor to get as close a look as possible at the world outside the iron door through the feeble gap.

The only thing he could see was a pale-yellow tiled corridor. There was nothing else notable.

Pinar immediately felt an indescribable pulse of fear surging from the depths of his heart and coursed all over his body and his breathing soon became erratic. He tried to crawl out of the room from the gap under the iron door but the gap was so small he could hardly pry his head outside, especially not his body that was growing plumper by the day.

“Help! Is there anyone out there!”

Within seconds, Pinar started to cry out for help. As soon as he cried out, he realized his mistake and immediately covered his mouth. His eyes were still widened with fear, fixed on the world outside through the tiny gap under the door.

Fortunately, nobody was drawn here by his voice.

Pinar heaved a sigh of relief as he stumbled back up and started to inspect the room.

The room was poorly furnished. The surrounding walls were depressing sheets of metallic iron while the floor was a layer of cold and hard marble. The only furniture in the room was the uncomfortably damp single bed positioned in the top left corner and a toilet on his right.

There was nothing else to be seen in the room...

Pinar began to jog through his memory. The last thing he could still vividly remember was sharing the bed with his mistress. He remembered he was bonding passionately with this mistress of him and the memory immediately cut to him waking up in this place. He had zero recollection of what happened in between these segments...

“D*mn it, who in the world kidnapped me!”

Pinar cursed out in a low voice. He was a congressman in Namibia and he had never imagined something ill-fated like this to ever happen to him. He thought, what sort of vile man would ever do something as despicable as this?

While Pinar was debating this question in his mind, he suddenly heard an agitated, piercing scream coming from somewhere outside. “B*stard, who are you! Let me out!”

Pinar’s expression turned sharply as he immediately threw himself on the ground again to investigate what was outside. No matter how hard he looked, he could not find the source of that voice.

It sounded like it came from right next to him!

Pinar quickly connected the dots and realized that this tiny black box he was in must not be the only such room in this place and that he must not be the only person locked here...

“Let me out, b*stard!”

The voice Pinar had heard soon turned into a bellowing roar as he could hear the desperation in this voice. After listening to it for a while, he seemed to have made out who this person was, it was...

“Is that you, Senator Nujoma!”

Pinar figured it was worth wagering a guess.

The bout of screaming immediately stopped. After a moment, he heard the voice asking, “Is that Congressman Pinar?”

“It’s me!”

Pinar felt himself becoming reenergized, feeling like he had found someone who could relate to his predicament. He got as close as he could to the door and shouted, “How did you get here, Senator Nujoma? Who brought us here?”

“I don’t know...”

To his disappointment, the answer he received did not provide any further clarity. He heard the senator explaining, “The last thing I remember is driving away from the parliament, I didn’t drive long before I suddenly lost consciousness. By the time I woke up, I found myself here... How about you, Congressman Pinar?”

“The same as you, I suddenly lost consciousness...”

Pinar mustered a strained reply before sinking into the floor, feeling defeated.

The good news was that although he was well into his sixties, the condition of his body had been greatly restored ever since undergoing Blackwatch’s age-reversal treatment. Not only could he afford to work longer hours, but he also found two mistresses for himself. He was fully experiencing his youthful prime again.

Hold on, Blackwatch?

Pinar felt a momentous explosion in his mind. He suddenly recalled the existence of this company and with it, remembered the reason he was in this place...

He remembered that when news of the Northern American government deciding to impose sanctions on Blackwatch began to circulate online, the Namibian congress immediately conducted a meeting discussing this matter.

The congressmen participating in the meeting were wholly separated into two factions. One faction brought up how much impact Blackwatch had on Namibia and that it was a wise choice to join forces with them and refute the sanctions of Northern America.

The other faction was convinced that Northern America was simply too powerful for Namibia to do anything about them, that it was a fruitless task trying to protect Blackwatch. They argued that it was better to comply with Northern America and impose sanctions on Blackwatch. There was a chance Namibia could come out on top in this situation and gain something from it.

Both himself and Senator Nujoma in the next room belonged to the latter faction.

Could this mean that it was Blackwatch who kidnapped the two of them?

Pinar took a deep breath to calm himself down as much as he could. If this was the case, there was still a chance for him to crawl out of this mess. All he needed to do was to give his word that he would side with Blackwatch in the future instead of taking the opposing party’s side. Perhaps there was still a chance to salvage the situation?

Pinar took a faint breath of relief upon considering this option. Before he had time to relay this to his fellow cellmate, he heard approaching footsteps from the gap below the iron door.

“Someone – someone is coming!”

Senator Nujoma immediately started yelling, “Hey, I’m here! Are you here to rescue me? I’m over here!”

It was only then when Pinar realized that there was a fairly large group of people approaching at a steady pace. Due to how uniform their strides were, it almost sounded like it was only one person approaching.

Could it truly be the troops coming here to rescue them?

Pinar saw a gleam of hope. It would certainly be excellent if that was the case. Not only this evil company took over Namibia and emptied the political cabinet but they also reigned over the economic center of Namibia. Although nobody would ever voice this thought out loud, many prayed for the downfall of this company, and he was one of them.

However, he would find himself disappointed very quickly since he noticed that there was no sense of urgency in these footsteps even after hearing Senator Nujoma’s cry for help. This meant that the footsteps belonged to the guards patrolling the corridor instead of a task force arriving here to rescue them.

The footsteps grew louder by the second and soon arrived right outside the door. The shuffling footsteps ceased immediately and were promptly followed by what was the electronic beep of the door opening. The large, bulky iron door suddenly opened, granting a passage to the outside world!

Bright light immediately invaded the room in a split instant, forcing Pinar to put his arms up to guard himself instinctively. After that, he felt himself carried off by two people, one on each side. He felt his feet being lifted off the ground and carried off by a powerful force!

“Hold on, who are you guys and what do you want! I’m a member of the congress! I’ll have the lot of you jailed for this!”

Pinar heard Senator Nujoma belting out angrily behind him. When his eyes had grown accustomed to the fierce light outside, he opened his eyes and looked to his sides and noticed the two expressionless soldiers on each side, both wearing positive pressure protective suits as they dragged him away.

He was in the middle of a narrow, long-winded corridor...

“Congressman Pinar, tell them, tell them who we are. Where do they think they are taking us?”

Behind him, Nujoma was still bickering endlessly. Pinar turned to look in that direction just in time to make eye contact with the panicked senator.

“Give it a rest, Senator Nujoma.”

Pinar sighed and expressed bitterly, “Don’t you understand yet, we’re being kidnapped here and the perpetrator is none other than...”

Before Pinar had time to finish his sentence, the two of them were brought to the end of the corridor. The men escorting them pushed open the door to reveal a laboratory filled with unrecognizable devices.

“That’s right, it’s me.”

Pinar and Nujoma craned their necks to the second floor of the laboratory where they heard a low and hoarse voice coming from. They immediately recognized a familiar figure standing by the observation window!

“It’s you!”

Pinar forced himself to smile as flattering as he could manage as he said, “Chen Chen, please calm down, I can explain this...”

“Explain?”

Chen Chen sat down on a chair. Beside him, there were several researchers dressed in large white coats. Meanwhile, Chen Chen had a stack of documents in his hand and was going through it while addressing his captives without looking at them, “You take my money but work for someone else behind my back. In this case, there’s no reason for you two to remain in your current position. It’s that simple.”

“Chen Chen, there’s no need to do this, we can still talk about this.”

Pinar started to panic. He had a feeling that Chen Chen was not joking around so he tried to persuade him. Before he could begin, Nujoma, who was beside him, suddenly spoke, “Chen Chen, you dare to kidnap official congressmen? Are you foolish enough to believe you think you can remain in Namibia once word of this gets out? Don’t forget that you have just reconciled with Northern America. If you get caught with another scandal, you won’t get away as easily this time!”

Chen Chen slowly averted his gaze from the documents to Nujoma when he heard this. He smirked ever so slightly. “Nujoma, I admire your boldness. I just wonder, will I still see this boldness later when I start dealing with you?”

“What, what are you trying to do?”

Nujoma started to caution harshly. “I’m warning you now, you don’t get to do whatever you like in Namibia. If you kill me, the Namibian government won’t let it slide!”

“Alright, I’d like to see how they plan to not let it slide.”

Chen Chen snapped his finger to give a signal. Within seconds, two guards pinned Nujoma to the ground while another researcher stepped forward and injected a syringe of red liquid into Nujoma’s back!

“Ah!”

Nujoma cried out in pain. Before he was done howling in pain, the guards forced him on his feet again and threw him into a small room in the laboratory!

The first thing Nujoma did when thrown into the room was to try to charge back out the entrance, but the door was promptly shut tightly to close him in. Nujoma found himself enclosed in a tiny room made with structured glass.

The caged Nujoma began to relentlessly throw his body against the glass but the glass was extremely resilient and hardly budged at all no matter how hard he slammed against them.

As time went on, Pinar, who was observing from outside, started noticing strange behavior. He noticed that as Nujoma was bashing against the glass door, he began to subconsciously scratch some sort of itch on his back. In time, he gave up on his efforts of trying to escape from the room and was rolling around the floor and crying out in agony!

“It hurts! It’s so itchy! What did you guys do to me!”

Nujoma’s screams echoed across the isolation room. The sight of the expression he wielded was so full of despair that it made Pinar shudder.

Little did he know, this was only the beginning...

As Nujoma was howling in pain on the floor, Pinar started to notice what seemed to be a small lump forming on the senator’s back. Upon paying closer attention, he verified that it was not a false impression. There was certainly a fist-sized lump forming on Nujoma’s back!

“Splat!”

Moments after that, Nujoma’s shirt was ripped open. What was revealed underneath was a large eyeball connected to a thick vein of muscle. It popped out from his back and started to look around curiously like a newborn baby before it made eye contact with Pinar...

“Wah!”

Pinar cried out in horror. He was under the impression that the syringe contained some form of drug or toxic. What it turned out to be was something that was completely out of his expectation that he had trouble ever fathoming what he was seeing!

Within seconds, there were waves of ripples across Nujoma’s spine again. Yet another eyeball the size of a ball of fist popped out from his spine, followed by a third, then a fourth...

Nujoma’s cries were growing weak and feeble at this point. There were now dozens of eyes lined along his back. He looked like some sort of eyeball-infested fungus. Shortly after that, Nujoma’s entire body began trembling violently...

“Hnghh...”

Pinar heard an abrupt screech of muscles being torn apart viciously as an unnatural amount of blood gushed out of Nujoma’s back like a steady stream of a fountain. After that, a monstrous octopus-like creature with countless eyes lined along its tentacles ripped through Nujoma’s spine and climbed out from inside his body!

Nujoma was finally dead.

“You’re up next...”

Chen Chen said without a shred of emotion in his voice. Pinar felt his body freeze when he heard this...

chapters.title: Chapter 433: I’M The Law
chapters.body:
Chapter 433: I’M The Law

Chapter 433: I’m the Law

After seeing the two congressmen morphed into monstrous goo and reduced to nothing more than experiment statistics, Chen Chen finally set down the documents in hand and descended into deep thought.

“Boss, this is the final batch.”

Hannibal suddenly appeared behind Chen Chen. He bent down and reported cautiously.

Chen Chen responded with a brisk nod before getting up from his seat. He looked at Watson who was unsettled by this and offered some words of comfort. “There’s no need to fear, Mr. Watson. Incidents like this are entirely unavoidable in biological experiments. Think of it as the drug phasing process during clinical trials. The advancement of Eco Science City must be mounted on top of countless sacrifices in the first place, so wouldn’t you agree that it’s more worthwhile to use people who shouldn’t exist in the first place as the sacrificial lambs?”

“I understand...”

Watson was still uncomfortable with this approach but did not dare to say more than what was necessary. He only provided a firm nod.

Chen Chen turned to the trio of young members. “Hannibal, Li Lei, Neumann, why don’t you guys take this opportunity to learn from Mr. Watson. You guys have established a solid foundation over the last two years. All that’s left now is proper practice and experience. Watson has five to six decades of experience under his belt, there should be a lot for you guys to learn from.”

“Yes.”

The three of them nodded humbly.

With that, Chen Chen turned around and exited the laboratory.

After going through the disinfection protocol that took half an hour and removing his protective suit, Chen Chen emerged from the laboratory feeling refreshed. Little X, who had been waiting for him by the entrance, hopped to his side to greet him.

“How did the operation go?”

Chen Chen asked.

“Everything has been sorted out.”

The obedient Little X assured Chen Chen confidently. “I had the Black Knights manufacture traffic accidents, suicides, and cases of fleeing the country. From here on, nobody will ever suspect you for the disappearance and subsequent death of the congress members.”

“Perfect.”

Chen Chen smiled and no longer pursued the subject.

To be frank, it was impossible to completely rid himself of any suspicions concerning this string of incidents. Anybody who was not completely oblivious to matters of politics would at least have a faint idea of who was behind the disappearance and death of the congress members that were openly against Blacklight Biotechnology.

Still, the fact remained that there was no substantial evidence that could be traced back to Blacklight Biotechnology. Besides, considering Blacklight Biotechnology’s continuously growing influence in Namibia, there was hardly going to be anyone bold enough to challenge the puppet master and investigate the death of the deceased congress members.

After this round of massive purge, most of the ones who remained in congress were either the ones who already sided with Blacklight Biotechnology or the ones who took a neutral stance. Now that they had seen what happened to the naysayers, they should naturally figure out what their options were.

...

Time went by in a hurry. In the blink of an eye, it was the tenth of January. It was the day where Falcon 12 would be officially launched.

Falcon 12 was the brother of Falcon 9 and just like its sibling, it was one of the most ambitious projects of SpaceX and only recently constructed in 2021. Although Falcon 9 carrier rocket had gone through many design modifications, its rocket and engine technology were still primarily founded in the first decade of the twenty-first century. Now that there was more optimal technology, SpaceX came up with a new solution to substitute the outdated Falcon 9.

This was how Falcon 12 came to be.

Its predecessor Falcon was the world’s first rocket that could reroute back to Earth after entering orbit. When the seventy-meter tall behemoth descended from the skies above and completed its safe landing, the entire world rejoiced.

Since then, humanity had entered the era where rockets could be recalled back to Earth instead of being geared as one-time-use devices that may never return.

Falcon 12 inherited the monumental mission of Falcon 9. Not only could its primary rocket be salvaged, but its payload fairing could also be reused, therefore cutting down the budget significantly.

The cargo that Chen Chen needed to launch was the five Gantz satellites. With a carrier like Falcon 12, all five satellites could be launched in one go.

As far as Chen Chen was concerned, this was Blacklight Biotechnology’s most important mission over recent years. He specifically brought Qian Wenhuan with him and headed over to the aerospace base in person.

When Chen Chen and Qian Wenhuan arrived at the aerospace base, there was already a large crew of project managers and rocket engineers waiting outside for them.

“President Chen! President Qian!”

A short and plump Chinese man shaped like a ball immediately greeted them enthusiastically and shook their hands warmly as he started with the introductions. “Allow me to introduce you to Kevin over here. He’s the senior engineer of SpaceX and he’ll be the primary engineer overseeing today’s launch.”

“Mr. Chen Chen, I’m Blake Kevin, the primary engineer for today’s launch. You can just call me Kevin for short.”

A man in a plaid shirt stepped up to greet and shake hands with Chen Chen enthusiastically.

“Good to see you.”

Chen Chen shook hands with this person courteously. The plump man continued introducing Chen Chen to the rest of the engineers, who all shook hands with Chen Chen as well. After they were finished with the introduction, the group started to head to the launch site.

The plump man was Zhao Guangjie. He was a rocket manufacturing expert that Qian Wenhuan had recruited from the Mainland due to the lack of suitable personnel on Blacklight Biotechnology’s end. This man was put in charge of the aerospace base along with today’s launch operation.

The launch site was already packed with close to forty technicians, each monitoring and charting a large array of data. They were all members of SpaceX. At the front and center of the launch site was an enormous, transparent glass wall.

Through the glass wall, the colossal launch tower that looked like Optimus Prime stood hundreds of meters away white a white round-headed rocket aligned right next to it.

It was Falcon 12.

From this angle, Falcon 12 looked like an enoki mushroom. Its stem appeared extremely thin in comparison to its bulky head. This was the trademarked rocket design of SpaceX.

Zhao Guangjie stood by the observation deck along with Chen Chen and Qian Wenhuan where he declared ceremoniously, “President Chen, President Qian, feast your eyes on Falcon 12, destined to glide across the galaxies and bring mankind into the future!”

“Glide across the galaxies and bring mankind into the future?”

Chen Chen expressed his doubts. “We might be getting a little too ahead of ourselves.”

“President Chen, you might not be aware of this since you’re an outsider, but...”

Zhao Guangjie rubbed his head and continued to fawn over the rocket. “Falcon 12 stands 109.8 meters tall, just slightly below 110 meters, and its diameter is measured at 8.5 meters which makes it size equivalent to another of SpaceX’s creation, the ‘Starship BFR’. The Falcon is the most advanced rocket of its tier and equipped with the Merlin 1DMerlin engine. The force of its thrust reaches up to 240,000 pounds and it can carry cargo weighing up to 150 tons into the orbit...”

“Alright Mr. Zhao, we get that this is a marvelous invention. It’s time you tell us what you learned about it.”

Qian Wenhuan rubbed his eyebrows in irritation and cut Zhao Guangjie off. “You watched them construct this rocket from the ground up, surely you must’ve learned about the technology employed to construct it?”

“Not really, they prohibited me from getting a closer look during the construction process.”

Zhao Guangjie scratched his head and expressed frustratingly, “And they never answered whatever questions I had for them... Then again, this wouldn’t be too surprising considering these are technologies owned by SpaceX themselves. Wouldn’t it be equivalent to forgery if we copy their technology? That would be unethical...”

Zhao Guangjie started to explain his views, not noticing Qian Wenhuan’s face that was slowly turning dark by the second.

Qian Wenhuan specifically sought out Zhao Guangjie since he was not knowledgeable about the workings of this sector. Based on whatever information he could dig up online, he filtered the list down to several individuals such as Zhao Guangjie from the Mainland. He knew that there was no problem with this candidate when it came to academic qualifications.

However, he had never expected this candidate to turn out to be such an imbecile.

“Alright, that’s enough explaining.”

Chen Chen stopped Zhao Guangjie’s rant and pointed at the command center. “Kevin is preparing for the launch.”

They turned to the command center just in time to hear Kevin announcing, “Alright kids, we’ll be commencing the twenty-third launch of our Falcon 12 now. I want to see everyone standing at full attention. I’ll kick your butt if I see you dozing off!”

“Yes!”

Everyone cheered enthusiastically.

Kevin nodded contentedly when he saw the energetic response from his crew. He proceeded to tap on a button which summoned a series of countdown on a large screen everyone could see —

“Preparation for launch operation. Counting down from 30 minutes. All personnel is advised to revise all relevant data...”

A robotic female voice that spoke with a standard accent was broadcasted across the launch site. Upon hearing this prompt, every crew member turned to their respective control panels and started to meticulously conduct their tasks.

Meanwhile, a giant panel of display automatically rose from the center of the hall. Cast on the display was a constant stream of data that flowed like a waterfall. Anyone who was not an aerospace operation professional would have a hard time comprehending what these statistics meant.

A heavy, sinking atmosphere immediately descended onto the launch site.

Qian Wenhuan allowed himself a moment to marvel at the supreme discipline of this group of world-class experts. The level of SpaceX’s rocket launch team was on a completely separate league of their own.

Then he stole a sneaky glance at Chen Chen and noticed that there was not the slightest trace of emotion on Chen Chen’s face. It was impossible to make out what was on his mind.

One thing Qian Wenhuan was certain of was that Chen Chen had extremely ambitious plans in the aerospace industry, otherwise, he would not have invested such an enormous sum into constructing an aerospace base.

“Mr. Chen Chen?”

While Qian Wenhuan was letting his mind run wild with speculations, Kevin suddenly approached. “Mr. Chen Chen, you can bring your subordinates with you to the observation hall. That way, you can get a clear view of the launch from there instead of having to wait here.”

“That’s right, I’ve already had coffee and tea prepared there.”

Zhao Guangjie chimed in. “We can drink merrily while we watch Falcon 12 take flight, isn’t that a wonderful picture?”

Chen Chen darted a glance at Zhao Guangjie and informed, “That’s alright, I like standing here and waiting.”

“Mr. Chen Chen, that is against the regulations.”

In response to this, Kevin frowned and made a gesture. “The nature of the procedures of our launch are largely confidential. I’d appreciate it if the three of you would abide by international protocol and step away for the moment. Our crew will take care of this.”

“That’s right, President Chen. It’ll take another half an hour and it will bore you to have to wait here.”

Zhao Guangjie chimed in as well. Before he could finish his sentence, he felt someone pulling him from behind his back. He instinctively turned around and saw that it was Qian Wenhuan grabbing him with a foul look on his face.

“President Qian, what are you pulling me for?”

Zhao Guangjie asked curiously.

Paying no mind to the imbecile, Chen Chen glared viciously into Kevin’s eyes and declared word by word, “This is my property. I can be wherever I please. Meanwhile, all you need to worry about is to fulfill your duties.”

The temperature of the air around them seemed to have dropped several degrees as Chen Chen said this. Even several research members conducting their pre-launch inspection turned to look in this direction. Kevin was especially startled by this. He did not expect this young, wealthy man to be this stubborn. He maintained his position. “The technology implemented in the Falcon is an officially protected technology belonging to Northern America. We will not be proceeding with the launch if you insist on staying here!”

“How so, we paid for you guys to be here. The dynamic between us should be that of an employer and an employee. We’re the employers.”

Qian Wenhuan suddenly interjected and shouted before Chen Chen had to say anything else, “I want you to conduct the launch now, why aren’t you doing it?”

Qian Wenhuan may appear enraged on the outside, but what he was feeling deep inside was a sharp contrast. He was so frightened that he could feel sweat seeping through his shirt. He had been working for Chen Chen for so long that he had figured out Chen Chen’s personality. On the outside, this young man half a decade younger than him was cool, calm, and collected. What he never let show deep inside was his dark, vile ambitions that would make a Mafia Godfather appear like a saint when put next to him.

Kidnap, assassination, bribing officials, human experiments, smuggling firearms... The hands of this young man had been stained by the blood of countless people. Killing was merely a trivial matter for him. To think that someone would dare talk to Chen Chen like this, was this person trying to rush to his grave?

Qian Wenhuan who was well-versed with his line of work knew that this was not the time to remain silent. He raised his voice and declared, “If you don’t conduct the launch as requested, our company will get in touch with Musk and see what he has to say about his employees!”

“Even if you contact Mr. Musk, I’ll still hold my ground on this decision.”

What Qian Wenhuan had never expected was for Kevin to turn out to be such a stubborn person as well. It was either because this was what Musk had instructed him to do or that he played several roles, one of them being an informant for the Northern American government.

“Clap clap clap...”

It was then when Chen Chen suddenly started to clap. A faint glimmer of beam appeared in his eyes as he softly clapped his hands together. “Excellent, I admire someone with integrity.”

At the drop of his note, the tightly shut entrance was suddenly barged open. A squad of armed soldiers in combat suits charged inside. They were dressed like special forces with the B.S.S. badge strapped in front of their chest.

“B.S.S. Quick Reaction Force!”

Qian Wenhuan was stunned by this sudden turn of events. He immediately spotted Bando’s figure among the squad.

In an instant, the entire launch site was surrounded by B.S.S. force soldiers, driving the crew into a mass of panic.

“Mr. Chen Chen, what are you guys trying to do?”

Kevin froze for a moment when he saw what was happening before he exclaimed, “This isn’t the Namibian government troops but your private military force instead. Military force doesn’t have the authority to arrest us. You’re breaking the law...”

Before he could finish, several B.S.S. members charged in his direction and proceeded to slam the butt of their guns square on Kevin’s head!

“Ah! Stop!

“Ah! Oh my God, you people are demons... What about the law?”

The launch site descended into chaos. Everyone watched in bewilderment as the group of soldiers gave Kevin a horrible beating. It did not take long before Kevin’s screams came to a stop.

“Alright, does anybody still have any objections?”

Chen Chen asked as he looked at Kevin who was lying in a pool of his blood.

chapters.title: Chapter 434: Interstate Teleportation
chapters.body:
Chapter 434: Interstate Teleportation

Chapter 434: Interstate Teleportation

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

A group of vicious soldiers took over all the channels leading to the launch site and guarded over them. Meanwhile, the crew was forced to return to their stations. Some were frightened and some only visibly agitated. There were a few women who were weeping silently.

There was the scent of blood lingering in the air. It came from Blake Kevin who was still lying motionlessly on the ground. There was no telling if he was dead or not.

When Chen Chen saw that everything was back on track, Chen Chen checked the time before turning around to leave the launch site.

Qian Wenhuan immediately trailed after him.

After they had left the main section, Qian Wenhuan quickly explained somewhat embarrassingly, “I’m sorry Boss, I’ll immediately have Zhao Guangjie replaced with someone competent.”

Chen Chen responded with a short nod, “Don’t worry about it. I get that with the rapid expansion of Eco Science City, there’s more for you to manage so there are certain facets that you cannot oversee. It should be time for you to change your management approach and leave some trivial matters to someone else. You don’t have to do everything by yourself.”

“Understood...”

Qian Wenhuan answered quickly, feeling relieved by this.

“Alright, Zhao Guangjie isn’t reliable so you’re still going to have to take care of that. Why don’t you spend the next few days here for now.”

Chen Chen did not chatter too much with Qian Wenhuan before dismissing him. He stood in front of the launch tower by himself, silently taking in the sight of the gigantic Falcon rocket.

It was the height of the evening as there was only a slight trace of the fading sun glimpsing across the horizon.

Falcon 12 positioned in the middle of the sunset pastel looked like the massive robots from Pacific Rim. It also looked like a majestic ivory tower. Even now, countless vehicles could still be seen going back and forth from the tower to conduct the final inspection duties.

Chen Chen leaned against the railing and took a moment to bask at the moment. He watched as the red in the sky slowly faded and gave way to the dark, gloomy texture of the night.

“Preparation for the launch countdown. Ten minutes remaining. All remaining personnel on the rocket are to leave their designated posts. Falcon 12 initializing automated inspection protocol...”

The announcement pulled Chen Chen away from his state of trance. There were only ten minutes left. Without his noticing, all the lights in the aerospace base had been turned on.

Countless high-power lights illuminated the entire base. Under the glow of the dazzling light, Chen Chen saw the crew retreating from the rocket launch tower. In time, not a single soul remained on the rocket.

The final glimpse of sunset faded away, plunging the sky into complete darkness.

“One minute to launch...”

The announcement echoed across the aerospace base, signaling the launch that would be commencing within moments. Chen Chen felt the earth below his feet starting to tremble faintly. It was the rumble of the rocket engines warming up.

“Ten seconds to launch...”

“Nine...

“Eight...

“Seven...”

...

“Three...

“Two...

“One...

“Launch!”

With the last of the announcements, a low and dull roar resounded across the aerospace base suddenly.

It was like a biblical beast awakening from its slumber. From a distance, Chen Chen saw a large column of black smoke gushing out of the tail section of Falcon 12, immediately followed by a sharp flash of flames!

The column of flame immediately grew stronger as it sent thunderous tremors into a large vicinity around it. Chen Chen felt strong gushes of wind mixed with scorching air rushing toward him!

From where Chen Chen stood, he could see the colossal Falcon rocket starting to take off. A tremendous pillar of flames that could engulf the world in flames slowly propelled it into the air as if the entire motion was caught in slow motion on a camera.

As the rocket gained velocity, the rumbling in the air slowly dissipated. The brightness of the flame, however, only became magnified as it illuminated the night sky and the clouds!

The rocket mounted with Gantz satellites had finally taken flight!

“Next up, putting the five satellites into orbit...”

A sense of longing appeared in Chen Chen’s eyes as he looked at the departing rocket. Launching the rocket was the easy part. The difficult part was getting it into orbit. Since it was a geosynchronous orbit of 36,000 kilometers, it would take nearly ten days to get into orbit.

There was no good reason to stay here any longer. Chen Chen breathed out, feeling relieved, before returning to his vehicle.

It was no longer Cheng Cao driving him but a Black Knight instead.

“Let’s head back.”

Chen Chen ordered with a gentle wave.

The engine started and the driver drove back where they came from...

...

Ten days later, Chen Chen finally received the good news that all five Gantz satellites had entered orbit. They were now implanted into the orbit as five permanent fixtures that would circle Earth for billions of years to come.

With the satellites in place, the full potential of the Gantz technology could now be unleashed. Chen Chen’s plans of globalization had finally been achieved.

At this moment, Chen Chen was inside the teleportation room located on the top floor of the Spire laboratory. Inside the room with him was a squad consisting of five fully-fitted Black Knights.

“Sir Godfather, we’ve installed a Gantz sphere and a mini Gantz server machine in Northern America, Europe, and the Central Continent. They are connected with an appropriate powering system as well. We can begin the teleportation trial any time now!”

Little X reported as she stood next to Chen Chen.

Chen Chen sized up three muscular Black Knights in the squad and nodded briefly.

Despite the sophisticated technology behind Gantz that was intricately connected to scientific sectors such as memetic science that humans had yet to understand, its underlying principle was simple to understand. As long as two Gantz spheres were under the signal coverage of a Gantz server, a point-to-point transfer could be conducted.

It was similar to how calls could be connected between two mobile phones as long as there was a central station signal.

With a Gantz satellite acting as a relay station, these forms of transmissions could be conducted even with only a single Gantz sphere.

This was shown in the plot of Gantz as well. Although there was no Gantz sphere in nearby proximity, the Gantz satellite could still collect the deceased participants and pull them into the Gantz sphere room to be resurrected. They could then subsequently be transported back to the streets of the city after accepting their respective missions.

This meant that the Gantz satellite was essentially an enhanced version of the Gantz sphere.

Even till now, Chen Chen had little understanding of how Gantz technology operated. He merely replicated what he saw from the movie to create the satellites.

Just like the Gantz server machines, the Gantz satellites were simple and elaborate structures that could be easily replicated as well. The only flaw was the vast amount of germanium metal required for manufacturing them.

Chen Chen already purchased four germanium mines all over the world and had Tara Strategic Resources operating intensively to extract germanium metal from them. The extracted resources barely sufficed to help create Little X’s body along with five supplementary Gantz satellites. Meanwhile, the other Gantz server machines installed in the other continents were the most miniaturized model possible.

Gantz spheres were in extremely short supply. So far, Chen Chen had only instantiated five Gantz spheres in total. Two of them were located in Eco Science City while the remaining three were distributed in three different continents. There was still some way to go before accessible global teleportation could be achieved.

With that said, this was a remarkable starting point.

Apart from a limitation imposed by the need for germanium metal, another problem to consider for the Gantz sphere and server machines was the power required.

A hefty sum of power was required to conduct teleportation. While Eco Science City had the facilities required to accommodate this demand, it was not the same for the Black spheres located in other continents. Chen Chen decided to go with his previous approach which was to purchase a local factory as a disguise and employ his Black Knights as factory workers. This was done to serve as a facade to outsiders.

It was a lot of hassle, but it was well worth it considering the advantages that came with having Gantz facilities.

With all this in mind, Chen Chen looked at the codename printed across the chest of the three Black Knights and suddenly ordered, “T00922 Black Knight, head to Northern America Continent. T00383 Black Knight, head to Europe Continent. T00568 Black Knight, head to Central Continent. Dispatch immediately.”

The three Black Knights went without a word. A flash of dazzling blue ray emitted by the black sphere cast on their bodies.

The next second, their bodies started to melt away into the surrounding from top to bottom.

The teleportation speed of the Gantz sphere was drastically faster in real life compared to inside the movies. In the movies, the process took approximately ten seconds, up to thirty seconds in certain cases. In actual practice in real life, the process only took upwards of several seconds.

When the blue ray faded away, the three Black Knights had also disappeared from the teleportation room along with their equipment, nowhere to be seen after that.

“Arrived at point of destination.”

Little X suddenly announced as the three Black Knights disappeared. At the same time, three semi-transparent holographic displays appeared in front of Chen Chen. They were the live footage from different surveillance cameras.

Each surveillance camera footage showed a room with a black sphere installed. Each room was empty apart from the black sphere that was positioned at the center of the room.

In an instant, a beam of blue light was projected from each one of these black spheres and the figure of the Black Knights immediately appeared after that.

Little X paused for a few seconds before announcing, “After initial scanning, there are no physiological abnormalities detected in the three Black Knights transmitted via the satellite. They have arrived in business districts in Los Angeles, Zurich, and Shangdu respectively.”

“Excellent work.”

Chen Chen was very satisfied with the result and nodded. “With this, the B.S.S. and Black Knights force can finally become globalized task forces.”

“That’s right, the quick reaction forces will be able to protect our company’s assets more effectively.”

Little X quickly added. “Although we don’t have many global assets at the moment.”

“We will very soon.”

Chen Chen noted immediately. “I’m already planning to establish branches and research facilities in the first-tier city of each continent. They will specialize in procuring cancer-targeted drugs and clinical trials. This will be the first phase.

“After the branch offices are officially established, we’ll start setting up local outlets to promote our company’s electronic products. This will serve as the foundation for our upcoming Four-phase Era project. This way, we don’t have to deal with hefty tariffs imposed with each transaction conducted in the future.

“Finally, I plan to integrate Blacklight Biotechnology into the everyday lives of people. This will span from pharmaceuticals to electronics, which will then further extend into finance and regular commerce until it reaches into all aspects of life. Blacklight Biotechnology will only establish its position as an influential corporation after all this is achieved.”

Chen Chen suddenly became silent upon mentioning this. Then, he proceeded to ask, “Right, did Cheng Cao find anything on his side?”

“None. So far, we haven’t discovered any clues concerning Wang Xi. Also, Cheng Cao is still trying to figure out how he can relocate the server machine.”

Little X answered.

Chen Chen frowned upon hearing this and began examining the issue.

It would not be fair to blame Cheng Cao for all this since anything that had to do with the USB drive tended to make for an extremely pesky issue. It was also interconnected with Wang Xi, Zhao Shanhe, even himself, as well as the entirety of the Earth Federation. Naturally, the antimeme was tied to all this one way or another as well.

All things considered, it was not surprising to know that Cheng Cao had not made much progress after all this time. Chen Chen would have reason to be alerted if he were to manage to find clues in such a short time.

With this in mind, Chen Chen suddenly informed, “X, send me to the Central Continent.”

“If you return without a visa, it may cause some problems...”

Little X was somewhat troubled by this.

“Don’t worry about it, I know what I’m doing.”

Chen Chen insisted. “Besides, isn’t this what I set up the Gantz servers for?”

“Yes!”

Since Chen Chen insisted, Little X responded agreeably. Immediately after that, the Gantz sphere projected a beam of blue ray on Chen Chen’s body and his figure immediately vanished.

Chen Chen only felt a glimpse of blue light flashing across his eyes. Within a second, he found himself relocated to another speckless, empty room.

There were no windows in the room and the only source of light was a lamp overhead. Apart from that, the only thing that caught his attention was the Gantz sphere located in the middle.

The entire process only lasted a brief instant, but Chen Chen knew that he was already transported from Eco Science City and returned to Shangdu, located tens of thousands of kilometers away.

He turned around and pushed open the door behind him and found himself in the middle of a factory.

The walls and the door in the Gantz sphere room were made of the same material. As soon as Chen Chen stepped out of the room, the door shut automatically and immediately fused itself into the walls again. If not for Chen Chen stepping out of the room seconds ago, he would have never realized that there was a hidden door.

Another thing to note was that upon stepping out of the room, Chen Chen felt a vague sense of familiarity. He looked at the wall of the factory and as expected, he saw a sign with a familiar label printed on top —

“Greenland Oxygen Plant.”

“How thoughtful, X.”

Chen Chen felt nostalgic when he read the sign. Greenland Oxygen Plant was the first factory he had where he charged the USB drive. He had never expected Little X to buy this factory specifically and refitted it into a secret Gantz sphere teleportation lot.

The factory was already purchased under an anonymous name. Several workers who were disguised as Black Knights were walking around the factory working on their tasks. Only someone who came in here to inspect would notice that this factory was little more than a shell purposed for something else.

With Chen Chen’s arrival, a designated vehicle had been arranged and parked outside the factory already.

Chen Chen exited the factory and entered the vehicle. The driver then drove them in the direction of Chen Chen’s school...

After all these years, Chen Chen was finally returning to his school again...

chapters.title: Chapter 435: Corroding Corridor
chapters.body:
Chapter 435: Corroding Corridor

Chapter 435: Corroding Corridor

Two hours later, a black Benz stopped and parked by the side of a road next to the school entrance.

Chen Chen exited the Benz by himself. He stood in the middle of the crowd coming back and forth and looked at the all-too-familiar while also foreign school.

It was January in Shangdu, which meant that the region was entering the winter season. It was evening when he arrived. Thick dark clouds loomed overhead, casting a unique faint yellow winter glow on the cityscape.

The yellowish glow was not the radiance of the sun but merely a reflection of the city’s light on the clouds instead. This was very common in the city during the winter season.

Under this warm glow, Chen Chen saw countless people coming back and forth from the main gate of the school. Young and vibrant teenagers gathered in groups of various sizes, each socializing merrily and unwinding under the relaxing atmosphere. The semester was finally over and they would be welcoming the joyous occasion that was the winter break.

Standing in the middle of the streaming crowd, Chen Chen could not help but recall the events from that one year. Although it had only been four years, he had a feeling like it was from another life he once lived.

If not for the USB drive, he would probably have graduated by now and worked a common nine-to-five job, living every day monotonously until he died.

Perhaps he would have taken the postgraduate entrance examination after graduation and become a first-level graduate student, where he would continue to pursue a hypothetical, grand future.

One way or another, he would only become just another common individual among millions and billions like him, instead of separating himself from that crowd entirely as he had done now.

The reality was that there was no such thing as a what-if.

With this in mind, Chen Chen cracked a faint smile. In an instant, it was as if the iceberg surrounding him had melted away and substituted with a gentle, warm ambiance around him. Chen Chen had properly blended himself into this group of students.

With that, Chen Chen took his first step toward the school entrance.

Nobody was stopping him. Chen Chen stepped right into the school compound, without the security posted in front even noticing that this person was not a student, but a complete outsider instead.

“Phew...”

When Chen Chen exhaled, a puff of white mist rose in front of him. He followed along the familiar road and came to the front of a building. He stepped right in and went to the fourth floor of the building.

Before long, Chen Chen had arrived at a dormitory with the sign 404 posted in front.

He brushed the surface of the door with his finger, feeling the familiar texture of the door that was the same as he remembered it. He could hear some rowdy noises coming from behind the door.

“Zhang Wei, you failed again. The only subject out of the eight compulsory subjects that you managed to pass is sports, dope!”

“So what if I failed, you think that word exists in my vocabulary? Wait till I get back and get my dad to donate an entire building to the school. Then you’ll see me graduating way ahead of you guys!”

“Another one of your stories, Zhang Wei?”

“You guys don’t believe me? Tell them, Zhang Jian, my dad is the richest man in Dachangzhen. Where I come from, they call me the King of Dachang, how much do you think one building is worth to me?”

There was a round of bantering coming from behind the door, but it was no longer the trio of Wang Wei, Li Bo, and Zhou Jie. They had all graduated now.

Chen Chen shook his head. After he had enough of this nostalgic tour, he turned around and left without so much as a final glance.

“X, where’s the school’s server room?”

After leaving the dormitory building, Chen Chen got in touch with X using the Bluetooth earpiece. “I’d like to head over myself.”

“Wasn’t the server machine you’re planning to look at already carried away by Cheng Cao?”

Little X asked.

“But we didn’t find anything inside the server machines, did we?”

Chen Chen pointed out. “If the server machines don’t contain any clues, I figured I’d make a trip there myself and see if there’s anything peculiar that even the effects of antimeme couldn’t erase.”

Although the server machine had not yet been shipped to Eco Science City, Cheng Cao had already paired it with Little X for several rounds of inspections. No matter how many tests Little X ran, the findings were still evident. It was only an ordinary, even ancient server machine that contained nothing worthwhile or odd.

“Understood, the school’s server room is located on the third floor of the basement of block one on the main office lot.”

Little X provided the directions. “But you cannot enter without the keys and proper authorization.”

“Why would I need something like a key or authorization?”

Chen Chen flicked an eyebrow at this mention and asked pointedly. He left it at that and proceeded to head to the main campus.

“Sir Godfather, do you need Cheng Cao to meet you there?”

Little X was still worried.

“No need for that. I get that I’m not on the field much these days, but with my current strength, even the Black Knights won’t be of much help if I stumble into a situation that I can’t handle.”

Chen Chen replied, “Besides, Cheng Cao had already gone there to investigate and couldn’t find anything. I’m just heading there myself to try my luck, see if anything happens.”

With that, Little X decided there was nothing else for her to add.

It did not take long before Chen Chen arrived at the main campus. He looked at the seven-story building. Although it was not a large building, it served as the primary administrative hub of the campus. The office of the principal and the administration office was set up here. Right by the entrance of the building, there was a security guard posted and tasked with the registration procedures of the people entering and exiting the building.

Without any hesitation, Chen Chen went to the registration desk where he wrote down “Chen Chen, Class 1 of the Department of Biological Sciences”, included with what was once his student ID.

The security guard hardly gave it so much as a glance and let him in.

Chen Chen stepped into the elevator in the lobby and selected the button that read “-3”. Just as the elevator doors were about to close, he heard racing footsteps quickly approaching the elevator, accompanied by a frenzied shout, “Ah, hold on!”

Chen Chen instinctively reached for the button to open the elevator doors again. Before he could even blink, a nice-looking girl with a bed of long hair dashed into the elevator.

“Ah. Phew, haaa, thank, thank you!”

The girl panted desperately while thanking Chen Chen. She then reached for the control panel on the elevator to select her intended floor before she noticed that the floor “-3” had already been selected. She immediately pulled her slender hands back and turned to Chen Chen. “Are you from the student council as well? How come I’ve never met you before?”

“I’m not.”

Chen Chen shook his head and mentioned with a faint smile. He may appear polite and kind on the outside but underneath his smile, there was a subtle intent of putting up a wall around himself.

“You’re not?”

The girl repeated the statement to herself before catching the hint that Chen Chen was not interested in furthering the conversation. She figured it was better to suppress her curiosity and only snuck several glances at this person.

Before long, the elevator had arrived at the third basement floor. The door opened to reveal a dark and long-winded corridor.

Perhaps due to an extensive period without exposure to sunlight, there were faint traces of mold tracing along the white walls on both sides of the corridor.

There was also a damp, moldy smell in the air.

Chen Chen waited for the other person to exit the elevator before stepping out himself as well. He looked at two closed doors along both sides of the corridor, trying to pinpoint the location of the server room.

It seemed as if it was this girl’s first time coming to this place as well. Just like Chen Chen, she was looking for a sign that indicated what the rooms were.

“Zhang Qiao, over here!”

It was then when a door in front of them suddenly opened and a tall man poked his head out from inside while shouting in their direction, “What took you so long, Zhang Qiao. You’re the last person arriving!”

“Oh... I’m coming!”

The girl answered briskly. She turned around to look at Chen Chen one last time before sprinting inside the room.

The man inside the room sized up Chen Chen cautiously. When he saw that Chen Chen did not show the slightest inclination of speeding up his pace to join them, he proceeded to shut the door.

“Who’s that guy?”

“I have no idea, he says he’s not from the student council.”

“Why is he here if he’s not from the student council?”

The man asked a steady stream of questions as the girl entered the room.

Chen Chen paid no mind to them and passed by the room nonchalantly. As he passed by the room, he faintly noted along the crack of the door that the room was a classroom with arranged desks, chairs, and a blackboard. Three Chinese characters that read “Student Council Publicity Department” were written on a plate beside the door.

Why would the publicity department of the Jiaotong University’s student council be relocated here?

Chen Chen found the subject curious, but did not give it any further thought and only passed by the room.

After advancing another fifty meters, far enough that the elevator was almost out of view, Chen Chen finally located the server room.

It was positioned at the end of the corridor and unlike the other rooms that were sectioned off with wooden doors, this room had a security door instead. There was a sign on the door that read: “Server room. Unauthorized personnel not allowed.”

Chen Chen did not heed the suggestion written on the signboard. He proceeded to put his right hand on the lock. In an instant, his Field poured into the lock and gelled into the shape of a key. He proceeded to print the image of a key in his mind based on the internal structure of the lock.

After a second, when he applied a gentle push, the tightly reinforced security door opened with a gentle “clack”. He felt a cool breeze of air coming from inside the server room.

It was the air from the air-conditioner inside the server room.

Inside the server room, server machines covered in dust were lined along storage racks. LED lights blinked continuously inside the shrouded darkness. He could also hear the routine buzz and humming of the machines.

Chen Chen grazed along the surface of the wall with his hands and found the light switch. He turned on the lights.

“Click!”

The room was illuminated. The entire layout of the server room immediately presented itself before Chen Chen.

There were six server machines in total, each one of them had their identifying label written on them with a red marker. Machine number six was positioned closest to the door. The numbering of the machines moved in descending order toward the innermost section of the room, where there was an unlabeled, brand new server machine.

It seemed that after losing server machine one, the school department decided to buy a new server machine to replace what was once server machine one.

Chen Chen moved to the center of the machine room and shut his eyes. He invoked his Field and summoned countless invisible, tiny hands, which he used to graze through every surface of the server machines. He slipped into every single crevice and fully inspected the insides of the server machines.

Several minutes later, Chen Chen slowly opened his eyes again.

Sadly, he was unable to find anything worth noting in the entire server room.

Chen Chen started to question his judgment at this moment. Was it merely a coincidence when server machine one somehow detected information concerning Professor Wang Xi?

With this in mind, Chen Chen returned to where server machine one used to be. He moved the new server machine and looked at the print on the ground.

It was a print left behind by having a heavy object placed over a spot for many years. Such prints could not be erased unless power washing tools were used. Such washing methods were not applicable in a server room such as this.

An idea suddenly occurred to Chen Chen. He immediately snapped his attention to the print and began to evoke the Field in his mind. He formed a mass of Field energy like a hammer and slammed it against the tile!

“Bang!”

A fist-sized crack appeared on the ground in an instant!

Chen Chen flicked an eyebrow in amusement when he saw this. His Field had deducted another separate space underneath the ground which he stood!

It only took a second for his Field to reach through the gap to inspect what was underneath. What he would come to discover was a corpse left in perfect condition!

“Professor Wang Xi!”

Chen Chen exclaimed. He discovered that the person buried underground was not just any other person, but Professor Wang Xi who had completely vanished from this world without a trace!

“How is this possible...”

Chen Chen’s eyes were widened in horror. The next thing that came to his mind was to reach through the crack to further investigate. Just as the idea occurred to him, he heard a steady sound of gurgling water coming through the crack leading to the underground space...

This was completely out of Chen Chen’s expectation. He saw traces of dark red liquid seeping out of the crack. The source of this strange liquid seemed to be Professor Wang Xi’s very own body. It swarmed the underground space in the blink of an eye and quickly overflowed into the server room!

A bizarre, sweet smell with a trace scent of iron filled the air...

Was it fresh blood?

Chen Chen immediately retracted his Field and stumbled backward. He realized that although this strange substance resembled human blood, there was no chance that the human body contained such a drastic volume of blood, nor would it creep across the ground like some sort of sentient monster in this manner...

Just as Chen Chen suspected, as he retreated, the sections of floor which the blood came into contact with began to corrode away, making a rustling sound as they melted. It was as if the world itself was starting to wither away as spots of rust began to appear on the surrounding walls!

In an instant, the texture of the floor had been transformed into a rusty, aged texture. The same effect soon spread to the wall, turning the originally white and speckless walls into a dirty, dark brown texture that appeared as if something filthy were pasted on them!

The server machines that came into contact with the corrosion turned into rusted blocks of scrap iron. What caught Chen Chen’s attention was that instead of the lights on the server machines fading out, they started to flicker rapidly instead. The LED lights that were once red and green had shifted into a strange cluster of dark red and dark green.

Chen Chen did not know what was happening, but one thing he knew for certain was that it was time for him to get out of here.

Without sparing a moment’s thought, Chen Chen backed away and tried to exit the room.

The moment Chen Chen stepped out of the server room, he immediately screeched to a stop again... The corridor he came from that seemed to stretch on endlessly was corroding at a rapid pace as well. He saw clumps of mold forming on the surface of the walls that congealed into strange, greasy substances!

While this was happening, Chen Chen also heard the steady growl of metal beating against each other coming from the previous room. It sounded as if some ancient machines were activated. The screeching sounds of their gear grinding against each other echoed in his ears...

Chen Chen’s expression had become unbearably dark upon noting this strange occurrence. He made his Field swarm around him like raging tides to form an invisible wall around himself to act as a protective barrier.

At the same time, Chen Chen heard panicked screams coming from a distance. He saw the classroom door further in front being thrown open as dozens of students charged out of the room, their faces were twisted in horror and mania.

The students barely had time to give Chen Chen so much as a glance as they charged toward the end of the corridor with the emergency exit sign.

chapters.title: Chapter 436: Phasmophobia
chapters.body:
Chapter 436: Phasmophobia

Chapter 436: Phasmophobia

“Where I come from, they call me the King of Dachang. How much do you think one building is worth to me?”

In Dorm 404, a lanky young man was bragging to his roommates. When he saw them roll their eyes at his proclamations, he suddenly sneered. “Don’t believe me? I’ll prove it to you.”

As he spoke, he closed the game Phasmophobia, picked up the backpack hung by the bed, and unzipped it. Suddenly, knickknacks tumbled out of the backpack. “See? I’ve spent a fortune buying all these at Vatican City!”

The lanky young man took out a palm-sized crucifix from the pile and flicked it, proudly saying, “A crucifix, made of pure silver, especially used to prevent ghosts from entering their hunting phase!”

Then, he took out a pack of incense and shook it in front of everyone. “Smudge sticks personally blessed by the Pope. It can also prevent the ghosts from hunting you.”

After that, he took out a camera with a mysterious expression. “You guys who only toy with your equipment will never understand the true realm of photography...”

Everyone did not bother paying attention to the young man in front of them and only a handsome young man next to him said, “Actually... I suspect you may have been cheated.”

“Surely not?”

The lanky young man looked disbelieving. He was just about to continue showing off the “holy items” in his backpack when he suddenly turned his head and stared at the computer in front of him.

“What’s wrong?”

The handsome young man on the side asked instinctively.

“Something’s wrong!”

The lanky youth raised his palms and made a gesture for silence. “I was arguing with you guys on purpose just now because I noticed from the camera at the door that a sneaky guy was listening in front of our door for a long time. He didn’t leave until just now!”

“Are you for real?”

The other three people in the dormitory huddled together. Sure enough, they saw an extra figure on the computer screen of the lanky young man. The figure put his hand on Dorm 404’s door and only turned around and left after some time.

“He was eavesdropping on our conversation!”

The lanky young man looked pensive and solemnly said, “Didn’t you notice? When he walks, he always touches the ground with his heel first. This shows that he has received military training because only the right marching technique of military training will produce this effect.

“At the same time, his left hand was down at all times. This is most likely because there’s a gun hidden in his sleeve. When he wants to kill, in just a flick of his wrist, the gun will fall from his sleeve to the palm of his hand...

“Moreover...”

At this point, everyone could not help but roll their eyes and disperse.

“Hey, there’s more. Why aren’t you listening?”

Seeing that everyone ignored him, the lanky young man turned and glanced downstairs. Sure enough, he saw that the stranger had walked out of the dormitory building and was walking in the direction of the teaching building.

Seeing this, the lanky young man simply picked up his backpack and left the dormitory, silently following the stranger.

The stranger’s walking speed was not fast and the lanky young man followed far behind. They finally came to the front of Office Building No. 1.

The lanky young man waited for a while after the other person walked through the door and only went in when he heard the sound of the elevator from inside the building. He noticed the elevator descending continuously and finally stopping at the third floor underground.

Upon seeing this, the lanky young man frowned and realized that there was more to this affair.

“Why did he go to the basement? Could he be hiding a secret?”

The lanky young man hesitated and after about five minutes passed, he walked slowly down the stairs...

He had to admit that Office Building No. 1 had been around for a long time. As the lanky young man descended bit by bit, gradually the air began to smell moldy.

Not only that, even the voice-activated lights at the bottom of the stairs were out. When he reached the second basement floor, the entire corridor was already pitch black. Seeing this, the lanky young man immediately opened his backpack and took out a powerful flashlight.

With a click, the flashlight emitted a strong beam, illuminating the path leading to the third floor underground.

“Gasp, something’s wrong...”

However, the lanky young man still felt uneasy. He only felt an inexplicable sensation washing over him. He could not tell what it was, but it made his heart pound.

Therefore, he took out a yellow thermometer from his backpack again, pointed it toward the front, and clicked.

“Beep!”

With only one beep, the LCD screen of the temperature-measuring gun displayed the numbers — 6.3 degrees Celsius.

“Phew, fortunately, it’s not bitingly cold...”

Seeing this, the lanky young man breathed a sigh of relief and then continued to walk downward.

When he reached the third floor underground, the lanky young man finally saw a light, but just as he was about to heave a sigh of relief, there was a rustling sound in the corridor as if thousands of insects were climbing over the walls. It sent a chill down his spine!

The lanky young man moved the flashlight toward the wall, only to see that the wall paint was rapidly aging and decaying. It was peeling off completely in the blink of an eye and assuming a rusty, dirty appearance.

At the same time, there was the sound of screaming and running from the depths of the corridor. It seemed that a dozen people were running toward this place together.

“F*ck!”

The lanky young man could not help inhaling sharply, but he immediately calmed down in the next second. He quickly opened the backpack, took out the crucifix, and held it in front of him, then quickly ran upstairs!

“Heh, fortunately, I’ve been playing Phasmophobia for two whole years. I’d better leave this place first!”

The lanky young man said as he ran upstairs, but when he sensed that he had reached the first floor, he was completely dumbfounded because the scenery on the first floor was still the same as the third floor underground.

Currently, the lanky youth finally saw a dozen men and women run out from the depths of the corridor, shrieking. They ran past him and headed up the stairs.

However, before the last few of them ran up the stairs, they saw the first few people who had run ahead were coming up from the stairs below next to them as if space itself had been twisted into a strange loop.

This scene that could only happen in a horror film suddenly stupefied everyone.

“The spirits are trapping us!”

Someone yelled and the group hurriedly found an alternative route and sped toward the elevator door.

Fortunately, with just one press of the button, the elevator door opened immediately, revealing the bright and clean space inside.

This group of people rushed up, scrambling to squeeze into the elevator.

However, after all, the elevator had a weight limit. As a result of more than ten people squeezing in together, no one could leave.

“Move in!”

“Squeeze in a bit more and let me in!”

“Stop pushing, we’re too heavy. You guys wait for the next elevator...”

“Get out!”

The small elevator became a battlefield for more than a dozen people, but at this moment, there was a nauseating sound of splitting metal from the top of the elevator. At the same time, the entire elevator began to sway violently as if some powerful force in the dark was tugging from the top!

“Something’s wrong, run!”

Everyone was taken aback. Someone yelled and everyone wanted to run out of the elevator.

Even so, by the time those few people nearest the door had run out desperately and while the remaining seven or eight people were still hesitating, the entire elevator rang out with a boom and dropped down quickly!

“Agghhh —!”

There was a harsh scream that drew further away. Sparks burst out from the elevator as it fell into a bottomless abyss...

Suddenly, there was only an empty black hole where the elevator had been.

“How, how could it be...”

The rest of the people watched all this in stunned dismay. One pretty girl covered her mouth and said in horror, “This is the bottom floor of the building. How could there be such a deep space?”

“The spirits are toying with this place, we’ve run into some spirits!”

Someone yelled as if that was the only way to vent their fears.

“Don’t panic, everyone, we’ll find a way out!”

At this moment, a tall sturdy man came out. He placated the rest of the crowd. “What we have to do now is not to panic, but to find out why this happened. We must believe in science. There must be a reason this situation has happened!”

It seemed that the man in front of them was a respected figure. At once, everyone looked over.

“Heh, how inexperienced, thinking that science can explain everything in this world.”

At this moment, a cold laugh came from the dark, filled with arrogance and worldliness. “Only through experience will you understand — there are some things that science can never explain...”

“Who’s there!”

The man turned his head abruptly, with a shocked look. Besides his own student council, were there others on this floor?

After this question, a young man carrying a black satchel, holding a crucifix in one hand, and a temperature-measuring gun in the other emerged from the darkness. A glint of arrogance appeared in his eyes and his head was held high, almost looking down his nose at everyone.

“Who are you?”

Seeing the youth in front of him, a look of uncertainty flashed in the man’s eyes, and he immediately asked.

“Isn’t it more polite to proactively report your identity before asking for someone else’s name?”

The lanky young man said solemnly.

“I’m Wang Liang, head of the Publicity Department of the student council!”

The man said immediately.

“Very good.”

The lanky young man nodded in satisfaction. “My name is Zhang Wei and I’m also a student of this school. Maybe you have never heard of my name, but I’m also a member of a ghost-hunting team. I have been in this role for two years.”

“Ghost-hunting team?”

A man among the crowd asked reflexively, “Are there ghosts in the world?”

“Heh, do you think I’m joking?”

Zhang Wei said in a deep voice. He did not explain himself, just stared at the other person until they lowered their gaze, not daring to meet his eyes. Only then did he turn back to the crowd. “Congratulations everyone, you’re now involved in a supernatural event!”

“So what should we do?”

As if clinging to a life raft, the group immediately surrounded him and asked at the same time.

“It’s very simple. If you want to exorcize ghosts and spirits, you must first determine their identity.”

Zhang Wei squatted, rummaged in his backpack, and said, “Ghosts are divided into twelve types namely — Spirits, Wraiths, Phantoms, Poltergeists, Banshees, etc. Only by knowing what type of ghost it is, will we know how to get rid of it. I just so happen to have a few tools to help us determine the type of ghost in this haunted house.”

With that, Zhang Wei took out a few candles and handed them to everyone. “Candles can slow down the decrease of our sanity level. The lower your sanity level, the easier it is to be hunted by ghosts.”

Even as he spoke, Zhang Wei suddenly slapped his head. “Oh, I didn’t bring the pill that will increase one’s sanity level, otherwise I’d be more assured in all this.”

Then, he took out a notebook. “You can put the notebook on the floor. If the ghost writes on it, it means that they’re a Spirit, Revenant, Shade, Yurei, Oni, and so on.”

Finally, he took out a few light sticks. “You can also use this either for lighting or to view the wall. If it’s a Revenant, Spirit or Poltergeist, it will leave fingerprints on the wall. So it can also be used to determine the type of ghost.”
