This was not the important thing. The important thing was there was only one person in the private room.

This was a young man who was even younger than Zhou Yi himself. At first glance, he looked very ordinary, but if one looked closely, one would find that he had a pair of unique eyes.

It was a pair of jet-black eyes, which seemed to be piercing, infinite, and deep, like a bottomless abyss. It was as if a single glance from him would be enough to sink anyone.

This was a pair of eyes filled with mystical power...

Looking at the appearance of this young man, Zhou Yi only felt that the other party seemed familiar. He subconsciously searched his memory, but then he suddenly jumped in shock!

This young man was actually that legend?

“You’re Mr., Mr. Chen!”

Zhou Yi blurted out instinctively.

“That’s me.”

The young man raised his head in surprise, took a look at Zhou Yi, then nodded and replied with a smile, “My looks are rather ordinary. Those who aren’t my acquaintances can seldom recognize me at a glance.”

“You really are Mr. Chen?”

Zhou Yi took a deep breath and felt a tremor throughout his body. He thought it would only be other high-level leaders of Blacklight Biotechnology who were having drinks with Qian Wenhuan, but he never expected that the other party turned out to be Chen Chen, the founder of Blacklight Biotechnology!

Upon hearing Zhou Yi’s babble, Chen Chen suddenly laughed. He pointed to the opposite seats and said to the two of them, “What are you doing standing around? Have a seat.”

“Alright.”

Qian Wenhuan did not stand on ceremony and he dragged Zhou Yi to the seats.

At this point, Zhou Yi realized that his second question made him sound like a total noob and he was irritated with himself. He did not expect that the first time he saw the founder of Blacklight Biotechnology, he would make such a mistake.

Zhou Yi wished there was some hole he could crawl into.

“This is Mr. Zhou Yi, right? He’s a great talent and he looks much better than you, Old Qian.”

Nonetheless, Chen Chen did not seem to mind. After the two of them sat down, he suddenly made a teasing comment.

“That’s for sure.”

Hearing Chen Chen’s remark, Qian Wenhuan did not get angry. Instead, he smiled drily. “When I was at Preston Biotech, I was good at negotiation and sales. It’s just thanks to this face of mine that my performance was greatly diminished. Otherwise, I wouldn’t be just a mere sales manager.”

“Mr. Chen, you’re being too kind.”

Zhou Yi also said quickly and at the same time, he raised his arm subconsciously. He was about to find a wine bottle and a wine glass to punish himself with three cups of wine[1].

However, he was quickly dumbfounded because besides the dishes, there were only three bottles of Sprite on the dinner table and nothing else.

“Hahahaha!”

Seeing Zhou Yi’s actions, Qian Wenhuan suddenly laughed. “Little Zhou, are you looking for a wine glass? There’s no need for that. Boss himself doesn’t drink and we’re outside China, so there’s no such drinking culture. Just relax and eat. ”

“So that’s it...”

Zhou Yi only took a scoop from a dish to hide his embarrassment.

“In the past half a month, you guys have done a great job.”

Chen Chen paid no mind to all this and just said casually, “I’ve asked for you today, Old Qian, just to learn about how things have been. And also to meet this high-achiever sent by headquarters. That’s all. Still, the purchase orders worth six billion might seem like a lot to us, but it’s only a drop in the bucket for the continents.”

Then, Chen Chen thought for a while. “I believe this is just the starter for now and the real lion’s share will come in the next three months.”

“Indeed.”

Qian Wenhuan also looked thoughtful. “Now all the continents have ordered the Mark drone series. After they’ve ascertained the superior performance of our XTN robot series, orders will spring up like mushrooms after a rain. Then it’ll be our harvest time.”

“I don’t value that very much. What I value is the policies that we can achieve after opening branches in various continents.”

Chen Chen nodded. “So, I hope you’ll work harder on the various weapons and technologies, and strive to obtain local policies which are more favorable for us. In that case, we can give up some of the profits.”

“Understood.”

“Understood!”

Qian Wenhuan and Zhou Yi quickly promised.

“Besides, there’s one more thing I need you to do.”

At this moment, Chen Chen suddenly turned serious. He lowered his voice and said, “I’m going to use Namibia as a test platform to implement XTN robots instead of police for law enforcement so that XTN robots will enter civilian use in advance. Old Qian, get ready.”

“So soon?”

Qian Wenhuan was surprised. “Is it too early? Would the people accept it?”

“Thus, I need you to conduct a survey.”

Chen Chen replied, “As for the results of the survey, it can also be manipulated by propaganda and public opinion, and if necessary, we can also use certain means to achieve the implementation.”

“For instance...”

Chen Chen thought for a while and suddenly wrote something on the table —

“Robocop”.

[1] A custom in Chinese drinking culture, where someone who made a faux pas must receive “punishment” by drinking three cups of wine.

chapters.title: Chapter 453: The Plan
chapters.body:
Chapter 453: The Plan

Chapter 453: The Plan

When Qian Wenhuan saw this word, he could not help but turn blank. He did not understand what it meant. On the contrary, Zhou Yi’s face flashed. “Mr. Chen, what you mean is that if the survey results show that police robots aren’t acceptable to the public, we can copy what they did in Robocop?”

“Yes, that’s what I meant.”

Chen Chen expressed approval. “I want to build Namibia into an experimental platform and make it a pioneer. As long as Namibia can become the country with the lowest crime rate in the world under the management of XTN robots, then, who would reject my XTN robots?”

“But...”

Zhou Yi suddenly hesitated to say something. “But would Namibia agree to this kind of reform?”

Chen Chen smiled, did not answer, but turned to look at Qian Wenhuan with a poker face. “It seems that Old Qian rarely watches Hollywood movies.”

“I have no time.”

Qian Wenhuan shook his head. “So what did they do in Robocop?”

“This involves the story plot.”

Zhou Yi immediately explained, “In short, there’s a company specializing in military robots in the movie. This company is exactly like us. Their products have been used in a large number of military services and the company has also made a lot of money.

“However, the top executives of this company aren’t satisfied. They want more benefits. So they think of the civilian sphere. As long as law enforcement robots replace traditional police in fighting crimes, the benefits they get will at least double.

“It’s just that when the company wanted to promote this plan, it was unexpectedly strongly opposed by the public. Most people believed that robots have no human feelings, don’t understand the value of life, and cannot make humans feel secure. They can’t trust these cold machines, so they wouldn’t hand over human lives to the judgment of robots.”

After thinking about it, Zhou Yi continued to speak, “However, despite the strong opposition from the public, the company still didn’t give up. So they thought of another way to gain the public’s approval — find some disabled police officers and provide them with free mechanized transformation. As long as the mechanized police officers can return to their posts and perform better than before, then after seeing this result, the public can naturally accept robot law enforcement calmly.”

“So, that’s it...”

Qian Wenhuan’s eyes lit up. “Indeed, this is a slow and steady plan. Free mechanized transformation can improve the company’s involvement in public welfare and the public has no reason to oppose it. After all, you can’t say that someone isn’t a human being just because they are installed with mechanical prostheses, right?

“After that, the transformed policeman will become a walking billboard. The more outstanding he is, the greater the impact on the people. But the only requirement is that all this must be attention-grabbing. If the whole thing receives no attention, it’s simply meaningless.

“Not only that, the disability can’t be a general physical disability, because even if we install prosthetic limbs for these mild disabilities, at most they can only return to their posts, but this will not have the slightest impact on their job performance. So we need severely disabled police officers at least. It’s best if it’s someone with a high level of paraplegia and the more serious the injury, the better. Only in this way will people count the police officers’ accomplishments as ours.”

In the end, Qian Wenhuan concluded. “In short, it’s best if the police officer who undergoes the transformation is known as a hero and he must be injured so badly that he needs to replace every body part except his head. He must be a popular guy too. Only in this way can he become an excellent billboard and function as commercial propaganda for our company!”

Hearing Qian Wenhuan’s summary, Zhou Yi on the side was secretly surprised. He did not expect that even though Qian Wenhuan had not watched the movie Robocop, he could instantly discern the key points of this plan. Sure enough, this man had a certain level of skill to make it in this position. Before this, Zhou Yi had thought that the other party was just an idiot who was good at flattery...

“Old Qian, your analysis is very good and you immediately grasped the point.”

At this time, Chen Chen could not help but clap his hands and praise, “If anyone just listened to what you said at that last part, they wouldn’t think that you haven’t seen this movie. Very good. What we have to do now is to follow the method shown in the movie. We’ll look for a few spokespersons and effectively turn them into advertisements so that the public can agree to robots replacing the police in law enforcement.”

“A police officer like this isn’t hard to find.”

Qian Wenhuan did not gloat just because of Chen Chen’s praise. He only said modestly, “It’s not difficult to find this kind of extremely injured police officer in the African Continent. The only difficulty is the technical aspect. With our current technology, can we create this kind of robot?”

“Don’t worry, we can even heal James Watson, let alone ordinary injuries.”

Chen Chen sounded nonchalant. “As for the modification of the human body, that’s the job of the research department.”

“Understood, I’ll give you a plan on Friday this week at the latest.”

Qian Wenhuan nodded immediately and agreed.

Zhou Yi on the side was totally stupefied.

He had never expected that, after sitting at the dinner table for just a few minutes, the two of them actually came up with a huge plan involving the country and even the entire Earth Federation. This efficiency was not comparable to any corporations in the market with their bloated systems.

Zhou Yi was deeply shocked but he could also understand that the reason everything was decided so quickly was that all the authority of the entire company was concentrated in the hands of the young man in front of him...

This was the power of centralization.

Next, Zhou Yi was stunned as he watched Chen Chen feasting heartily. He thought that the table full of food was just a luxurious habit of the rich. He did not expect that when they left at the end of dinner, all the dishes from that table had entered Chen Chen’s stomach and Qian Wenhuan seemed hardly surprised by this astonishing sight.

After they left, Qian Wenhuan beckoned to Zhou Yi again and asked the driver to take Zhou Yi to the gate of his community. After the dinner, Zhou Yi’s views on Qian Wenhuan changed a lot and the two became familiar with each other.

“Did you feel a little unaccustomed to all this?”

On the way, Qian Wenhuan suddenly asked.

“A little.”

Zhou Yi replied, “I used to drink alcohol on the job and because I was young, I got blackout drunk almost every time. This time, I saw Mr. Chen but didn’t drink a drop. It’s a rather new feeling.”

“It’s fine. Our company is still very young and in a period of rapid development. With your ability, you can get promoted in three to five years.”

Qian Wenhuan smiled mysteriously. “At that point, you’ll slowly adapt and at that time, you’ll also know many secrets. It’s possible that many things that you can’t understand before will seem a matter of course.”

Hearing these words, Zhou Yi’s pupils shrank slightly. Sure enough, there were many secrets in Blacklight Biotechnology.

Now he was a weapon sales manager, but he was still below the truly high-level staff. Even so, if some of the information he knew about the weapons was leaked out, it would shake up all of society. In that case, as for the secrets known only to the higher-ups, how earth-shattering would they be?

Zhou Yi was filled with anticipation...

...

Qian Wenhuan waved his hand and said goodbye to Zhou Yi when he had sent Zhou Yi back to the gate of the community.

Looking at the back of Zhou Yi gradually walking into the community, Qian Wenhuan’s expression changed from gentle warmth to an impassive look.

“CEO Qian, you seem to have high hopes for this sales manager?”

The secretary in the front suddenly turned his head and asked.

“Somewhat. Young people are still very capable.”

Qian Wenhuan nodded. “He’s just a bit arrogant still, but people who come from college are like this. They don’t think much of someone unorthodox like me.”

The secretary shook his head and said nothing.

Next, the first thing Qian Wenhuan did when he returned to his villa was to find the movie Robocop from the Internet and watch it from beginning to end.

Instead of watching it dumbly like ordinary people, Qian Wenhuan recorded every detail on paper and saved a screenshot at the critical moments.

After watching the movie for four hours, Qian Wenhuan checked the time. It was already one o’clock in the morning and he had written more than 5,000 words.

Nevertheless, Qian Wenhuan did not rest because this new version of Robocop was a movie produced in 2014. As early as 30 years ago, there were three older versions of Robocop.

Therefore, Qian Wenhuan gave the old versions of Robocop another quick watch.

Then, he started to write out the plan.

When he went to work the next day, Qian Wenhuan notified his team and gave the draft to everyone and then began to discuss and trim the content of the plan.

Qian Wenhuan and his team discussed late into the night for three days in a row, until the end of Friday, when they finalized the plan at last. After that, Qian Wenhuan directly sent the 30,000-word plan to Chen Chen’s mailbox.

At this time, Chen Chen, who was sitting in the office, immediately received the message.

“Old Qian has gotten it done so quickly?”

Chen Chen leaned on the chair and swiped his right hand lightly, and suddenly opened a holographic webpage out of thin air on the empty desk. At the same time, the mailbox automatically opened, revealing Qian Wenhuan’s plan.

“Sir Godfather, this is Qian Wenhuan’s plan.”

Little X’s voice came directly from the speaker. “According to Qian Wenhuan’s plan, first of all, the prerequisites for the selection of targets — this plan requires the selection of targets to be a bit strict. Number one, the identity of the other party must be a legal police officer in Namibia. This is a must. At the same time, there are a few other requirements:

“First, they must be severely injured, with a high level of paraplegia.

“Second, they must be upright and honest, with a strong sense of responsibility. They must also be highly obedient to orders, to prevent their being out of our control, which might result in what happened in the movie where the protagonist kills the company boss.”

Speaking of this, Little X said, “Qian Wenhuan is indeed right. Although we can use the God chip to control them, there might be unexpected developments, so it is best to choose the target wisely at the beginning.

“Then the third requirement: the target must get injured in a fight against criminals. Only in this way will they be more conducive to shaping their public image and triggering public opinion.

“Fourth, the target must have good facial features and an upright temperament. It’s best if they are suitable as a police officer because the nature of their appearance is very important to the public’s impression.

“The fifth and most important one is that the target’s family situation must be taken into consideration. If necessary, we can send their parents and families to the Eco Science City to prevent unexpected situations in the future.”

Hearing these five requirements, Chen Chen frowned. “Erm... It sounds rather like nurturing an idol for a talent show.”

“According to Qian Wenhuan’s plan, it’s best if the injuries of the target are heavy and obvious so that it will help us to promote his past deeds. If possible, we can use a slogan to promote the target as a ‘superhero’, turning him into a super idol who wouldn’t lose in popularity to international A-list superstars.”

Little X continued.

“Yes, we can test the waters while we’re at it. Maybe Blacklight Biotechnology can enter the entertainment circle in the future.”

Chen Chen snapped his fingers and said half-jokingly.

“These are the five requirements for the target and only those that meet the requirements can become the target we’ll train for the plan.”

When Little X heard Chen Chen’s words, she could not help but smile. “Next, it’s Qian Wenhuan’s official plan. He subdivided it into fifteen steps. It will take about two years to create for the target an image of a perfect superhero and make him the nemesis of criminals and a world-class hero.”

“Does it take two years?”

Chen Chen frowned. “It’s too long... What’s the estimated budget given in Old Qian’s plan?”

“Promotion costs are about one billion USD.”

Little X replied, “That is to say, according to the plan given by Qian Wenhuan, including some special performances, just one billion USD is enough to create a world-renowned international superstar.”

“Then make it three billion dollars.”

Chen Chen replied without hesitation, “I hope the timeline can be accelerated to less than one year.”

“I’m afraid that’d be difficult.”

Little X shook her head. “In addition to the method of throwing more money at this, word-of-mouth popularity is also very important. Qian Wenhuan’s plan contains all these. After my calculations, two years is indeed the limit.”

“Then try to compress it.”

Chen Chen spread out his hands and then asked, “But the plan is just a plan and we’ll deal with the implementation later. The first thing to do now is to select the target and conduct a survey before selecting the target.”

“Survey the public’s acceptance of robot law enforcement?”

Little X blinked and asked.

“Yes, although this answer is foreordained, the process is indispensable, not to mention I’m not the President of Namibia.”

Chen Chen exhaled, stood up, and said, “X, call Zurama for me, I should also talk to him about this.”

Zurama was the Minister of Resources who promised three exemptions and five reductions for Chen Chen when he first came to Namibia. Due to their business relationship, the two hit it off at once thanks to being mutually beneficial. Zurama had reaped enough benefits from Chen Chen’s side that would not be used up in several lifetimes. Chen Chen had also obtained a great deal of convenience from Zurama, so it could be described as a win-win situation.

Now the two had worked together for nearly four years and they had developed a deep “friendship.” Moreover, with Chen Chen’s help, Zurama had taken the throne as the spokesperson of the largest party in Namibia. He could expect to be the next president. Of course, he must also help pull the strings for the promotion of law enforcement robots now.

chapters.title: Chapter 454: One Out Of Three
chapters.body:
Chapter 454: One Out Of Three

Chapter 454: One Out of Three

The plan for remodeling people into robots proposed by Chen Chen was eventually named the Robocop Plan.

The next afternoon, Chen Chen walked out of Minister Zurama’s office, got into the car with a contented expression, and hurried away.

At this time, on the balcony on the third floor of the Ministry of Resources building, Zurama looked at Chen Chen’s departing figure with a complex expression, speechless for a while.

“The stage is set. All of Namibia is under his charge...”

Watching the car slowly leave, Zurama suddenly had an idea deep in his heart: if this businessman’s car suddenly exploded at this point, would it be more beneficial to the Namibian government?

Although Blacklight Biotechnology would be thrown into chaos and huge economic losses would ensue, Namibia could also take the opportunity to acquire a large amount of wealth and technology. This wealth was even enough to help Namibia surpass the Rainbow Nation and attain the status as the overlord of the African Continent...

However, in the next second, Zurama completely dismissed the idea.

He knew that once Chen Chen died, in addition to the above-mentioned situation, there was another outcome that was more likely. Namely, Mainland China and Northern America would immediately move to seize the assets left by Chen Chen. That pack of wolves would be waiting to pounce. Unless Namibia was willing to go against these behemoths, it could only reluctantly return these technologies to China.

Then, whoever became president would die miserably...

Moreover, if Chen Chen died, who would support his election? Who else could ensure he would sit on the throne of Namibia’s presidency?

Therefore, this thought merely flashed through Zurama’s mind and then disappeared again.

At this time, Zurama’s heart was a complicated mess. On one hand, he hoped that his country could be self-reliant and independent. On the other hand, he hoped that he could hold the highest power in Namibia. These two options were incompatible...

Thinking of this, Zurama could only close his eyes in suffering and took a deep breath. When he opened his eyes again, he took out his phone and tried checking his bank account that was opened in Switzerland.

Just now, under the name of this account, an extra 30 million USD had been deposited silently...

Despite seeing such a thrilling number, Zurama’s eyes did not flicker at all. The money had not much meaning for him. Perhaps a few years ago, this huge number would have made him ecstatic, but now, it could only get a glance from him.

Zurama instantly turned off the account, cleared all the usage records, and finally shut down the computer.

...

While Zurama was running around for the sake of the Person Remodeling Act proposed by Chen Chen, Qian Wenhuan had already started casting.

At this time, Qian Wenhuan sat at the conference table in boredom, watching his team continue to bring in a thick stack of documents from the police station. These documents had been piled up on the conference table until they were the height of half a person, but there was still new material moving in continuously.

“CEO Qian, these are the data of the police officers who are currently injured and disabled in Namibia, from 2000 until now. It’s all here.”

An assistant came over silently and notified in a soft voice, “There are three hundred and twenty copies in total.”

“Very good.”

Qian Wenhuan clapped his hands. Everyone raised their heads reflexively and looked over at once.

“Everyone, I hope we can find someone that fits the requirements of selection in the ‘Robocop Plan’, in accordance to what the plan necessitates.”

Qian Wenhuan reminded. “Because this is just the preliminary selection, the requirements can be more relaxed. After the number of qualified people is finally determined, we can proceed with the next round of screening based on the number of targets.”

“Yes, understood...”

There were scattered replies from everyone. After that, they picked up the documents and opened the envelopes one by one, beginning to read through them.

Qian Wenhuan did the same. He did not hand over this task completely to his subordinates but worked with everyone to screen and search for qualified personnel targets.

The era they were starting with was a bit distant, something they might as well give up on. Firstly, because it had been 25 years since 2000, the police officers who were injured and disabled at that time were at least 40 or 50 years old now. At that age, one’s adaptability and reflexes were greatly diminished. Coupled with years of disability, even if those officers could still move, they would have lost the ability to continue handling cases.

Therefore, Qian Wenhuan focused his search on police officers who were injured and disabled in recent years.

However, in that case, the screening became a little restrictive.

In the next hour, Qian Wenhuan went through more than a dozen files but was satisfied with none. Either they were too old or not sufficiently disabled, only missing a limb or two. There were rarely any cases that were paralyzed throughout the body.

However, on second thought, Qian Wenhuan had a realization again. After all, Namibia’s national strength was inherently weak and neither medical treatment nor other conditions were guaranteed. The people were even more impoverished. Whenever there were any major accidental casualties, those people essentially could not be saved.

Even if they were saved, they would starve to death due to poverty and the loss of their ability to provide for themselves.

Thinking of this, Qian Wenhuan frowned and continued searching.

Finally, an ID photo on a document made his eyes lit up.

It was a white man wearing the uniform of a first-level superintendent. His name was Brooke Humphrey, a resident native who immigrated here when the Germans colonized the country.

He had brown hair and black eyes, not the blond hair and blue eyes unique to Caucasians, but his features were as sharp as a Caucasian’s, with a high nose, deep eye sockets, piercing eyes like a falcon’s, and thin, fine lips.

Brooke was practically bestowed with a righteous and awe-inspiring face. Although he looked a little rough, his face was surprisingly pleasing to the eye.

Immediately, Qian Wenhuan continued to read on.

Brooke was only thirty-two years old this year. Two years ago, when he turned thirty, in a conflict during an arrest, he was unfortunately hit by a stray bullet in his spine.

At that time, the situation was not so bad as to cause total paralysis, but when he was sent to the hospital, the doctor made a major mistake during the operation, which eventually caused him to be completely paralyzed from the neck down.

“Not bad... Young, good-looking, sufficiently injured, and the injury is in line with the publicity plan, now only the two requirements of family and character are left.”

Seeing this, Qian Wenhuan was over the moon. He took out a marker, made a mark on this document to signify selection, and then put the documents back in the file again.

Qian Wenhuan’s only concern was that two years had passed and he did not know whether this person was still alive.

Next, Qian Wenhuan found another relatively suitable file, but unlike before, this was a black man.

This black police detective named Blake Morton was only twenty-five years old. He was ordinary in appearance, so in terms of that requirement, he was a little lacking. Nonetheless, he was of the majority race in this country — that was one thing that could not be ignored.

His experience was even more frightening because he was caught in an ambush by a drug dealer while tracking down a drug case on his own accord. His limbs were cut off and he was left with just his torso, but in the end, he survived tenaciously.

All this happened less than a year ago.

Seeing this, Qian Wenhuan hardly hesitated and directly included this file on the candidate list, with an even higher priority than Brooke from before.

Next, Qian Wenhuan sat quietly for more than half an hour, until he had read all the 40 or 50 documents in front of him. He still did not find another one that met the requirements. Either they were too old or the injury was not serious enough, which meant it would be hard to create the narrative they wanted.

While stretching, Qian Wenhuan raised his head and tossed the two files into the center of the conference table. At the same time, he said, “I’ve selected two. Get on with it and after the first screening, send all the selected targets to my office.”

With that said, he turned and left the meeting room.

Next, Qian Wenhuan returned to his office to check a few documents. It was not until the end of the morning that his secretary walked in with just seven or eight files.

“CEO Qian, all the materials we’ve selected this time are here, please have a look.”

Qian Wenhuan nodded and asked his secretary to put these materials on the desk, after which he took one of the files and opened it up.

This time the process was much faster. After only ten minutes, he had read all the seven documents selected by the team.

Then, Qian Wenhuan hesitated for a while and ultimately kicked out the files of the two oldest police officers. The two police officers were over forty years old. Although they were just entering middle age, they were still dropped by Qian Wenhuan.

Following that, he removed a police officer who had more abstract facial features, and a police officer who was only paralyzed in the lower body, but whose arms were still usable.

In the end, in addition to the two files he had selected before, only one other met the requirements.

This was a man named Hodge. He was different from the previous two. It was because he was chasing a culprit’s vehicle and his vehicle rolled over due to a collision with the other party, which eventually caused a fire and resulted in severe burns all over his body.

This man named Hodge had lost all the skin over his body and at the same time, his hands and feet had been burned to charcoal, reducing his physiological functions. Even his five sense organs had been completely burnt.

The burnt parts also included his trachea and lungs. Now he could only live in the intensive care unit and hang on to life by sheer will.

Looking at the date when the incident happened, it turned out to be only half a month ago. Seeing this, Qian Wenhuan could not sit still anymore. He just stood up and said to the secretary, “Go and inform the company’s office in Windhoek. Ask them to go to the Windhoek General Hospital right now, look for a patient named Hodge, and tell the hospital that no matter how much money it spends, it must save the life of this patient.”

Then, Qian Wenhuan thought for a while and added. “By the way, tell the transportation department of the company that I need a private helicopter for a trip to Windhoek.”

“Understood.”

The secretary understood immediately and he quickly walked out of the office.

Looking at the three files in his hand, Qian Wenhuan was caught in a dilemma. He wanted to choose Hodge directly, but the other two were also very qualified, so he thought about it and took all three files. After that, he got into the elevator and went up toward the top floor.

After making that familiar journey to the highest floor, Qian Wenhuan instantly saw Chen Chen sitting in front of the floor-to-ceiling windows opposite the elevator because the entire top floor was an office.

“Old Qian, you’re here.”

Chen Chen did not seem to be surprised by Qian Wenhuan’s arrival. He just stared at the tabletop extremely seriously as if looking at something that ordinary people could not see.

Seeing that Chen Chen was maintaining this posture the entire time, Qian Wenhuan could not help feeling a little mystified. He looked at Chen Chen’s table but became even more dumbfounded because there was nothing on the table where Chen Chen was staring.

“Boss, what are you doing?”

Qian Wenhuan said in amazement.

“I’m looking at the subatomic world.”

Chen Chen did not look up, but calmly replied, “There’s a planet in front of me and there are a group of strange-looking aborigines on the planet, but the lifespan of these aborigines is extremely short.

“Also, because I’m too large, they can’t observe my existence, yet I can look down at them and observe their lifetimes silently...”

Qian Wenhuan opened his mouth again but said nothing. He did not quite understand what Chen Chen was referring to.

“To put it plainly, it means that I’m observing a dust particle in front of me and a group of bacteria on that particle.”

Only then Chen Chen raised his head and a smile curled up at the corner of his mouth. He said, “Old Qian, do you think there’s an observer in the sky above our earth? The reason we can’t find him may not be that he’s hiding, but that we don’t even have the power to discover him...”

“But wouldn’t that be God?”

Qian Wenhuan frowned. At this time, he had been deeply influenced by the dogma of God’s Forbidden Zone and he was completely skeptical about the existence of God.

“No, of course, that’s not God.”

Chen Chen shook his head. “According to the Bible, God created this world, but the observer only observes. He’s not responsible for ‘creation’, but often represents ‘destruction’.”

At the end of this line, Chen Chen suddenly stretched out a finger, pressed it lightly on the desk in front of him, and then wiped it viciously across the surface...

When Chen Chen raised his finger again, a thin black smear had appeared on the desk in front of him.

The dust particle was crushed by Chen Chen.

Seeing this scene, Qian Wenhuan’s expression flickered slightly. Although he understood the meaning of Chen Chen’s words, he did not understand Chen Chen’s actions.

Moreover, could human beings see microbes like bacteria with the naked eye?

Still, Chen Chen had only brought this up casually. He did not explain further, but instead asked, “Has the candidate been decided?”

“Not yet, there are currently three options. I find it a little hard to choose, so I specially came to ask you, Boss.”

Qian Wenhuan shook his head, took out all files of information, and placed them in front of Chen Chen.

He saw Chen Chen turning the pages nonchalantly at a rate of two seconds per page. It only took less than three minutes for Chen Chen to read all the information.

Still, Qian Wenhuan was accustomed to this, because every time he supplied information to Chen Chen, Chen Chen would read it at this speed. Not only would he finish reading it, but he also never forgot the contents of what he read.

At this time, Chen Chen tossed the three files back onto the table. He thought for a while and then said, “All of these three fulfill the requirements well. In this case, why don’t we let them form a group?”

chapters.title: Chapter 455: Trio
chapters.body:
Chapter 455: Trio

Chapter 455: Trio

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

With Chen Chen’s approval, the helicopter was prepared. Qian Wenhuan went directly to the roof, got on the helicopter, and immediately flew to Windhoek.

More than an hour later, Qian Wenhuan met the police officer Hodge, who was now staying in the intensive care unit and was burned to the point where he no longer looked human.

Across the glass, Qian Wenhuan, accompanied by the hospital director, silently looked at the brightly lit intensive care unit, in which there was only one person who was wrapped like a mummy.

As his hands and feet were so burnt, the man’s arms and legs had been amputated. If one was not paying attention, one could not even tell that the person on the bed was a person.

“CEO Qian, this patient is Hodge.”

At this time, the director of Windhoek Hospital was by Qian Wenhuan’s side and he exclaimed, “He has been here for half a month and he’s still struggling not to die almost every day. To be honest, he can live till now is already a miracle.”

“Do you have no hope of healing him?”

Qian Wenhuan looked at the room inside the glass window with a calm, still expression.

“No, the patient’s wounds have started to deteriorate a few days ago.”

The director shook his head. “His external defense system has completely collapsed, his trachea is damaged, his lungs are damaged, and his internal defense system is going to be on the verge of collapse. It will end in one or two days. No one can pull him back from the brink of death.”

Qian Wenhuan glanced at the director, but the man played dumb and only chuckled. “What are you going to do, CEO Qian?”

“I want to meet Hodge’s family, please link me to them, Mr. Director.”

Qian Wenhuan said solemnly.

“Well...”

The director hesitated for a while, wearing a look of embarrassment. “That’s not so easy for us. After all, this involves the privacy of patients. As a company, if you...”

Before he finished talking, Qian Wenhuan looked directly at the secretary behind him. The secretary nodded, took out his mobile phone, dialed a number, and then said, “CEO Qian, Hodge’s father is now on his way to the hospital. He’ll be here in three minutes.”

“Come, let’s go meet him on the first floor.”

Qian Wenhuan turned around and left, leaving only the bewildered director and a group of doctors standing outside the intensive care unit.

“Dir, director?”

A doctor asked stutteringly.

“Let’s head down too!”

The director snorted coldly, followed Qian Wenhuan, and walked toward the first floor.

The hospital had only four floors in total. The ward for the ICU was located on the top floor and it took only two or three minutes to walk down from the fourth floor.

Soon, Qian Wenhuan saw Hodge’s father at the doors.

Hodge was only twenty-four years old. He was younger than the other two eligible targets. Fortunately, he was the son of a wealthy white businessman in Windhoek.

If he were just the son of an ordinary Namibian citizen, he would probably not have enough money to live in the intensive care unit and he would have been buried in the ground by now.

However, according to the information, although Hodge’s father was a wealthy businessman, he was only slightly richer than ordinary people. The intensive care unit treatment in the past half month had almost been unsustainable for him.

Still, when he learned of the arrival of Blacklight Biotechnology who was willing to treat his son for free, the wealthy white businessman finally displayed a relieved expression despite his lingering doubts.

“Mr. William, we at Blacklight Biotechnology already know about your son’s situation. If you agree, we can transfer him to the Eco Science City to treat him with everything at our disposal and even install the most advanced bionic prosthesis on the market, so that he can return to his police post.”

The secretary next to Qian Wenhuan took out a prepared contract and handed it to the other party. The contract had more than 50 pages and there were over 1,000 clauses.

Looking at the contract in his hand, the white rich businessman seemed to understand something. He said solemnly, “Just tell me, what is your purpose, and what do you need us to do?”

“We’re currently developing some new bionic technologies that require people to try them on. Such people cannot be normal people, but disabled.”

Qian Wenhuan did not speak, but the secretary beside him said directly, “This is our purpose and what you have to do is very simple. That is, move to live in Eco Science City. However, to make up for your losses, we’ll subsidize your relocation and we can also take care of your business.”

“I... I need to think about it.”

The wealthy businessman opened his mouth, seeming to be a little tempted, but he was still hesitant. “I need to find a lawyer to help me go through this contract.”

“There’s no time left, Mr. William!”

The secretary suddenly increased his volume and said sternly, “Hodge has now shown signs of infection in his wounds. Do you know how long your son can last? He may not even last a day. You hesitate so much, do you think there’s some conspiracy in our company?”

“Then... Okay.”

The wealthy businessman felt his heart clench, so he had to agree. At the same time, he gritted his teeth and directly signed his name in the position of “Party B”.

At this moment, everyone from the hospital also arrived in front of Qian Wenhuan. Hearing Qian Wenhuan’s secretary persuading Williams, the director said with a gloomy face, “CEO Qian, I’m afraid this won’t do because Mr. Hodge can’t bear the impact of being transferred anymore. Do you think Hodge’s injuries can withstand four or five hours of being jolted about?”

“There’s no need to worry about this point, Mr. Director. Our Eco Science City has phased out ambulances six months ago and now we have been using medical helicopters.”

Qian Wenhuan said lightly, took the contract handed back by the rich businessman, and left the hospital with his secretary and bodyguard without looking back.

“CEO Qian, the director dared to get in our way. Do you need me to send someone to teach him a lesson?”

As soon as they were out the door, the secretary lowered his voice and asked.

“No need.”

Qian Wenhuan shook his head. “This hostility toward us is very common in Namibia. After all, we’ve seized a lot of resources and markets in Namibia, so the locals naturally don’t feel favorable toward us.”

“Then let’s forget it?”

The secretary nodded and relaxed.

“Forget it. Just call the Minister of Health and ask him to find a reason to sack the director of this hospital.”

Qian Wenhuan waved his hand and said placidly.

Secretary: “...”

They sent a notice for Eco Science City to send a medical helicopter to pick up the patient. After that, Qian Wenhuan and his team found the second target’s family.

This was a low-rise bungalow on the outskirts of Windhoek.

Although this was the capital, the suburbs of Windhoek were mostly bungalows made of stones. Although the place was not as prosperous as the city, it was at least a little better than those living in the slums.

However, compared to the other bungalows along the way, this bungalow was undoubtedly more run-down. Most of the red paint on the door had peeled off, exposing the bare door panels, and the doorway was even more disordered. Life here was no bed of roses.

Without waiting for Qian Wenhuan to get out of the car, the bodyguard who had been following the group got out and knocked on the door of this house.

About half a minute later, when the bodyguard was getting a little impatient and thinking that there was no one in the house, he finally heard approaching footsteps, and then the wooden door opened with a creak. A slovenly dressed black woman appeared at the door, and behind her, two biracial children who were four or five years old, were there.

The two children poked their heads out behind their mother, looking at Qian Wenhuan and the team with some curiosity and fear.

“We’re looking for Mr. Brooke.”

The secretary spoke for Qian Wenhuan, “Is he at home?”

“Please come in...”

The woman was stunned, then she moved aside and let everyone walk in.

As soon as they entered the room, Qian Wenhuan and the secretary frowned at the same time, because there was a pungent stench in the room as if this was not a private house, but a public toilet.

Not just any toilet, but a pit toilet at that.

They followed the woman to the innermost room. Finally, Qian Wenhuan and the others met the purpose of their trip, a plump, scruffy man.

The bedding under the man’s body had lost its color long ago and he was lying on the bed like a mountain of meat, completely lacking the hard lines and sharp eyes that were in his photo. All he had was a deathlike numbness.

What was even more frightening was that although the man’s face and body were extremely plump, his limbs were shrunken like bones. At first glance, it looked like four toothpicks were stuck in a mass of flesh...

Detecting the disgusting smell coming from the man, the secretary resisted the discomfort and said, “Mr. Brooke, is it? We’re from Blacklight Biotechnology. This is our general manager, Mr. Qian Wenhuan...”

The secretary waited for a while, but the man in front of him still did not respond. He had to continue. “We want to sign a contract with you. If you agree, we can remodel you...”

Nonetheless, up until this point, the man still did not move and not even his eyes flickered.

“He’s become like this a long time ago.”

Just when the secretary was at a loss, the voice of the black woman suddenly came from behind them. Her voice was calm as if talking about a total stranger. “I’ve tried. No matter what happens outside, he will not respond. He’s dead, spiritually dead.”

Qian Wenhuan glanced at the woman, then stepped forward and squatted directly beside the man’s bed. “Mr. Brooke, I know you can hear our words, so let me skip the nonsense. What if I say we can make you stand up again?”

Even so, the man still did not move. If it were not for his opened eyes and the visible rise and fall of his chest, everyone would think that he was just a corpse.

Seeing that the man still did not react, Qian Wenhuan finally shook his head and prepared to turn around and leave.

However, when he turned around, a hoarse voice suddenly came from behind him. “What you said — is it true?”

“Of course it’s true, Mr. Brooke.”

Qian Wenhuan turned his head and flashed a meaningful smile. “But the only requirement is that you need to sacrifice something.”

“I have nothing to give up.”

Brooke slowly raised his head and it was as if there was something shivering in his pupils the color of cold ash. “As long as I can stand again, I’ll give up all I have...”

A few minutes later, Qian Wenhuan and the others walked out of the room because Brooke had easily signed the contract.

Two of the three targets had been achieved and next, there was only one target left.

However, the remaining target was not in Windhoek, the capital of Namibia, but in a small village farther away.

Thus, Qian Wenhuan took the helicopter again and hurried toward Neham Village, a hundred kilometers away.

This time, it took two hours for them to find the black detective named Blake.

As a native of Namibia, this man named Blake had completely lost his mobility after his limbs were cut off by drug dealers. However, he did not sink into despair and give up like the previous Brooke but found a new job — weaving straw ropes and fishnets.

With his agile mouth, Blake could sit at the table all afternoon and weave a few meters of straw rope. Of course, this was if someone had put the raw material for the straw rope in front of him.

With this job, he lived with his parents and could even make a living with this.

“Who are you people, was it the police who sent you here to solve my physical problems?

“Oh please, how many times have I asked the police station to please send me a woman, I don’t care if she’s white or black, fat or thin. But they send me a bunch of big guys every time?

“D*mn, am I going to get my cherry popped? Those drug dealers that chopped off my limbs had spared my little guy, but now you guys are gonna pop my cherry?”

Looking at the black man yammering away while sitting on the stone bench with a row of straw ropes in front of him, Qian Wenhuan’s secretary could not help being a little surprised.

It was not as if he had not considered the man’s situation. If Blake wanted to survive in a poor country like Namibia without his limbs, he would need to support himself in addition to having a certain family background, but the secretary did not expect that Blake would be so optimistic.

Although he was full of swear words, everyone could tell that the man in front of them was not broken down at all.

“I, I am...”

When the black man in front of him had tirelessly rapped his piece, the secretary came forward. When he was just arranging his words in his head, Qian Wenhuan patted him on the shoulder and said directly, “Mr. Blake, we’re from Blacklight Biotechnology, not sent by the police station.”

“So that’s it? No wonder it’s not a group of black dudes.”

The black man bared his white teeth and laughed. “So what is the purpose of you Chinese guys coming here? Surely it’s not because I’m some sort of tourist attraction? My stinky neighbor always gets this idea, he’s discussed it with me. Ten Namibian dollars for a ticket to visit me, twenty for a photo with me, but I think tourists surely can’t be bored enough to visit some black man when there are plenty of us all over the street.”

“We have another purpose.”

Qian Wenhuan smiled. He did not respond to the other party’s mouthful of babble and just said, “Listening to your speech, it seems that you’re deeply influenced by American culture. Then I think you must have seen a Hollywood movie, the name of that movie is Robocop...”

“No, I haven’t watched it. It sounds a bit like a superhero movie.”

Blake shook his head blankly. “I have only seen one superhero movie, it’s called Black Panther.

chapters.title: Chapter 456: Operation
chapters.body:
Chapter 456: Operation

Chapter 456: Operation

The next day, when Chen Chen got up from the Medpod 3000, he had already received the news that the three people had been transported back.

It was not only the three targets but their families, who would also move to Eco Science City within this week. Qian Wenhuan had arranged three suites for them at a district in the city center.

Seeing this, Chen Chen left the Spire laboratory directly after washing up and returned to the Biological Research Center.

The Biological Research Center also had its own independent ward and it was far more advanced than Eco Science City Central Hospital. At this time, in a ward of the lab building, Chen Chen got a good look at the police officer named Hodge.

At this time, Hodge had been stuffed into a life incubator and it was also filled with amniotic fluid. Hodge was suspended in the center of the incubator, with a large number of tubes connected to his mouth, nose, and even all over his body. He was like an unborn baby.

The life incubator was surrounded by seven or eight expert surgeons.

“Mr. Chen!”

“Boss...”

“Boss!”

When Chen Chen arrived, the group greeted him immediately.

“Thanks for your hard work, everyone.”

Chen Chen nodded in response and said, “The purpose I invited you to come is very simple. It’s to do everything possible to save the life of this police officer. I think it shouldn’t be difficult for you all.”

“It’s not difficult if we’re only keeping him alive, but the difficulty is in getting him out of this life-saving device and him still surviving.”

A middle-aged man with a ruddy complexion said, “At present, his burns are too serious. Anyone who experiences this kind of injury will die on the spot. But not only did he not die, but he also held on for half a month in a regular ICU ward. I must admit, it’s quite a miracle...”

“It shows that the patient has a strong willpower and the desire to survive.”

Someone else nodded and admitted, “But this is also related to how his physical fitness was strong enough. I can hardly believe that his immune system is still working and hasn’t been defeated by infected bacteria...”

“Now we have disinfected his body and placed him in a sterile solution to cut off the channels for bacteria to multiply. Now his life can be preserved.”

The middle-aged man continued to speak, “And now the first step we need to do is to replace the lungs. Sixty percent of the patient’s lungs have stopped functioning. We’d better searched internationally to purchase a pair of lungs that match his DNA to perform a transplant.”

“No need for that.”

Hearing this, Chen Chen replied immediately, “Project Xingtian’s department has developed mature bionic organs, including bionic stomachs, bionic hearts, and bionic lungs that can replace one’s digestive organs. These can be used on Hodge first and the department members of Project Xingtian will soon contact you.”

“Thank God...”

“This is inspiring news...”

Many doctors immediately perked up. Chen Chen’s news was exciting because the advent of this technology meant that in the future, it was very likely that some patients would no longer need to wait for an organ donor.

After all, this group of people was only contract workers of Blacklight Biotechnology. Although they were all internationally renowned professor-level doctors, they were not members of God’s Forbidden Zone. Therefore, they would not know that, back when Project Xingtian was launched, there were already corresponding bionic organs, just that Chen Chen felt they were too advanced, so they were struck off.

However, it had been a year since the launch of Project Xingtian. Now Blacklight Biotechnology had successfully leveled up to become a legal arms dealer. Under this circumstance, some technologies that had to be concealed before could now be released without fear of being coveted by other countries.

Now, all continents and countries had regarded Blacklight Biotechnology as an existence of equal rank, rather than a piece of pie for everyone to fight over.

“In addition, we also need to use his DNA to cultivate the skin and give him skin grafts, otherwise, he will not be able to leave the incubator.”

At this moment, someone on the side suddenly added. “It will take about two to three months for all this to be done. Then it will be time for the bionic prosthetic design department to do its job.”

Chen Chen nodded when he heard these words. Two to three months was still within a time frame he could accept. At this, he found a reason at random to turn and leave.

If Chen Chen used the Medpod 3000, he could indeed heal Hodge’s injury within a few minutes. Although the excised limbs would not grow back, at least Hodge would be instantly pulled back from death’s door.

However, if this was the case, his healing process would become a mystery. In the future, Hodge might be a world-famous member of the Interpol, exposed to thousands of spotlights. At that time, every wisp of mystery surrounding him would be explored, so Chen Chen was naturally unwilling to leave behind any clues now.

Therefore, Chen Chen decided to use traditional methods to treat Hodge. At the same time, his treatment process would be recorded every day, which would become evidence of his fight against the disease. It would be publicized when Hodge became a Robocop in the future, naturally winning him even more fans.

Next, Chen Chen went to the next room and saw the two other targets — Brooke and Blake.

Brooke suffered from spinal paralysis and he had not been kept in shape for many years. His limbs had completely atrophied and become necrotic. Even if the God chip were installed, he could not control his necrotic limbs to stand up again.

Not only that, but he was also plagued by various complications such as liver cirrhosis, hydronephrosis, diabetes, and so on. Therefore, the first operation he had upon coming to Eco Science City was to remove his necrotic limbs and hollow out the liver in his abdomen and one of his kidneys, plus a liposuction operation.

On the flip side, Blake was in the best shape.

Blake relied on his flexible mouth and tongue to weave straw ropes all year round and his lips were unusually nimble. He even boasted that he could bend down and bite himself, but because of this, he suffered from serious cervical spondylosis and needed a corrective operation.

At this time, both of them were wearing hospital gowns and sitting in wheelchairs and in front of them was an attending doctor in charge of them. The few of them did not notice Chen Chen’s arrival, who only heard the doctor say:

“Mr. Brooke, in the next operation, we’ll amputate your limbs and we also need to remove your kidneys because they have lost their function in the past few years.”

The doctor looked regretful. “But please be rest assured, we naturally have a replacement. After your organs have been substituted for new ones, your body will be healthier than before. Then, using our bionic prosthesis, we’ll make you stand up successfully once more.”

Still, no matter what the doctor said, the man named Brooke just remained collapsed in the wheelchair and did not move at all.

However, by this point, the doctor also knew that Brooke had heard him clearly, but was just unwilling to respond. This was a type of post-traumatic stress disorder caused by a huge blow.

On one side, nonetheless, Blake could not sit still anymore. He shot an unkindly look at the fat Brooke, and then sighed. “Man, you’re willing to let yourself go after a small setback like this? Are you The Thinker or something? What are you meditating on, are you like some little girl who’s praying silently in her heart?”

With that, Blake put on an aggrieved, feminine manner, and at the same time said in a high-pitched voice, “God, come and save me, save me, I’m trapped under this sack of meat and can’t move anymore. Who can help me? I want to stand, I want to run. Run, b*tch, run...”

“Er...”

The attending doctor wore an awkward look. “Mr. Blake, your body is very healthy. The only thing is that you have cervical spondylosis. We only need to perform an operation on you to correct it.”

“These operations are free, right?”

Blake perked up and quickly asked, “After all, I don’t have the money to pay. If I have to pay back the bill, I think I can become a toy for those rich ladies. Have you ever heard of a human toilet? Here I am! And I’m so hard, all the villagers call me Granite...”

“Enough, enough, enough...”

The doctor raised his hand, gestured for a pause, and tamped down his inner anger. “Stop! Mr. Blake, I, we at Blacklight Biotechnology spent hundreds of millions of dollars to tailor a Black Panther armor for you. As a result, you now want to be a human toilet? Would you please respect our profession?”

“Please buddy, don’t you want to try it?”

Blake was not ashamed but proud. He was just about to say something but saw Chen Chen was standing there at some unknown time. “It’s another Chinese guy. Hey buddy, do you think what I said makes sense?”

“It makes sense. So what do you plan to name your armor?”

Chen Chen stepped forward and said casually, “The Granite or the Black Panther?”

“This...”

Blake was dumbfounded and fell into a dilemma.

“Mr. Chen!”

The attending doctor greeted him immediately, “Why are you here?”

“I just came for a look, don’t mind me.”

Chen Chen waved his hand and stood aside to listen.

Next, the attending doctor explained a series of things that those two should pay attention to. Brooke remained as still as a corpse. Even if Blake was on the side, no matter how vulgar his words were, Brooke still did not respond. Moreover, Blake kept cracking offensive jokes, greatly infuriating the attending doctor.

Seeing this, Chen Chen did not know whether to laugh or cry, because he understood that Blake also had a huge psychological issue. It was just concealed under that cheerful appearance and the fact that his mouth never slowed down was the best proof.

Nonetheless, Chen Chen could help treat their physical injuries, but they could only rely on themselves to recover psychologically.

More than an hour finally passed before the attending doctor asked the nurse to bring them down.

Leaving the lab building, Chen Chen saw Little X as soon as he walked out the door.

At this time, Little X was wearing a modern white dress, squatting on the lawn outside the door, holding her chin with both hands, and obediently waiting for Chen Chen to come out.

Chen Chen had become accustomed to the fact that Little X used this body to run around. Not only him, but even everyone in Blacklight Biotechnology already knew that there was a fifteen or sixteen-year-old girl by Chen Chen’s side, who had extremely high authority and could go in and out any place she liked.

As for the real identity of this little girl, no one knew about it. Even Qian Wenhuan, who had the highest authority in the Eco Science City, did not know. He only thought that she was seen by Chen Chen as a godsister and she was also a knowledgeable scientific research genius.

Seeing Chen Chen coming out, Little X immediately smiled in elation, and ran to Chen Chen in two or three steps, twirling around like a butterfly. “Sir Godfather, do I look good in my new clothes?”

Chen Chen took a casual look and said perfunctorily, “It’s okay, it looks no different from before.”

“Oh...”

Little X suddenly became a little crestfallen and then said, “Sir Godfather, I have simulated the results of the surgery. I think Brooke and Hodge need a more thorough remodeling operation, which not only shortens the time but also prevents the risk of death for Hodge now.”

Chen Chen was immediately engaged. He nodded and said, “Tell me.”

“Isn’t Hodge burned all over? Not only did he lose his skin, but even his muscles were burned away, so I think his body is no longer necessary for salvation.”

Little X tilted her head and said, “We can amputate everything starting from the bottom of Hodge’s neck. This saves us the time waiting for him to heal. Anyway, with our current technology, even a single head can be kept alive and the same goes for Brooke and Blake.”

“This is indeed the best choice.”

Chen Chen smiled and then explained, “But we have to consider more than these factors. In addition, we have to consider a very important thing — human sympathy.

“Don’t forget, we’ll need them to stand under the world’s spotlight in the future. Then everything about them will be revealed. If someone exposes their medical records, won’t the entire world soon know that we’ve removed some of their functioning parts?

“That’s giving them the evidence they need.”

Chen Chen shook his head and half-jokingly said, “So, we try not to make trouble on these issues. Don’t forget how the company’s bosses always die in the movie Robocop. I don’t want to repeat the same mistakes.”

“Ok...”

Little X shrugged and replied with a pouting mouth, “Then we’ll just follow the doctor’s plan, but it will cost more money and we’ll need to design unique armors for them. This way, the cost is even greater.”

“It’s alright, let’s just go all out.”

Chen Chen encouraged her. “Don’t forget, what we need now is a long-term investment. This is a very abstract thing, but you should understand that just like your Prophet System, human vision for the future also counts as a sort of prophet system, only it’s not as precise as yours.”

“Yes...”

Following the conversation between the two, Chen Chen and Little X returned to the headquarters of Blacklight Biotechnology.

Following that, it was a period of advanced publicity and waiting.

They were waiting for those three to be remodeled fully before being presented to the world again.

However, although the three were remodeled by Blacklight Biotechnology and signed a large number of agreements with Blacklight Biotechnology, for the sake of legitimacy, on the surface they were still police officers in Namibia and not affiliated with Blacklight Biotechnology.

This was because Blacklight Biotechnology was only a private company and thus did not have law enforcement powers in Namibia. That belonged only to the police.

chapters.title: Chapter 457: Hodge
chapters.body:
Chapter 457: Hodge

Chapter 457: Hodge

This was a three-story bungalow located in the Windhoek villa area.

The towering European-style building, the grassy courtyard, and the iron fence with gold trimmings in front of the door all illustrated the wealth of the owner of this bungalow.

In Namibia, this house undoubtedly represented the identity of its master.

At this time, Hodge was looking at everything in front of him in confusion. This was his home.

He could not remember when he came to the door nor why he stood here. In his memory, he had been driving after several murderers when he was hit by his opponent on the right side of the vehicle.

Then, there was a whirl of heaven and earth...

Hodge took a breath subconsciously, but suddenly he noticed a pungent burnt stench. Something seemed to be charred, but when he looked around, he found that there was nothing.

There was no fire, nothing burning.

Had I been rescued?

Hodge looked at all this in confusion. He did not remember when he appeared here or even how long he stood in front of his door. It seemed that he had just arrived home and it seemed that he had been standing for a long time...

Just when Hodge was a little puzzled, he saw the door slowly opening behind the iron fence. A stern middle-aged man appeared behind the door. His gaze toward Hodge’s carried both anticipation and disappointment.

“Father.”

Hodge hurriedly waved to the man, then directly opened the fence’s door and walked quickly into his home.

“Father, I’m back!”

Hodge said loudly, but before he finished speaking, he was taken aback because he discovered that his father had disappeared at the door somehow. Not only that but all his relatives were gathered in his home and the atmosphere was buzzing with activity. They seemed to be having a party.

“Jenny, is there some celebration today?”

Hodge asked the babysitter next to him, but she did not even look at him and walked directly past him. Hodge yelled, but she still ignored him.

“Okay!”

Hodge spread his hands and made a helpless gesture, but then he saw his mother and grandmother huddled together, walking toward him.

“Hey! Mom, what happened at home?”

Joy erupted across Hodge’s face and he asked his mother like a little child, but in the next second, he watched the two pass by him.

What surprised him even more was that not only his mother, but even his grandmother who loved him the most, was acting as if he did not exist.

The joy on Hodge’s face suddenly froze and he gradually noticed that something was wrong.

He did not know when, but the room was filled with a trace of smoke, with a pungent scorched stench that made it difficult for Hodge to breathe. He coughed and at the same time, an inexplicable panic seized his heart...

Why was no one able to see him?

With this in mind, Hodge was just about to desperately find his father to clear things up, but he saw his father in formal wear suddenly leading a girl in a wedding dress to the living room.

The moment he saw the girl, he was immediately stunned.

“A, Amanda...”

Hodge looked at her blankly. The girl in front of him was wearing a white and gorgeous wedding dress, with an aura that was ethereal and pure, as flawless as a pearl in the sea.

The girl was none other than his fiancée — Amanda.

“My son, today is your wedding day, don’t you remember?”

His father’s voice was rich and booming, and he broke into a slight smile, and finally looked in Hodge’s direction. “I’m proud of you, Son!”

Following his father’s words, Hodge’s mother, grandmother, and even a group of relatives appeared all around the living room, applauding ceaselessly. The room, for a while, was filled with a warm atmosphere of joy.

“Father...”

At this point, Hodge finally relaxed. He wore a hearty smile. It turned out that everything just now was a prank for him planned by his family?

Solemn music gradually drifted in the air. With everyone’s blessing, the girl in the wedding dress walked toward Hodge step by step, with a shy blush, slowly taking Hodge’s hand in the hazy smoke of the house.

Then, with the crowd watching, Hodge and the girl performed a western ballroom dance.

At this moment, an immense sense of rapture enveloped Hodge. He closed his eyes, intoxicated, and only hoped that the world would linger at this moment forever...

“He’s smiling, maybe he’s having a good dream...”

“Preparing to bring the patient out of the protective drowsing state. He has been in this dream for three months, it’s time to wake up...”

“Starting to lower the dopamine. Start the electric shock device, prepare for electric shock...”

Just when Hodge was immersed in endless happiness, a series of conversations was faintly heard all around him. The noises seemed to be right in front of him and also seemed to come from a distance. In short, it was very inconsistent with his dreams.

“What’s going on...”

Hodge opened his eyes subconsciously, but he was taken aback because he discovered that the girl in front of him had disappeared some time ago, and vanishing at the same time were the relatives in the room who were wishing him well.

Father, mother, grandmother, and Amanda... Everyone disappeared!

“No, no, this isn’t real!”

The smoke in front of Hodge’s eyes became thicker and thicker, almost blocking his vision. He subconsciously wanted to escape this weird room, but found that at some point, his home began to fill with endless flames!

Hodge looked desperately at the flames rapidly surrounding him and at the same time, the burnt smell reached its peak. Then there was a sudden sting!

This tingling pain came from his abdomen as if he had been shocked by an electric jolt. There was a burning prickling sensation in his abdomen.

“Agh! Ah ah ah agh agh!”

At this moment, Hodge suddenly roared. His voice was filled with anguish as if everything had exploded at this moment!

“The patient has woken up!”

Currently, in a sterile laboratory full of high-tech equipment, several doctors were looking solemnly at a person lying on a special instrument in front of them.

This person only had a bare head exposed and his body was covered by a set of matte black armor. The gaps in the armor interlocked with each other, but in accordance with the structure of the human body, it produced the shape of the chest and abdominal muscles, which looked very attractive.

By looking at this face, Chen Chen could immediately recognize that he was Hodge who was burned before.

However, at this time, Hodge’s skin and facial features had been completely repaired. Not only that but his hands and feet had also reappeared on his body. The only dissonance was that his hands and feet were wrapped in the same layer of black armor as his body. There were even red and blue patterns on the surface, like some kind of artwork.

Even so, at this time, Hodge was lying on a special instrument made of metal and at the same time, he kept snarling with a deafening cry.

“What’s the matter, is he out of control?”

“The timing of his waking up is wrong. The patient is releasing too much adrenaline. At the same time, his blood pressure is rising rapidly. Get ready to inject a tranquilizer!”

“No, wait a minute!”

Just when the nurse was about to follow the doctor’s command, a gray-haired middle-aged doctor suddenly spoke with a solemn expression, “Don’t interfere with him, let him vent first. This is good for his psychology and physiology!”

This doctor had a very high status even among the many authoritative experts. As he spoke, everyone suddenly fell silent.

Hodge screamed for half a minute and stopped when he was completely out of breath. He gasped heavily while opening his eyes blankly.

“You’re awake.”

A group of doctors quickly gathered around, looking at Hodge one by one, revealing irrepressible delight as if they were sculptors looking at a perfect sculpture created by their painstaking efforts.

“Where... Where am I...”

Hodge looked at the people above his head with a glimmer of horror. He subconsciously wanted to sit up but found that he could not move a finger. Not only that, there were a lot of unfamiliar numbers and tables in his vision. As his gaze wandered, these squares in his eyes were still shifting constantly...

Could it be that he was still dreaming?

“Your current location is the Biological Research Center of Eco Science City under Blacklight Biotechnology.”

The gray-haired middle-aged man displayed a gentle smile. “We’re your chief physician and designer of bionic prostheses. My name is Sheridan Todd. You can call me Doctor Todd.”

“What happened, why can’t I move?”

Hodge asked suspiciously, “Also, why can’t I feel my body?”

“Please relax, Hodge.”

Doctor Todd replied slowly, trying to get Hodge into his own rhythm of conversation. “Your body is still there, but something has happened... A little problem, but it’s okay, it’s fixed now.

“However, we still need to do some tests before letting you resume movement. For example, do you still remember your last memory?”

“My last memory?”

Hodge’s face was blank. He silently recalled the fragments in his mind before he woke up, but after thinking about it, there were only the bits and pieces of dreams in his mind, but he had long forgotten how he got here...

“I’ve forgotten...”

Hodge shook his head.

“It’s fine.”

On the contrary, Doctor Todd appeared to be relieved. “Please rest assured, this is just a type of post-traumatic stress disorder. The fact that you’ve forgotten may not be a bad thing, but on the contrary, could be a good thing. At least it delays and reduces the extent of further damage to your heart.”

“So what the f*ck are you talking about? What the f*ck happened to me?”

Nonetheless, even when he heard these words, Hodge became furious. He began to yell and at the same time, began to struggle with his head as if he wanted to sit up again from his lying position.

“Don’t get worked up. We’ll tell you what happened, but not now.”

Doctor Todd waved his hand and all the other doctors and medical staff retreated in all directions. “Hodge, don’t struggle. We have now disabled the mobility function of the XTN-118 police bionic armor. No matter how hard you struggle, it will be useless. But if you’re willing to calm down, we can have a discussion and help you start up your mobility...”

Hearing these words and seeing the attitude of the people, Hodge took a deep breath, suppressed his rising tension, and tried to lower his heart rate at the same time.

“Very good, that’s it, calm down...”

Doctor Todd spoke slowly and at the same time looked at a medical staff nearby. The female nurse immediately reacted. She took up the tablet in her hand and said at once, “The patient’s heart rate and blood pressure are rapidly decreasing. The current heart rate frequency is 120 beats per minute, still decreasing... It has been reduced to 80... No, it’s 75 beats per minute...”

“Very good.”

Seeing this, Doctor Todd finally nodded in satisfaction. He walked slowly in front of Hodge and at the same time placed his hand on the instrument where Hodge was lying, where there was a red button.

“Director Todd, will this...”

The other attending doctors hesitated a little and reflexively stopped him. “Will there be danger?”

“He’s also human and a qualified police officer. He will not hurt us.”

Doctor Todd shook his head seriously, then turned to look at Hodge. “I’m going to release you now, Hodge.”

With that, before Hodge could speak, Doctor Todd pressed the button directly.

“Click, hiss —!”

With the sound of something being unlocked and even a sound of a valve releasing air, Hodge saw only two rows of probes between his ribs being automatically pulled out and his body suddenly loosened!

In the next second, Hodge moved quickly and sat up from the instrument!

Suddenly, except for Doctor Todd and a few doctors in front of him, everyone in the entire laboratory subconsciously took two or three steps back and their gazes toward Hodge were full of vigilance.

It was not like they were looking at a human being, but some other species...

Seeing this scene, Hodge lowered his head dazedly and looked at his body slowly, but realized that he was wrapped in a layer of black armor from head to toe.

Not only that but as he stretched out his hand and curiously touched his body, he also had a strange tactile sensation.

This sense of touch was slower than his original body, but it was unexpectedly acute in the details.

“What is this...”

Hodge slowly raised his head, looked at Doctor Todd, and asked in a low voice, “You placed a suit of armor on me? Is this an April Fool’s Day spoof? A tribute to Iron Man? Or Cyborg?”

“None of those. We’re saving your life!”

Doctor Todd spread out his hands with an excited expression. “You were in a car accident and almost died from a serious injury, but our Blacklight Biotechnology saved you and helped you survive the almost fatal injury, you know?”

Hearing Doctor Todd’s explanation, Hodge fell silent.

The chaos in his brain had gradually become clear from the beginning. It was only now that he slowly remembered that he seemed to have been driving a police car to chase a few murderers, but because he was hit by the opponent’s car, his vehicle flipped over on the highway...

As for what happened next, his memory was blank as if someone had deleted his memory...

The only thing that remained indelible in his mind was the pungent burning smell and the dense smoke that filled his eyes...

“Agh...”

Hodge suddenly let out a scream as if touching something deep in his soul. He slumped to the ground while covering his head and convulsed uncontrollably.

“Director, the patient’s blood pressure is starting to rise again!”

“His EEG has entered the peak stage and is still going up!”

“We can’t control him!”

“Inject a tranquilizer!”

Seeing this, Doctor Todd finally stopped hesitating and gave the order to inject a tranquilizer.

In the next second, under Hodge’s armor layer, an unseen needle swiftly popped out and pierced Hodge’s body directly...

At this point, Hodge gradually calmed down and fell asleep again.

At once, Hodge’s body moved on its own. He climbed back to the special instrument that looked like a bed and slotted his own frame neatly into the groove under him.

Seeing this scene, Doctor Todd could not help sighing and then said with satisfaction, “At present, the patient’s physiological indicators are all standard. There’s also no problem with the XTN-118 police bionic armor. Next, we’ll wait for him to wake up. After he’s calmed down, let’s take the measurements again.”

When everyone heard these words, they all cheered. The result of three months’ worth of work was ultimately not in vain.

chapters.title: Chapter 458: Awakening And Promises
chapters.body:
Chapter 458: Awakening And Promises

Chapter 458: Awakening and Promises

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

After an indiscernible amount of time had passed, Hodge slowly opened his eyes again and came to accept reality.

He was familiar with the reputable name of Blacklight Biotechnology, as well as the existence of Project Xingtian. It was the bionic prosthetics project launched by Blacklight Biotechnology last year.

The rest of the puzzle was easy to piece together — something must have happened to him during the previous pursuit that brought him to where he was now.

This should also suggest that something horrible must have happened to his body...

What was in front of him was still the speckless, enchantingly white laboratory. The laboratory was so bright that it could easily cause someone a headache just by gazing at the lights. However, his eyes were not even remotely strained by this even when he stared directly into the light source.

“The patient is awake.”

Before he could fully comprehend his situation, he heard a feminine voice coming from beside him. When he attempted to turn in that direction, he realized that his body refused to budge at all.

“Hodge.”

There was another familiar voice. Doctor Todd appeared in his field of vision soon after that, the doctor looked down at him and asked earnestly, “Did you sleep well?”

“Tell me, what happened to my body?”

Hodge gulped and asked with a deep grumble, “Why can’t I move and why am I wearing this blasted thing. What exactly happened to my body?”

Doctor Todd remained silent for a moment as he inspected Hodge’s data readings. He finally answered, “Sooner or later, you’ll have to face reality.
