Chen Chen’s expression sharpened. Little X was the fastest supercomputer in the world. If something made her suspicious, then there was a real problem.

“The location of the incident was the Herrank Warehouse District. There are countless items stored there from all over the world, both in the district and outside the district. Even the industrial park of our Eco Science City has a certain level of international trade contact with this place.”

Little X said softly, “But just yesterday night and the night before that, two murders suddenly occurred. The first was the night before yesterday when a worker in the warehouse area was killed by having his neck twisted by an unknown method.

“Initially it seemed that it was just a chance incident. The Los Angeles police withdrew after investigating and collecting evidence. However, just last night, the case suddenly escalated. Eight people were killed again in the same night and all eight people died in the same way. All their necks were twisted until they died and some of the deceased even had their spine pulled out.”

Hearing this, Chen Chen could not help looking solemn. “Their necks were twisted? X, do you suspect this is an abnormal murder case?”

“I’m not sure. I only feel that Los Angeles has usually been safe, but just as we arrived, this kind of thing happened suddenly.”

Little X explained, “So, I wonder if these changes are related to our present actions?”

“Maybe.”

Chen Chen did not deny that directly. He raised his head and felt the strong constant wind against his face. “Maybe this is just a coincidence or maybe all of this is related to the movie plot, but we can’t stop the plan because of this abnormality, otherwise, we’ll easily disrupt the plot.

“I’m not sure what will happen if we do so. Maybe nothing will happen or it might cause an even worse chain reaction, even resulting in a rejection by the laws of the world. Just like in Final Destination, we might be directly killed by some universal law. But this still requires a little bit of trial and error.”

As he spoke, Chen Chen sighed suddenly, then the corners of his mouth turned up slightly. “X, haven’t you noticed? Are we now the same as before, groping for the rules of the USB drive bit by bit?”

“Yes...”

Little X nodded nervously.

“With that magical USB flash drive, just when we think we’ve understood it sufficiently, it will always display more changes. Just like an iceberg floating on the surface of the sea — you’ll never know how much of this iceberg is hidden below sea level...”

Chen Chen narrowed his eyes and said quietly, “It’s the same thing now. If I want to control the USB flash drive, I must constantly test out more of its rules. Unless I’d rather stop without progressing, this is a path that we cannot avoid.”

“I understand, we will not run from all this.”

In the headset, Little X’s voice became solemn.

“Very good.”

Chen Chen smiled and said, “Contact the computer ‘Adam’ of the Earth Federation headquarters and ask him to make a prediction to see who the murderer is behind this case. This is what we can do now and everything else will proceed as usual.”

“Got it!”

After Little X’s promise, Chen Chen kicked off with one foot and completely stepped out of the building. As he stood amid the strong gale, his clothes fluttered furiously. After that, he fixed upon a direction, abruptly leaned his body forward, and then there was a loud “bang”!

Up ahead, there was only a trail of scattered mist but Chen Chen’s figure had vanished without a trace!

chapters.title: Chapter 469: Persuasion
chapters.body:
Chapter 469: Persuasion

Chapter 469: Persuasion

Yellowstone Park, Northern America, in an uninhabited area.

A building hidden amid thousands of trees stood tall. There were no villages and roads within a hundred miles of it. There were only a few intricate secret military bases, guarding this square building like bodyguards.

Here was the true core of the Earth Federation — the Federation’s Quantum Deity Laboratory and in this building was the world’s most precious thing, “Adam”.

Adam was a quantum computer but its form was very strange, like an abstract sculpture built by an artist. It was composed of large and small translucent glass-like gears as well as irregular parts. Many tassels were hanging down too, full of strange beauty.

If one day “Adam” broke down or lost its power of prediction, then within a month, the entire Earth Federation would fall apart, disintegrating like the Soviet Union in the past. There would once again be a situation where nations were separated and conquered each other. ...

Even so, on such a night, the quantum computer “Adam” suddenly started up by itself, jingling with the sound of clinking glass.

“Clink clink...”

“Clink clink...”

However, just such a small collision was comparable to the storm of a century in the eyes of the many monitoring personnel outside —

“Report, Group 14 has detected abnormal fluctuations!”

“Report, Group 10 has detected abnormal fluctuations!”

“Group 17 has also detected that the analytical curve valves of the third and sixth indexes are still rising, from an average of 30% to 102%. The eighth index’s curve has increased by 1,000%!”

“What happened?”

“It’s a curve disturbance. Adam is predicting the future of an event!”

As everyone yelled out updates in a disorganized manner, a harsh alarm blared out loudly. In an instant, the entire monitoring team was in chaos. Everyone tried to parse the strings of codes for the quantum computer’s operations, but what was shocking was that these codes were all encrypted!

“Why! Why did ‘Adam’ suddenly use encryption codes?”

“We don’t know, this phenomenon has never happened before!”

“Decipher them quickly, hurry up!”

However, it was not easy to parse the encryption codes of a quantum computer. This group of people spent every effort and used the computing power of 200 supercomputers around the world, only to barely arrive at a conclusion — if they wanted to crack Adam’s encryption codes, it would take these supercomputers three hundred years without break to crack them!

After seeing this conclusion, everyone was completely dumbfounded...

When the entire Federation’s higher-ups were in an uproar over Adam’s anomaly, Chen Chen was standing in the air at a height of a thousand meters. The wind roared, causing his clothes to flap in the wind, and below his feet was the world-famous capital of film and television.

At this moment, Chen Chen lowered his head and looked stoically at the brightly lit city under his feet.

Besides being the second-ranked city in Northern America, Los Angeles was famous for another reason — it was the place where Hollywood was located.

Since 1911, after the first film company was established in Los Angeles, the city had quickly become the world’s center for film and television, and it had also become a cultural export center in Northern America. The name Hollywood had attracted the attention of billions of people and guided the general direction of movie entertainment globally.

This was Chen Chen’s second time coming to this city.

The last time he came here, it was also for something film related. As he was sharp enough to notice that the movie Deadly Crisis: A Shocking Scheme had a strange causal connection with reality, he came to Hollywood specifically to investigate the director and crew who shot the film.

The investigation results naturally did not tell him much. This movie did not show any signs of being meddled in by mysterious forces.

Thus, Chen Chen came to a judgment at the time: some movies shot on the USB flash drive were likely to affect reality!

However, now that three years had passed, the original judgment had come true. Although the physical appearance of the characters in that movie was not the same as in reality, everything else was the same as the movie’s plot, whether in terms of name or other details as if the story of the movie was being faithfully reproduced.

Even at this time, Chen Chen had received a report that the young man named Keith had created AD-001 in his research and was now being ordered by Professor Irwin to secretly deliver the medicine to the founder of Osmond Biotech.

Chen Chen had obtained AD-001 as early as three years ago. Although the drug had not been disclosed to the public so far, it was already well known in God’s Forbidden Zone. More than a dozen family members of the Disciple Council who had been suffering from Alzheimer’s disease had even been treated and were 100% cured.

Now that this drug had been “produced” by others who would claim it as their own, of course, Chen Chen would not agree to this.

Not only Chen Chen would not agree but even the other Disciples’ families also would not agree.

At this time, Chen Chen had arrived at the headquarters of Osmond Biotech. This was a dilapidated office building with a history of at least 20 years. This building was also close to the suburbs and it was now nighttime, so the surroundings looked a little desolate.

Chen Chen landed in a free fall from a high altitude, but when he was about to reach the ground, his speed dropped suddenly. His entire body spun around, going from an upside-down position to an upright one. Then, he settled firmly on the rooftop of the office building.

All this time, no one saw this amazing scene.

A place like a rooftop was naturally deserted at usual times, not to mention that it was now late at night. Even so, Chen Chen saw that the bellows of several central air conditioners on the rooftop were still buzzing.

According to the positioning information given by Little X, Parker, the founder of Osmond, was now in his office.

Thus, Chen Chen walked directly to the stairwell on the roof and looked at the battered iron gate in front of him. Chen Chen sneered and pushed the door directly as he went in.

“Creak —!”

At once, the iron gate that had not been opened for a long time made a loud rubbing squeak as Chen Chen directly pushed it open.

When he walked down from the rooftop, Chen Chen saw that he was in a long and narrow corridor and there were offices on both sides of the corridor. Other than that, there was nothing worthy of attention.

In the row of offices on the right side of the corridor, the first one was what Chen Chen was looking for. He saw the words “Chairman and General Manager’s Office” on the signboard of the office.

Yes, Parker was not only the founder of Osmond Biotech but also the chairman of the board of directors and the general manager of the company.

To be able to continuously raise funds for a small biotechnology company until it reached its current size — although this could not be compared to a behemoth like Blacklight Biotechnology, it was already impressive. Parker must be quite talented.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen walked to the door and directly opened the wooden door of the office.

“What’s the matter, didn’t I tell you? You can solve this kind of thing by yourself!”

As the door was completely opened, Chen Chen heard a male voice coming from inside and Chen Chen’s eyes took in the scene in the office at the same time.

This office was not big although it seemed to have been formed by knocking down a wall and combining two offices into one. Still, the interior decoration was more tastefully chosen, composed of black and white. Whether it was the floor or the wall, the surfaces were all black and white, which created a unique sensation when one was standing in the space.

Opposite the door was a large mahogany writing desk. Opposite the writing desk was Parker whom Chen Chen was looking for. This was a middle-aged man with a special face. He seemed to be of part-Germanic lineage with a slim face, large bones, black hair, and brown eyes, with eyebrows parallel to his piercing eyes.

As Chen Chen opened the door and entered, the middle-aged man at the mahogany writing desk raised his head impatiently. He was just about to lose his temper, but he was taken aback when he saw Chen Chen.

“Who are you, where’s Michelle?”

“Do you mean your secretary?”

Chen Chen walked into the office as if he was familiar with the place. He looked around and said casually, “I didn’t see her at the door, maybe she’s already got off work.”

“Got off work?”

The middle-aged man became even more puzzled. He looked at Chen Chen suspiciously and asked again, “Then who are you and why are you here?”

“Me?”

Chen Chen could not help but grin. He pulled up a chair from the side and sat opposite the middle-aged man. At the same time, he smiled nastily. “I’m the boss of this company.”

“What sort of joke is this?”

The middle-aged man became furious. He picked up the phone next to him and yelled into the receiver, “Where are the security guards, what have they been doing? There’s an outsider in my office! Come over now!”

After a woman’s voice replied on the phone, the middle-aged man hung up huffily. He solemnly looked at Chen Chen. “I don’t know how you got here, but before the security guards toss you out on the street, you have half a minute to get out of my office, right now!”

“Why bother?”

Chen Chen did not feel disgruntled just because of the other party’s words. At this time, he said leisurely, “Mr. Parker, don’t get so worked up. There’s no exaggeration in what I said before. Now, let me formally introduce myself. My name is Chen Chen. You should’ve heard of me.”

“I don’t want to know who you are...”

The middle-aged man snorted coldly but before he finished speaking, he suddenly stopped and widened his eyes in an incredulous expression. “What, who did you say you are?”

Chen Chen did not answer. Instead, he spread his hands and said casually, “I know you have heard me. I don’t have to state my identity again. Mr. Parker, I’m here to make a deal with you.”

As he spoke, Chen Chen suddenly leaned forward and said in a low voice, “I want the remaining shares of Osmond owned by you as well as the management rights of your company!”

“Impossible!”

The middle-aged man immediately refused without thinking about it. At this time, there was already the sound of running footsteps approaching outside the door and it seemed that several people were coming here quickly.

After hearing this noise, the middle-aged man seemed to feel more secure and his voice became calmer. “I will not sell the shares in my hands, let alone transfer the management rights of the company I created to another person. I don’t want to know who you are, just leave now immediately, otherwise, I’ll ask security to carry you out!”

After Parker’s speech, the sound of footsteps had reached the door and at the same time, five strong men in suits rushed in.

“Get him out of here!”

Seeing the security guards who had arrived, Parker looked a little delighted and ordered at the same time.

“I’m afraid it won’t be as you wish...”

Chen Chen’s eyes gleamed with a flicker of mock regret. He snapped his fingers casually and in the next second, the five security guards who had just entered felt a stab of pain in the back of their heads as if someone had hit them hard in the back. After that, their eyes rolled over at the same time and they collapsed to the ground in unison!

This was the use of Field energy. Now that Chen Chen was able to rely on the power of Field to break through the sound barrier, the simple trick of knocking people out was even simpler.

Even so, to Parker, it was as if the young man in front of him had mastered some mysterious power. Chen Chen had just snapped his fingers and caused five people to fall to the ground at the same time!

Seeing this eerie scene, Parker could not help but gasp sharply. He could not sit still anymore and stood up with horror on his face while quickly backing away until he reached the window.

“There’s no point in escaping.”

Chen Chen leaned back in his chair and continued to persuade Parker unhurriedly. “The reason you’re unwilling to be acquired by me is that you’ve received news from Professor Irwin, right? That AD-001 was successfully developed?”

“You...” Hearing this, Parker felt as if he had been struck by a thunderbolt. He looked at Chen Chen in shock. “How could you possibly know this!”

“Of course, I’d know.”

Chen Chen waved his hand and said with a chuckle, “Not only do I know this but I also know that to prevent the newly developed AD-001 from falling into our hands, Professor Irwin specially ordered Keith to travel by road to cross 2,600 kilometers and deliver the drug to you personally. Are you imagining the endless flow of cash that will come pouring into your pockets?”

Parker opened his mouth when he heard the words and fell silent again.

Only this time, as Chen Chen exposed his secret, his original arrogant expression had wilted significantly.

“Why don’t you listen to my offer?”

Seeing that Parker did not respond, Chen Chen said directly, “I’m willing to buy all the shares in your hands at a price of one billion U.S.D. This price isn’t low. After all, after several rounds of financing, you only own 11% of the original shares.”

chapters.title: Chapter 470: Hidden Mastermind
chapters.body:
Chapter 470: Hidden Mastermind

Chapter 470: Hidden Mastermind

Blacklight Biotechnology currently owned 50% of Osmond’s shares and now it had increased by 11%, bringing it to 66%. As the legal owner of Blacklight Biotechnology, Chen Chen also naturally became the big boss of Osmond in the eyes of the law.

In one night, the entire company had entered a new dynasty but the employees of the company had been kept in the dark. Only Parker, after being forced to agree to all this, was slumped in his seat, his expression clearly more aged and strained.

Although he got the money, he had lost the future. His belief that was once full of ambitions to make the company bigger and stronger collapsed instantly. At this moment, Parker only felt that his heart was empty and it seemed that he was missing something.

After persuading the other party, Chen Chen did not stay on. Although Parker was pretty good at his job and Chen Chen had considered roping him in as a manager of a Blacklight Biotechnology branch office, Chen Chen still discarded this idea after thinking about it.

If he roped in Parker, this man would gradually wonder where Blacklight Biotechnology’s AD-001 came from and even discover that accursed movie. If he had a fertile imagination, he might even speculate that Chen Chen had some kind of method to foresee the future.

Moreover, Parker himself was not meant for the same path as Chen Chen.

Therefore, Chen Chen finally gave up on this notion.

After Chen Chen turned to leave the office and returned to the rooftop, he stood on the edge of the building and silently stared into the distance.

Everything was in place. Following this, he just had to wait for Keith to come into the story.

However, just when Chen Chen thought that everything was under control, Little X suddenly piped up in a somewhat anxious voice. “Oh no, Sir Godfather, something had happened in the Earth Federation Quantum Deity Laboratory!”

Chen Chen raised his eyes when he heard the words. “What’s the matter?”

“After I sent a prediction request to the quantum computer ‘Adam’, it took only half an hour to automatically generate a video but after I watched this video, it felt both familiar and unfamiliar!”

Little X’s voice was uncertain. “I’ve only felt something like this when it comes to memes!”

Chen Chen was shocked when he heard these words. He stood up straight. “You mean, ‘Adam’ created a meme by himself?”

“It’s not that!”

However, Little X explained, “I analyzed the code of the video itself and didn’t find anything related to memes. That is to say, the video isn’t problematic. The real problem isn’t the video itself but the content in the video!”

At this, Little X hesitated. “I’m familiar with the coding of memes. From this video, I’ve got a feeling similar to ‘This Man’, which means that this video is likely to be highly infectious. But I’m not sure for the time being how many ways the content of this video can spread and what will be the means of transmission...”

Chen Chen immediately grasped that the transmission of memes was ever-changing and not necessarily limited to one type. Although it was a video, meaning that it was certain that the transmission mode was visual communication, it did not mean that transmission would not occur if language was used to describe its content.

Still, at this point, Chen Chen suddenly looked in a certain direction in Los Angeles, which was the direction of the Herrank Warehouse District that Little X had mentioned in her previous report.

If the quantum computer predicted that it was a meme, did it mean that there was a memetic effect happening in the Herrank Warehouse District and those who had died by having their necks twisted were all killed by the meme?

However, everything related to memes was deadlocked in the Spire, so how could it appear outside here and in the strongest continent, Northern America, at that?

“What’s the content of the video, can you describe it?”

Chen Chen asked urgently.

“I dare not describe the specific content for the time being, but one thing I can say is that the main subject in the video is a statue, an... Angel statue covering its eyes...”

“An angel statue covering its eyes?”

Chen Chen muttered in puzzlement and then he seemed to have thought of something. He jerked up, showing a shocked expression. “It’s that angel pattern!”

At this moment, Chen Chen suddenly remembered that when he went to China’s Jiao Tong University for an investigation, he had been ambushed with an iron bed that seemed to have originated from the dimension of Silent Hill, which had almost killed him.

Although that ambush was ultimately foiled by fate, as the iron bed’s meme was unintentionally deciphered by a college student, an error still occurred after Chen Chen had transported the iron bed back to Namibia.

During the memetic detection experiment, Chen Chen not only failed to discern the iron bed’s function but instead caused a mural to appear next to the iron bed.

That was a mural painted in blood — on it was a woman curled up with her eyes covered and on the woman’s back, there was a pair of wings...

At that time, Chen Chen did not see the whole picture, only a part of it, but fortunately, that was the case. Once he saw the meme pattern completely, it was likely that he would be infected. Even if he died, he would not know what he died of.

At this time, following Little X’s reminder, Chen Chen immediately thought of something and he quickly asked, “Didn’t four Black Knights look directly at the meme before? How were they destroyed in the end?”

“I sent them to the steel mill.”

Little X seemed to understand what he was driving at and she quickly said, “It can’t be. After the chips were dug out of the four Black Knights, their bodies were all dissolved in the molten steel.”

“Dissolved?”

Chen Chen could not help looking suspicious. If they had been destroyed, why did the meme appear in Los Angeles again? Was it just a coincidence?

Even so, in the next second, Chen Chen dismissed the idea and asked again, “Are you sure? Then what of the pot containing the molten steel that dissolved the bodies of the four Black Knights, where did it go?”

“I’ll look it up now.”

Little X replied, paused for a few seconds, and then said again, “Sir Godfather, your guess isn’t wrong because I found that after the pot of molten steel had dissolved those four, it was cast as building materials and sold to Northern America. What’s more, the buyer is a construction company in Los Angeles...”

Hearing this, Chen Chen and Little X understood everything in a flash.

“As I thought.”

Chen Chen stared solemnly at the dark night ahead. In the suburbs of Los Angeles, there was an area full of warehouses, none other than the famous Herrank Warehouse District. “This batch of building materials was mixed up with the memetic effect. It was transported to Los Angeles along with the rest of the building materials. Then, when the materials were temporarily stacked in the Herrank Warehouse District, it broke out because certain conditions were met...”

“What a tricky meme.”

Little X’s tone was a bit distressed. “I didn’t expect that even after the bodies that might be infected with the meme had been destroyed, it can still spread. This memetic effect is too terrifying. In this case, I’m afraid that the Quantum Deity Laboratory is a goner...”

“You mean, in the Quantum Deity Laboratory, this video has been leaked?”

Chen Chen took a deep breath and only felt his scalp turn numb. He could not imagine what would happen if this meme appeared in reality...

“Yes, after all, ‘Adam’ itself is over there with them. I can’t prevent them from getting the answer. At most, I can only choose to encrypt the information, but...”

Little X’s voice was a little helpless. “Originally, ‘Adam’ had used encryption when predicting the Herrank Warehouse event. It stands to reason that the Earth Federation cannot get an effective answer. It’s a pity that the meme is unreasonable. After the video was generated, not only did I receive this video but the Earth Federation also got a copy. I originally thought that these encryption methods couldn’t be cracked by the Earth Federation, but they succeeded in decoding it. All the encryption methods became ineffective measures in the face of this meme!”

“In other words, at this time, everyone in the Quantum Deity Laboratory of the Earth Federation had seen that video?”

Chen Chen’s face turned even darker. At this moment, Zhao Shanhe’s face flashed in his mind and then Zhao Shanhe’s face changed and became the image of Professor Wang Xi. Chen Chen could not help but laugh in anger. “Great plan, what a truly great plan...”

“What do you mean...”

Little X sounded astonished.

“All this would be impossible if no one was planning it.”

Chen Chen’s expression became even colder and harsher. “The other party would destroy the entire human civilization indiscriminately to get the USB flash drive or to kill me. Isn’t this a great plan? What’s more terrifying is that the other party is probably not on Earth or even the same space-time and timeline where I’m in. Rather, they’re in another place that I can’t reach or discover...”

“Then what shall we do now?”

X could not help asking.

“Call off most of the Black Knights who are chasing Keith.”

Chen Chen said without thinking, “With Keith, just let everything happen as it will. These Black Knights are to go to the Herrank area, find out everything that’s going on, and see what the h*ll is killing people!”

As he spoke, Chen Chen hesitated a bit and finally said, “By the way, contact the President of Northern America and the Mayor of Los Angeles and ask them to immediately seal off the Herrank area to prevent the spread of the meme!”

“But they won’t listen to us...”

Little X hesitated.

“Then tell them what a meme is!”

Chen Chen said coldly, “Although the time isn’t quite right, I can no longer care about that. We’re just laying our cards on the table. We don’t necessarily need to be afraid of Northern America now.

“X, I’m relying on you here. You’ve never let me down in the past and the present as well. I hope it will be so in the future!”

“Yes!”

Little X immediately perked up when she heard this and her voice was loud and clear. “I understand, Sir Godfather, leave it to me!”

“Very good!”

Chen Chen smiled in relief and suddenly turned his head to look north. At this moment, the night breeze suddenly brushed by, causing Chen Chen to slightly narrow his eyes. There seemed to be an undercurrent churning in his gaze. “As for the Federation’s Quantum Deity Laboratory, I’ll take care of it.”

With that said, Chen Chen walked straight to the edge of the building but when he reached the edge, he did not stop and walked into thin air. However, the strange thing was that although there was a 100-meter-high void below his feet, he seemed to be walking on solid ground every step of the way!

At the same time, Chen Chen’s body was leaning forward slightly and the Field energy in his mind exploded like a spark plug. In the next second, Chen Chen’s body suddenly produced an extreme thrust, causing Chen Chen to race forward with unbelievable acceleration!

“Boom!”

A white mist formed behind Chen Chen but at this time, he had already appeared hundreds of meters away. After that, in another blink of an eye, Chen Chen disappeared completely!

After Chen Chen’s figure had vanished, the panes of glass in the Osmond building under his feet shattered instantaneously. In a flash, half of the building’s glass had exploded and the fragments scattered all over the place!

At this point, Chen Chen had flown away at an acceleration that exceeded human limits. This was no longer just thanks to the Field energy. Even Andrew in Chronicle would not dare to do this.

Andrew might have a stronger Field than Chen Chen’s, but his body was still that of an ordinary mortal.

Many people would think that one’s body would become stronger after being irradiated by the crystalline. The source of this rumor was a scene in the movie: Andrew had stabbed a fork into his cousin’s hand when he was playing tricks on his cousin, but his cousin’s hand was fine, yet the fork was bent, so those uninformed would think that their bodies became even tougher after being irradiated by the crystalline.

Nevertheless, this was a misunderstanding because at that point of the story, Andrew was testing out his Field. He later explained that the reason the fork would bend and his cousin’s hand remained fine was because he used his Field to shield his cousin’s hand. The reason he did this was just to show that one could have a higher degree of control over one’s Field and it had nothing to do with physical durability.

Chen Chen was different. At this time, Chen Chen was like a mixture of Alice and Andrew. He had both Alice’s physical fitness strengthened by the T-virus and Andrew’s Field power, so only he could accelerate to supersonic speed in half a second without worrying about his body being unable to bear the strain.

In the aviation field, there was a technical term called “overload” and the unit was represented by “G”.

It could also be called the gravitational acceleration experienced by an object on the surface of the earth, which could be expressed as: when standing on the ground and remaining motionless relative to the ground, the gravitational acceleration experienced by a person was 1G and what one would feel was one’s body weight.

However, during flight, the aircraft would often accelerate and the pilot would likewise undergo acceleration at the same time, so an acceleration would be generated. The greater the acceleration, the greater the acceleration overload. For example, if the pilot had attained an overload unit of 2G during flight, this was equivalent to the pilot’s weight doubling out of thin air.

Generally speaking, ordinary people could withstand an overload of 2-3G and those fighter pilots who were carefully selected for their physical fitness as well as having undergone professional training, could withstand overloads far beyond ordinary people, even reaching 5-7G. Some of the better ones could even withstand a 9G overload.

As for Chen Chen, accelerating from a standstill to the speed of sound, he had suffered several times the overload of a pilot and more than a hundred times that of an ordinary person. This would be impossible for Andrew in the movie. Even if he tried that, he would die of cerebral hemorrhage a few seconds later.

chapters.title: Chapter 471: Annihilation
chapters.body:
Chapter 471: Annihilation

Chapter 471: Annihilation

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

“It’s out!”

When they cracked the encryption code of “Adam”, those in the technical department could not help but cheer. The group of representatives from all continents, the “shield bearers”, gathered around the computer at once, waiting for the release of new information from “Adam”.

“What’s the matter, isn’t it encrypted information? Why was the technical department able to crack it?”

“Who knows, I heard that the technical department didn’t spend any effort at all, but this matter has something to do with the super-intelligent Adam. Maybe there’s some deep meaning behind all this.”

“This computer is amazing...”

Many “shield bearers” gathered in the conference room and continued to talk anxiously and excitedly, until the chairman of the Quantum Deity Conference spoke and everyone quieted down.

“I think you all know the reason for this emergency meeting.”

This was an old man from Europe. He only said excitedly, “I won’t waste your time. Now, as for the video information cracked by the technical department, please take a look...”

Following the old man’s words, the screen in front of everyone suddenly turned on and a video of only ten seconds was played in front of everyone.

However, to everyone’s disappointment, the image in the video was not some big secret but a statue of a woman with her back facing everyone, her head down, and her hands raised to cover her eyes.

The lines on the statue were smooth and the details were extremely delicate as if they were carefully crafted. What was more conspicuous was the pair of soft wings growing out the back of the statue.

Anyone who looked at it could understand at a glance that this statue was none other than an angel.

This video was only ten seconds long. It had ended before everyone could react. After watching this video, everyone glanced around at each other, feeling a great sense of disappointment.

What was this, a prank by “Adam”?

“That’s it?”

One of the “shield bearers” spread his hands and looked at the chairman of the Quantum Deity Conference. “Are you sure that the technical department didn’t switch out the video?”

“The technical department is loyal. What you suggest is impossible.”

The old man shook his head. “Perhaps ‘Adam’ wants to tell us a secret through this video.”

“I guess so...”

Shortly after, the meeting room became noisy again.

However, this group of people did not notice that, when they turned away and started arguing loudly, on the frozen screen, the statue also turned its head slowly...

...

At the same moment, in the Los Angeles City Hall, Mayor Parsons was rubbing his temples furiously. A thick stack of files was in front of him.

A few minutes ago, the chief of the police department in charge of Los Angeles public security had been given a thorough dressing down and he ran away from the city hall and fled Parsons’s angry roar.

“They are all pigs who are good for nothing. Not a single one is useful at this critical moment!”

At the thought of the horrible murders that had happened, Parsons was so enraged that he swept the reports off of the table, cursing in a low voice.

“Mr. Mayor, there’s probably a vicious murderer hidden in the Herrank District.”

The aide on his side reminded in a low voice. “What we should do now is to seal off the warehouse area of ??Herrank, get all the workers out, and conduct strict interrogation of everyone at the same time because there’s a high probability that the murderer is also still hidden in the warehouse area.”

“Would they be so stupid?”

Parsons looked at his aide with an icy face.

“It’s not like that, Mr. Mayor.”

The aide did not panic at all, but said solemnly, “Many people died at the hands of the murderer. This is beyond the scope of a vendetta. Therefore, I suspect that these killings are random killings, which means that the other side is taking pleasure in killing people. This kind of person’s mind has long been distorted and cannot be judged with common sense.”

Hearing this, Parsons started to think. What the aide said made sense.

“Of course, I’m just speculating. Anyway, we can just try this. We can also send the police to do a sweep of the Herrank Warehouse District. Even if we can’t catch our target, it’d be good if we can stop the murderer’s actions.”

The aide continued to persuade.

Hearing the words, Parsons finally nodded. “Then we’ll do as you say. We’ll seal off the Herrank Warehouse District and dispatch...”

Before he finished speaking, the computer on Parsons’ desk chimed and turned on by itself.

The two heard the sound and looked over at the same time. After seeing the computer turned on by itself, Parsons frowned. He stretched out his hand as if about to turn off the computer again but in the next second, a little girl’s crisp voice unexpectedly came out from the computer —

“Sir Parsons, I don’t recommend you do that.”

In the computer, the girl said in precise English, “If I were you, I’d directly close off the Herrank Warehouse District and wait for orders from higher authorities, instead of sending police to the Herrank Warehouse District to their deaths.”

“Who’s there!”

Hearing that the computer started to speak, both of them looked stunned. The aide exclaimed, “It’s a hacker, who is eavesdropping on our conversation!”

With that, the aide was ready to notify the guards outside but Parsons hesitated and finally stopped him. “Listen to what she has to say.”

Hearing Parsons’ order, the staff glanced at the computer with some anxiety.

The computer at this time no longer had its usual Windows interface. Instead, there was a pure black screen and the screen had a red logo design, which contained specifically three lowercase “i”s.

The three “i” letters were laid out in a circle and there were two arcs between each pair, forming a radial ring with a structure that was like the sign of nuclear radiation.

Below the logo, there was a line of letters:

Infohazard.

This meant “information hazard”.

“Sir Parsons, I don’t recommend that you send the police to the Herrank District.”

At this time, the little girl’s voice continued to persuade. “Because it wouldn’t help and it may cause more intense memetic infections.”

Hearing this, Parsons frowned. He waved his hand slightly and the aide seemed to understand this gesture immediately, hurriedly backing out as if he was about to get someone else.

“Memetic infection, what is that?”

Parsons’ voice continued to remain cool and aloof, without mentioning what he had just done.

“Memes belong to a subject that hasn’t yet been discovered by humans.”

The little girl’s voice whispered, “I believe you should know that the essence of living things is continuation and continuation is the fundamental purpose of life. The reason life can go on is because of the continuation of genes, which undergo inheritance, mutation, and so on. They support the basic structure of life and store all the information about the race, blood type, gestation, growth, and apoptosis of life.

“At the same time, in addition to heredity, genes also have the characteristics of mutation and selection. Otherwise, our current biological world wouldn’t be this ever-changing. But did you know that in addition to genes, there’s something else in the world that has the three characteristics of transmission, selection, and mutation?”

“What is it?”

Hearing the question from the voice on the computer, Parsons’ expression flickered and he asked calmly.

“It’s a meme.”

The little girl continued. “A meme is like a special kind of information that is spread and mutated through human cognition. It can transform human cognition of things, change the structure of matter, and even change the rules of reality. A single malicious meme and even the entire Earth Federation will be destroyed because of it. What is happening now in the Herrank Warehouse District is a serious memetic effect.”

Hearing this, Parsons’ expression shifted and he cracked a disdainful smile. “Is this an ‘information hazard’ then? So, what should we do?”

“My suggestion is to lock down the Herrank Warehouse District and at the same time, isolate all those who have entered the area to prevent the spread of the meme.”

As she spoke, the little girl suddenly changed her tone. “But it seems that you don’t believe what I said, otherwise you wouldn’t send your aide to mobilize the Los Angeles network reconnaissance team to track down my IP.”

Parsons’ expression changed. “So, you think I’d believe a hacker who only deals in half-truths and wouldn’t even show themselves?”

“You can only follow my advice.”

The little girl’s voice continued to say without emotion, “Otherwise, all of you will die.”

“Would we be killed by the so-called meme you mentioned?”

Parsons turned cold, not susceptible to the threats from the computer.

“No, but because I’ve sent the Black Knights to install fifty plastic bombs in the city hall.”

The voice continued to say without any sentiment, “If you don’t want to listen to me, then the entire city hall will collapse into ruins in an instant. I’m not kidding you.”

“You!”

After hearing these words, Parsons’ expression changed. He was about to erupt in fury but right then, the phone on his desk rang without warning.

Parsons turned to the sound and saw that the caller information on the phone turned out to be the number of the President’s office in the White House.

When Parsons picked it up and heard the order he was given, his face turned even gloomier because the White House only sent him one order:

Seal off the Herrank Warehouse District!

...

When Little X contacted the Northern American officials, Chen Chen was crossing multiple regions in Northern America at an absurd speed. In less than an hour, he had traversed 1,500 kilometers and arrived at the famous Yellowstone Park.

Surrounded by the Rocky Mountains on all sides, this was known as the “spine” of Northern America. It stretched from north to south for more than 4,800 kilometers. By this time, even Chen Chen’s forehead was slightly sweaty.

Looking for a building in such a vast area of ??mountains was like finding a needle in a haystack. Moreover, it was still dark. If Chen Chen was asked to search for it by himself, it would likely take him several months to find the headquarters of the Earth Federation Quantum Deity Laboratory.

Nonetheless, it was a good thing he had Little X’s help in all this. In addition, day and night had little effect on Chen Chen’s vision. Soon, Chen Chen arrived in the unnamed forest where the laboratory was located.

After arriving, Chen Chen began to lower his flight altitude. After all, as the top secret of the entire Earth Federation, this place was heavily guarded. If he flew too high, it was easy for him to be detected by radar.

After lowering his altitude, Chen Chen broke out from the supersonic state and flew at a low speed for ten minutes. After flying over several checkpoints and a military base, he finally found the goal of his trip — Quantum Deity Laboratory that could shield all radiation.

The Quantum Deity Laboratory was a square structure as a whole, towering in the endless swathe of forest, but it was very inconspicuous because of the camouflage applied to its surface. It was only thanks to Chen Chen’s eyesight that it could still be easily seen.

When Chen Chen approached the laboratory, his communication device also began to receive strong interference. As he flew closer and closer, the interference continued to intensify and he was finally completely disconnected from Little X.

“It’s only been an hour since the meme was leaked. I hope I’m on time.”

As Chen Chen continued to descend, he soon came to a space above the laboratory but after seeing the scene on the ground, Chen Chen’s heart suddenly sank.

There was already a mess outside the gates of the laboratory. Dozens of guns were discarded on the ground and the regional flags of all continents were scattered all over the place. Several corpses could be seen even on the road.

Among these corpses were soldiers in military uniforms, researchers in white coats, and “shield bearers” in suits and leather shoes.

However, the necks of these corpses were all twisted past three hundred and sixty degrees, like long twisted doughnuts.

The horror before death and the pressure from the twisting of the neck were shown in the eyes of the corpses. All the corpses had their eyes wide open. It seemed that the eyeballs would fall from the sockets any time now and it looked terrifying.

However, this did not mean much to Chen Chen. He just looked gravely in the direction of the laboratory but saw that despite being the ultimate secret of the Earth Federation, this Quantum Deity Laboratory even had its doors thrown wide open. By now, the government dignitaries of various countries and also the researchers had abandoned the place like rats deserting a sinking ship, having fled into the depths of the forest one after another.

Seeing this scene, Chen Chen knew that he was too late and the meme had completely spread and infected the core center of the Earth Federation.

However, Chen Chen did not hurry into the forest to pursue them. At this point of the matter, the spread of the meme was inevitable. Currently, he focused on this most mysterious laboratory in the entire Federation. Perhaps there may be some clues related to the meme inside.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen went straight down the passage and walked into the laboratory.

The doors of the laboratory were kept open as if welcoming guests. As soon as he entered the door, Chen Chen saw a long, narrow, and deep corridor. On both sides of the corridor were the police and medical rooms. Now all that remained were data sheets scattered on the ground and bloody handprints on the door.

Chen Chen did not waste his time here. He knew about the layout of this place from Little X. At this time, he walked by without stopping and went straight to the core of the laboratory, where the single most important object of the entire Earth Federation was located.

chapters.title: Chapter 472: Eyes Closed
chapters.body:
Chapter 472: Eyes Closed

Chapter 472: Eyes Closed

What surprised Chen Chen was that there was an inexplicably oppressive atmosphere in the laboratory.

As he stepped in, he did not feel it at first but as he gradually went in deeper, a cold aura slowly enveloped him. In front of him, there was a corridor with no end in sight. On both sides of the corridor were rows of doors. The curving shape of the corridor interrupted Chen Chen’s line of sight, leaving Chen Chen trapped in a semi-enclosed space.

This feeling...

Chen Chen frowned. This feeling was similar to what he felt, trapped in the “Otherworld” when he went to Jiao Tong University. There was an unnamable sense of being stifled but the only difference was that the oppressive air was now much stronger. Moreover, it was not the Otherworld meme that was enveloping the laboratory, otherwise, the group of scientists and politicians would not be able to escape from this place.

At this time, Chen Chen’s Field energy had encased him heavily as if he was wearing invisible armor. However, noticing the oppressive aura of the laboratory, Chen Chen was still highly vigilant and his mind was tightly strung.

Every four or five meters, there was a light above him to provide illumination but it seemed that due to insufficient voltage, the light was very dim, like narrow boundaries that could only illuminate each section of the corridor. In between two sections of lighting, there was a special dark area.

At this time, Chen Chen was walking in the quiet metal corridor. Every time he took a step, a hollow echo reverberated, highlighting the peculiar emptiness of the corridor. Moreover, there were various objects scattered messily on the ground. There were data sheets as well as various experimental equipment. It was as if a crisis had erupted in the laboratory during the previous emergency evacuation, so the people who had been evacuating in an orderly manner at first suddenly discarded the information in their hands, screaming as they ran for their lives.

Chen Chen continued to walk forward. This time, he walked about a hundred meters and crossed the many barriers as before. At this moment, a set of heavy double doors appeared in front of his eyes.

This door was completely transparent. Through the thick glass, he could see the dazzling light emitting from behind the door, which was much brighter than the lighting in the corridor.

Behind the door, there was a cliff-like drop and a completely suspended laboratory in the depths.

Chen Chen remembered that the shape of the Quantum Computer Laboratory was like a square within a square, with a completely enclosed space in the middle, where the air was drained to maintain a vacuum state. The laboratory where the quantum computer was stored was suspended in mid-air only by electromagnetic force.

The advantage of this was that it isolated all the electromagnetic radiation of the universe and it was as clean as when the universe had not yet been born.

At this time, as Chen Chen looked around, he could see an elevator suspended on the other side of the door emerging in the vacuum chamber. This elevator was the only channel leading to the quantum computer but this channel was not on Chen Chen’s side, but the side of the internal Quantum Deity Laboratory.

Chen Chen had used the quantum device from the movie Spectral to manipulate a person into entering the laboratory, so he understood that this situation meant that someone was already in the Quantum Deity Laboratory.

Sure enough, as Chen Chen continued to look, he immediately saw an old man standing on the other side of the laboratory. He and Chen Chen stared at each other through the glass wall and the old man seemed gripped by disbelief.

He did not understand how Chen Chen got in here.

Chen Chen tried to knock on the glass wall in front of him but found that there was only a slow, dull reverberation. No sound could pass through, so he immediately gave up on asking the old man about the matter.

Chen Chen did not know this old man but his suit and leather dress shoes indicated that he should be a new “shield bearer”. Based on his race, he was probably a representative of Northern America and Europe.

Still, after seeing Chen Chen, the old man began to bang on the glass door in front of him and at the same time, he kept yelling as if he wanted to tell Chen Chen something.

Chen Chen could not quite hear him, so he tried to read the man’s lips, but found that the man was just saying “hey” over and over without any valuable information at all. Thus, Chen Chen thought about it. He turned his head and looked at an unmarked door next to him, turned around, and cautiously walked in.

As Chen Chen pushed the door and entered, a room with a large number of surveillance monitors appeared before his eyes. The furnishings inside the door were all toppled and disordered papers were scattered all over the floor. At the very front, there was a huge electronic platform inlaid into the wall.

It could be seen from the furnishings here that this place must have been equivalent to a command room.

Chen Chen swept his Field across the room. After concluding that there was no unusual danger, he looked down and started searching. Soon, he found a few pages of A4 paper and a black pen.

Holding the paper and pen, Chen Chen walked out of the room again. Sure enough, the old man was still leaning against the door. Seeing this, Chen Chen smiled. He took out the pen and wrote “What happened here?” in English.

Seeing Chen Chen’s behavior, the old man wore an indescribable expression. He suddenly became extremely agitated. At the same time, he pointed at Chen Chen animatedly and shouted.

From his lips, Chen Chen could only read one word — danger!

Just when Chen Chen was a little bewildered, suddenly, there was a “bzz bzz” sound from the top of his head, coming from a short circuit. The lights began to flicker violently in the corridor where he stood.

What was going on?

Chen Chen’s heart clenched and he watched the light flickering continuously as it kept crackling loudly. In its feeble glimmering, it was as if something unseen was slowly making its attack!

At this moment, Chen Chen instinctively developed an indescribable sense of crisis and then a special numbness hit him from behind. He could not define what it was like. His Field was detecting nothing behind him but that feeling kept on approaching, causing Chen Chen’s hair to stand up on end!

This was a kind of instinctive fear for his own life!

At this second, Chen Chen suddenly realized that the old man might not be pointing at himself but behind him!

In the next second, Chen Chen whipped his head back like lightning!

“Swish!”

In Chen Chen’s swift glimpse, he unexpectedly saw that at the boundary between the two lights, a dark shadow had appeared vaguely!

This black shadow was only ten meters away from Chen Chen. It stood between the shadows of the two lights, so Chen Chen could not see it at all. However, as Chen Chen’s Field power stretched over, he found that the shadowy figure was none other than a humanoid statue with two wings...

This statue held up its hands as if it had been crying but its posture indicated that it had just finished crying. Its hands were just leaving its face, revealing a pair of enormous eyes and a very lifelike face.

“What the h*ll?”

Chen Chen could not help but narrow his eyes.

The light above his head was still flickering but because the Quantum Deity Laboratory behind him seemed to use another electrical system, so the same power failure did not occur. The light shone through the two transparent glass doors. Even if the hallway lights above his head were completely extinguished, Chen Chen would not be in complete darkness.

However, now was not the time to care about the overhead lights because, in Chen Chen’s memory, he could be sure that when he came over, this statue was completely non-existent...

Not only that but he could also be sure that when he had his back turned, there was no such statue in the Field energy’s detection as if this statue appeared the moment Chen Chen turned around.

What shocked Chen Chen even more was that the moment he turned around, the crisis instinct that originated from his body disappeared without any warning. When he looked at the statue in front of him, it was as if the other party was just a statue, nothing unusual at all.

Chen Chen subconsciously took a deep breath and recalled everything from before. As he had taken the initiative to come to the Quantum Deity Laboratory to face all this, Little X had still told him about the content predicted by the quantum computer. What Adam had predicted was a ten-second video file and in the file was an angel statue, the same as the statue in front of Chen Chen at this time...

Coupled with the mural of the crying woman that appeared out of thin air when he was doing memetic experiments on the Otherworld, Chen Chen could already be sure that he was dealing with the meme directly now, one with a completely unknown memetic effect.

In that case, how was this meme transmitted?

Chen Chen did not rush forward rashly. He just stood still and stared at the statue motionlessly. However, to be cautious, he only stared at the statue’s foot and not its entire body because the previous memetic patterns had similar properties, whereby if one saw the picture of the meme in full, even if unintentionally, one would get infected.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen’s heart jolted. He once again used his Field energy, trying to push the opponent back.

“Crunch...”

With a sound of stone rubbing against the metal tiles, the statue that was not too far from him was pushed back.

Chen Chen’s Field energy could extend as far as 25 meters and because the corridor was curved, Chen Chen’s line of sight only covered a distance of 20 meters. Therefore, Chen Chen tried to push the statue out of the corridor, out of his line of sight.

However, Chen Chen would not consider destroying this opponent first without clarifying what it was. After all, he did not know the nature of the meme. If by chance destroying the statue would trigger the meme’s infectious ability, then Chen Chen would have brought death upon himself.

He had to admit that Field energy was useful. As Chen Chen kept pushing the opponent away from the corridor, the opponent gradually disappeared in front of him. However, just when Chen Chen was wondering if this statue was so easily dealt with, his Field energy suddenly detected nothing but a vacuum!

After he had pushed the statue completely out of his line of sight, it disappeared into thin air even under the shroud of Field energy...

Chen Chen could not help but raise his eyebrows.

Why did the statue disappear even while within the Field at the same time when it was out of sight?

Chen Chen did not run up to check but stared at the end of the corridor with a pensive look.

By now, it was already a certain fact that the statue could move and kill people. Those whose necks were broken were likely to have died at its hands.

That being the case, why did it not step up and attack him when he saw it?

Not only that but he could also use his Field to push it out of the corridor without facing any resistance. It was only until it disappeared from his line of sight that it disappeared from the Field energy’s detection simultaneously?

At this moment, Chen Chen recalled the previous details again and then his pupils shrank as if he had thought of something!

Could that really be the case?

At the same time that a certain thought came into his mind, Chen Chen took a deep breath, and he slowly backed away until he was pressed tightly against the glass wall behind him while he closed his eyes.

In the instant he closed his eyes, Chen Chen opened them quickly and vigilantly, but as he looked ahead, nothing had happened in the corridor in front of him...

Could it be that the statue saw that he was a difficult target, so it left?

Chen Chen shook his head. He knew it was impossible. After all, memes were not alive. They had no thoughts or consciousness. They were like the wind, thunder, and rain, a phenomenon like any other, only memes were relatively rare.

Since it could not have left, then there must be another reason.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen looked behind him but saw that, on the other side of the glass door, the old man was staring at him in amazement. Seeing that Chen Chen had turned around, he knocked on the door with excitement.

Seeing this, Chen Chen made a reassuring and soothing gesture, indicating that someone would come to rescue him later. Then, Chen Chen walked deep into the corridor without looking back.

This time, Chen Chen had only walked less than ten meters before he once again saw the statue at the end of the corridor’s curve.

At this time, the statue had changed its posture. It used to have its hands raised but now it was standing upright with its arms hanging straight down while its eyes were fixed in the direction of Chen Chen’s arrival.

Chen Chen made this out in the dim residual light, so he could only see the statue’s general position. Still, Chen Chen found that when he could see the opponent, the opponent also showed up in his Field at the same time.

This was a very discomfiting feeling. In a special sense, Field energy was even sharper than Chen Chen’s vision. It could observe behind Chen Chen as well as the blind spots that Chen Chen’s vision could not cover.

However, faced with this peculiar statue, his Field energy seemed to be at a loss.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen had gradually realized something. He pushed the statue toward the depths of the corridor again using his Field, letting the opponent go out of his sight.

As expected, when the statue was out of sight again, his Field lost its sense of the statue at the same time.

It was as if the statue disappeared into thin air...

Seeing this, Chen Chen took a deep breath again. His heart was pounding wildly and unusual anxiety slowly gripped him. He could not tell what kind of feeling it was. It was as if he was an extreme sports enthusiast and now, he was preparing to carry out a life-and-death challenge.

In the next second, Chen Chen resisted the pressure and slowly closed his eyes.

The moment he closed his eyes, Chen Chen suddenly felt an acute sense of danger. Although his Field energy was still picking up nothing, the feeling of surging adrenaline made Chen Chen reluctant to keep his eyes closed for a second longer. At almost the same time he closed his eyes, Chen Chen opened them again!

The instant his eyes flew open, Chen Chen immediately saw that the angel statue had appeared in front of him and this time, it was one step closer, less than five meters away!

Not only that, the statue’s expression and position at this time had become very different from before. Its arms were extended forward like an evil spirit and a sinister grimace was stretched across its face. Its entire body was leaning forward as if it would pounce right in front of Chen Chen in the next second!

chapters.title: Chapter 473: Don’T Blink!
chapters.body:
Chapter 473: Don’T Blink!

Chapter 473: Don’t Blink!

Chen Chen’s pupils shrunk as he stalked the nearby statue from the corner of his eye. Instead of retreating backward, he displayed a look of intrigue.

‘I see...’

This time, instead of choosing to move the statue or back away from it, he started to walk around it like a tourist examining a curious artifact. He candidly took a lap around the statue, which remained completely idle even after he had finished the lap.

Chen Chen had completely fine-tuned his control over Field and the limitations of his body at this point. The moment he felt the tingling sensation in his eyes, he quickly returned to his original position and pushed the statue out of his vision range using Field.

“Creak...”

The harsh rubbing sound rang in his ears.

After the statue disappeared from the range of Chen Chen’s Field, he opened his eyes again. When he blinked again, the statue suddenly appeared again less than five meters away from him, just as expected.

Was this how the statue repositioned itself?

Chen Chen displayed a look of perplexion. He started to piece together everything he knew at this point.

The first time he encountered the statue, he had his back facing the statue and the instant he turned around to face it, yet another statue appeared right behind him. At the time, he was unable to sense anything with Field.

It should be common knowledge that statues were unable to move around on their own. Since they were able to sneak past the detection of Field and appear behind him out of thin air, they must not be any ordinary statues but something else instead...

However, when Chen Chen fixed his attention on the statues, they became completely idle and cemented to their original spot like ordinary statues.

Even when Chen Chen used Field to observe them, the statues still refused to give away what was off with them. In other words, the statue seemed to only exist in the form of regular statues at certain points...

Whenever Chen Chen moved the statues out of his vision range using Field as a safety measure, they seemed to vanish into thin air in the range of Field. As perplexing as it was, it did prove one thing and that was – when the statue was out of his regular field of vision, even Field could not detect it!

At the end of the day, this was a mere hypothesis. To prove this hypothesis, Chen Chen slipped away from the field of vision of the elderly man and went ten meters further down the corridor. This way, the next experiment he was about to conduct should not be disturbed by any external factors.

After that, to prove the validity of his previous hypothesis, Chen Chen shut his eyes and opened them again. As expected, the statue drew closer to him again while he had his eyes closed and immediately froze in place when he opened them again. Another notable difference was that its posture had changed...

With this, Chen Chen had figured out a crucial element concerning the behavior of this statue. It was a vision.

At this point, it was clear that this statue was no ordinary statue but a unique memetic entity instead. Unlike regular memes, it assumed a very unique form, which was what Chen Chen was looking at now.

There was one key characteristic that separated this statue from ordinary statues. When observed by a human eye, it turned into a regular, immobile statue. However, whenever the person looking at it blinked, turned away from it or did anything that averted gaze from it, the statue came to life. The nature of its movement could not be observed even with Field.

This was not the first time Chen Chen noticed something of this variant. Previously, when he was dealing with the “Thud” meme inside the Gantz dimension, he had a similar experience with the white figure. The white figure displayed similar characteristics as well, despite being observable to the naked eye. However, it was completely invisible to Field...

This suggested that memetic entities, at their root, were merely a concept and concepts were abstract and intangible.

Going by this rhetoric, everything that Chen Chen was facing suddenly made sense.

In that case, what would happen if the statue collided with him?

Chen Chen glanced at the corpse in the corner whose neck was twisted into what seemed like misshaped branches. It was self-explanatory.

Another thing that Chen Chen confirmed at this point was that the statues did not actively turn into idle statues. It was a passive behavior triggered by certain conditions. It only turned into statues when observed by humans or in other words, when receiving direct human attention. This observation was similar to the famous double-slit experiment.

When placed under direct observation, electrons were susceptible to altering their patterns but in an unmonitored environment, there were countless existing possibilities. The result was only certain when an observer turned to look at it.

This seemed to be the characteristics which this statue Chen Chen was facing equipped as well. Under regular human observation, it was an ordinary statue. It was only when the observer stopped looking at it when it transformed into a lethal entity that snapped off people’s necks.

Additionally, it seemed to possess the ability to disrupt electric currents. This was derived from the fact that the light bulb above Chen Chen was flickering continuously. The only reason the room was not plunged into darkness yet was that there were plenty of light bulbs above Chen Chen and they all flickered at different intervals.

Chen Chen suspected that the moment the corridor was plunged into complete darkness was when the statue would no longer be bound by its limitations...

These speculations raced past Chen Chen’s mind and once he had figured everything out, he squinted his eyes again and pushed the statue away from him. However, instead of only nudging it out of his field of vision, he concentrated his Field into the form of a giant hammer when the statue went around the corner of the corridor and slammed it into the ground ferociously!

Since he had a firm understanding of a part of the statue’s nature, it was time to attempt to destroy it.

Since Chen Chen had absorbed all of the remaining crystalline fragments, the potential and intensity of his Field had become comparable to Andrew. Even if Chen Chen may not be able to pull off something like flipping around dozens of police cars just like Andrew did near the ending of the movie, destroying several statues was as easy as lifting a finger. Even if these statues were forged with steel, Chen Chen could still tear them apart using Field!

Chen Chen’s Field smashed into the statue in the form of a gigantic and invisible hammer. With a devastating blow, the statue was blasted into smithereens that scattered across all directions!

Even the metal plate supporting the statue from under it caved in as well. The ground itself emitted a sharp screech of metal being bent as a shallow pit nearly one meter deep appeared was formed!

Chen Chen was taken aback when he saw the statue crushed into powder. He was initially expecting this approach to fail. He had never expected the statue to shatter into pieces as a regular statue would.

“Impossible...”

Chen Chen could not hold back a disconcerted frown when he saw this. He refused to believe that this would be settled so effortlessly. After he considered for a moment, he closed his eyes and snapped them open again quickly!

Chen Chen could not believe what he was seeing when he opened his eyes!

The moment he opened his eyes, the statue that should have been blown into dust had pieced itself back to its original shape and appeared right in front of him!

Was it indestructible?

Chen Chen discarded the idea of destroying this statue when he saw it appearing again. He kept his eyes on the statue while moving past it and returned to the Quantum Deity Laboratory walking backward.

Chen Chen specifically came out here to prevent the meme from spreading but since the members of the research team were either dead or had already escaped, there was no reason for him to linger any longer.

After leaving the laboratory, Chen Chen even made sure to shut the door of the laboratory before he turned around.

What Chen Chen saw when he turned around startled him!

Chen Chen saw what seemed to be countless statues with their eyes covered spread across the area outside the laboratory. Out of nowhere, some of them appeared hidden inside the forest while some were out in the open!

Cold sweat started to trickle down Chen Chen’s forehead.

“Why are there so many of them?”

From the corner of his eyes, Chen Chen saw the dead bodies still lying on the ground. This meant that these statues were not transformed from the bodies on the ground but were born in other methods...

“Was it the video?”

Chen Chen had the faint impression that perhaps the video created by Adam was the source.

With this in mind, Chen Chen immediately opened the door to the laboratory and retreated inside as quickly as he could, and promptly shut the door behind him as soon as he entered!

“Swoosh!”

The moment Chen Chen turned around, a pair of razor-sharp claws appeared in front of him. The statue inside the laboratory had snuck up to him without his noticing. If not for Chen Chen’s supreme reflexes, he would have had his neck snapped in half!

Chen Chen stopped lolling around. He immediately moved past the statue and went down the corridor while ensuring he had his eyes on the statue at all times. When he arrived at the end of the corridor, he opened the door using Field and stepped through the door.

Several minutes later, Chen Chen had arrived at the further end of the corridor, which was where the electromagnetic levitation elevator that led to the quantum computer was located. The old man who was hiding in the quantum computer room immediately shouted something upon Chen Chen’s arrival.

The vacuum partition walled off by a piece of glass between the two laboratories muffled everything the old man said.

Chen Chen only glanced at this person for a brief instant and proceeded to ignore him. The reason Chen Chen came was to borrow this person’s sight since two people’s sight was undoubtedly better than only one.

It seemed like since there were two people present this time, the statue did not appear at any point along the corridor. Chen Chen waited for about ten minutes before he continued heading forward.

As soon as Chen Chen turned a corner, he saw the statues pursuing him again, just as he expected. Instead of just the lone statue, there were nearly thirty of them this time, each one of them had their eyes covered and crowded the corridor. It seemed as if they planned to trap Chen Chen inside this space.

Unbeknownst to them, this was exactly what Chen Chen had intended as well. Without sparing a moment of hesitation, Chen Chen unleashed his Field!

In an instant, the statues were blown apart as if they had been hit by a nuclear bomb. All of them were blasted into smithereens in a matter of seconds!

As soon as the statues were destroyed, Chen Chen took large strides forward and stepped through their ashes. Chen Chen repeated this step with every corner until he made it out of the laboratory. As soon he stepped out of the laboratory, he shut the door behind him to keep the statues trapped inside there.

After making it out, Chen Chen cautiously turned to the dense forest again. This time, he finally got to have peace of mind since there were no signs of any statues hidden in the forest.

After flying a certain distance above the Quantum Deity Laboratory, the communication device finally picked up a signal again. Little X’s voice came through immediately. “Sir Godfather, what’s the situation over there? The Northern American troops from the nearest military base are dispatched and currently on their way to the laboratory for rescue.”

“Tell them they can proceed with their plan. There are probably a good few who ran into the deeper parts of the forest. Make sure to quarantine them after rescuing them.”

Chen Chen looked below him as he said this and it was just as he said, several people were trudging through the forest. There was no telling if these people had been infected by the statue meme.

“Quarantine? Alright, I’ve informed them and told them about the threat of the memetic force.”

Little X answered promptly.

“While you’re at it, tell them there are nearly thirty of these murder statues inside the laboratory.”

Chen Chen added. “These statues possess extremely odd characteristics. When put under direct observation, they remain as ordinary statues. However, as soon as you take your eyes away from them, they immediately charge at you stealthily from behind your back at extreme speed to break your neck!”

As soon as the attentive Chen Chen mentioned this, he suddenly paused because he seemed to have realized something. It appeared that whenever he spotted the statues, they had their eyes covered. This applied to both the statues inside the laboratory and the ones outside, hidden in the forest...

However, the first statue that Chen Chen saw rested its arms that were covering its eyes and did not cover them again. It remained in this position until it came inside the laboratory and appeared on the corridor with the other statues.

When Chen Chen saw the group of statues in the corridor, he saw that all of them assumed the same position of keeping their eyes covered. Even the one that uncovered its eyes previously kept them covered again. This raised the question. What was it that made the statue behave differently when alone as opposed to when in a large group?

Unless...

Chen Chen thought for a moment but did not state his speculation out loud. Instead, he only noted to Little X. “As it is, it’s best to have the soldiers split into groups of three so they can take turns and focus their attention on a single target at all times. They can put these statues into a sturdy container. I don’t know if there exists any container strong enough to keep them enclosed just yet...

“Also, inform them to not watch the prophecy video created by the quantum computer ‘Adam’ no matter what. That video is the infection source of the meme.”

chapters.title: Chapter 474: Eyes
chapters.body:
Chapter 474: Eyes

Chapter 474: Eyes

“Remember, don’t blink!”

“You’re dead meat if you blink!”

“Don’t avert your gaze!”

“Keep your eyes on it, don’t get distracted!”

“Don’t blink! Don’t blink no matter what...”

“Everyone, I wish you good luck...”

...

Inside the office of the mayor located in the city hall, Parsons watched the “Response Protocol” sent by the hacker. The same video was also sent to plenty of confidential departments in Northern America. It included the picture of the statue, detailed information of its behavior, and its method of claiming its victims.

“Statues that broke people’s necks? There are all kinds of strange happenings these days...”

Parsons turned off the video and seemed to not take the entire thing too seriously. He turned to Mu Liao and asked, “Has the police sealed off the Herrank Warehouse District?”

“It’s already sealed off.”

Mu Liao answered promptly, “Sir Mayor, our men have entered the warehouse district and are searching for these murder statues following the protocols stated in this video. Based on Blacklight Biotechnology’s predictions, there should be four statues lying in the Herrank district.”

“Ridiculous...”

Parsons shook his head, feeling speechless. If not for the White House constantly sending notices, he would never heed the advice of the hackers himself.

Since when did the great Northern American government listen to some foreign forces whispering in their ears?

Parsons was largely dismissive of this event. Suddenly, there was a knock on the door to his office.

“Come in.”

Parsons turned to the door and saw another one of his staff coming inside with a hard drive in hand. “Sir Mayor, we’ve ordered people to go through the surveillance footage in the Herrank Warehouse District, as according to your instructions. We’ve successfully found the statues you described. It’s inside this hard drive now.”

“What took you so long?”

Parsons frowned with mild irritation.

“I’m very sorry, the surveillance in the Herrank Warehouse District isn’t connected to the Internet so we had to head to the area ourselves and collect all of the footage manually to look for the culprit for this incident. Before we could pinpoint a specific culprit, we received orders that you wanted to look for the statues...”

The reprimanded staff answered dejectedly.

“Alright, work faster next time.”

Parsons finally got up from this seat and took the hard drive with faint interest before plugging it into his computer.

Parsons seemed to have thought of something before he played the video. He proceeded to turn to Mu Liao, “Why don’t you head out first, close the door for me.”

“Alright.”

Mu Liao nodded and exited the mayor’s office with the other staff that delivered the hard drive.

After closing the office door behind him, Mu Liao chatted with the staff, “Mr. Robinson, are the technical department busy recently?”

“It’s alright, things are getting better.”

The staff answered plaintively, “Looks like there’s going to be a lot of work to do tonight though.”

“Hahaha, there’s no helping it, I suppose.”

Mu Liao clapped Robinson on his shoulders and asked curiously, “Where did you buy these lenses?”

“Lenses? I’m not wearing any lenses...”

Robinson was slightly startled by this and proceeded to rub his eyes. “I think it’s just my eyes drying up which makes them appear redder than usual? I’ve been looking at the surveillance footage for a while.”

Mu Liao did not think too much of it and only shrugged. The two split up after turning a corner to work on their respective tasks.

Inside the mayor’s office, after asking Mu Liao to get out, Parsons moved the cursor on his computer.

Just as he was about to play the video, he suddenly thought about the hacker who seemed to have eyes everywhere. With this in mind, he disconnected the computer from the network.

This way, he would not have to worry about the hacker stealing this video. It was time to settle everything himself.

The operations in the Herrank Warehouse District were severely outdated, so most of the areas did not have proper surveillance, which included most of the warehouse interior.

There were some surveillance cameras installed along the roads of the warehouse district more than a decade ago. Most of them were damaged or malfunctioned after years of weathering the rain without proper maintenance. Most of them were hardly functioning at all anymore.
