While speaking, Secretary Lin secretly warned, “Remember, talk less and observe more at the meeting. You must remember every detail in the meeting. This is very important to us, understand?”

“Yes!”

Seeing this, Yu Qi naturally had no choice of shirking his duties. He nodded repeatedly. After Secretary Lin stepped forward, he followed behind at an appropriate distance.

Being able to participate in such a major conference was an unforgettable, once-in-a-lifetime opportunity, an opportunity to continue climbing up and even enter the core of power in China!

Thinking of this, Yu Qi only felt a surge in his heart. He quickly took a deep breath and followed everyone into an elevator.

However, Yu Qi looked at the buttons in the elevator but found that no one had pressed the buttons all the while. He could not help but look at his immediate superior in confusion, but the other party seemed to know what he was thinking and only shook his head silently.

About ten seconds later, the elevator doors closed with a “ding”.

Yu Qi was wondering why no one had pressed any buttons in the elevator when he suddenly felt a sense of weightlessness. He subconsciously looked at the elevator display, only to find that the screen display was going by negative numbers!

In other words, the elevator was not heading upward at this time, but was going downward!

What, after having stayed here for so long, he did not even know that there were underground facilities in the Earth Federation headquarters?

Yu Qi was extremely surprised but when the elevator arrived at its floor, he recalled something when he saw Secretary Lin stepping out of the elevator first and skillfully guiding the elegant man forward.

Secretary Lin used to be a spokesperson like Yu Qi was. It was after several years of training in this position in the United Front that he finally got on top and became the present Secretary Lin...

chapters.title: Chapter 485: Weeping Angel
chapters.body:
Chapter 485: Weeping Angel

Chapter 485: Weeping Angel

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

Faced with this scene, Yu Qi’s heart was pounding wildly. This was not an ordinary international conference. The leaders of the four strongest continents of the entire Earth Federation had gathered. What kind of major incident had happened to cause this?

What kind of fortunate opportunity was it for him to be able to attend this event in person?

Yu Qi only felt that the blood in his body was about to boil. His breathing was heavy and his adrenaline surged. If he had not learned to keep his calm on the frontlines of the world through years of experience, he would have barked out laughing.

Regardless of the excitement in Yu Qi’s heart, after everyone walked into the venue from their respective entrances, they naturally sat in their own positions. Occasionally someone would politely nod when they met another party. Even when they greeted each other, the entire venue was silent, without any conversation.

Yu Qi looked behind him but saw that, except for the two bodyguards, the remaining soldiers had stood at the fringes of the venue, guarding their respective doorways.

Seeing that all four parties were here, Secretary Lin in front of Yu Qi looked at the people from Europe and asked aloud in English, “Is that person here?”

Hearing Secretary Lin’s fluent English, Yu Qi was taken aback for a moment and then he immediately realized why. As the meeting was a secret meeting and not due to any formal occasion, everyone used English by default to facilitate communication.

“He has already arrived and he’ll enter the venue in three minutes at most.”

In the seat of the European Continent, a senior official replied.

However, after hearing the exchange between the two, Yu Qi was even more surprised. It was obvious — the reason they came here was to meet someone?

Who was it that warranted the highest leaders of the top four continents in the Earth Federation to meet here like this?

Just when Yu Qi’s mind was exploding in astonishment, he suddenly heard a deep cough.

The sound came from the door behind him. As the meeting had not started yet, the doors behind everyone were not closed, so the sound of the cough easily traveled along the corridor into the venue.

This cough sounded weak as if it belonged to a dying patient and there was a slow set of footsteps.

Before Yu Qi saw who it was, he heard this voice first. Upon hearing this voice, Yu Qi subconsciously conjured up the appearance of a decrepit old man with gray hair.

Then, the footsteps gradually became louder, approaching the venue. Yu Qi looked back subconsciously and finally saw a pale, thin, sickly-looking man appearing outside the doors.

Was it a Chinese man?

“Cough cough cough...”

The soft cough came again. The man was wearing white pajamas. He covered his mouth with a white handkerchief and walked toward the front stage step by step.

This man seemed to have lost his eyebrows and hair after a long period of chemotherapy. Not only that, his body was as thin as if he had lost his human form and his face was so bony that one could no longer see his original countenance.

However, even though this man looked to be near death’s door, he ignored everyone’s gaze and walked toward the stage very matter-of-factly...

Was he whom the supreme leaders of the four continents were waiting for?

Seeing this man, Yu Qi was surprised. He watched the man walking past his side. At the same time, all the politicians he had met before flickered through his mind, but none of them matched this man’s appearance.

Who was this man who was so thin that his age could not even be discerned?

Still, it was obvious that no one in the silent venue would answer Yu Qi’s questions at this time. Everyone just watched the man walk up to the podium step by step and even Yu Qi was a little worried about what they should do in case this man suddenly died halfway as he was walking.

At this moment, Secretary Lin on the side suddenly tapped Yu Qi lightly under the table. Yu Qi turned his head but saw that the big boss and Secretary Lin had both turned their heads to glance at him.

Yu Qi’s heart leaped and he suddenly realized what to do. He quickly stood up and walked over directly to prop up the man. “Old sir, let me help you up.”

“Thank you...”

The man immediately nodded with a smile, allowing Yu Qi to help him along as they walked to the podium under everyone’s gaze.

After helping the man onto the podium, Yu Qi walked off, a little embarrassed, and took his seat again.

“Everyone... I, ugh cough cough cough cough!”

The man was just about to speak when there was another rapid burst of coughs, which caused Yu Qi to wince.

After coughing for a while, the man breathed a sigh of relief. He slowly put down the handkerchief and raised his head, panting slightly.

Suddenly, the man’s eyes met the eyes of everyone there for a moment.

When the man’s gaze swept across Yu Qi, he felt a sudden tremor because he was shocked that the man’s eyes were completely different from his frail, dying image. They were extremely dark and piercing, just like eternal stars in the night sky. They were also like black holes that could draw in human souls. These two extremely contrasting sensations were intertwined together, creating a strange feeling.

He had never seen such a unique pair of eyes!

“Good afternoon, gentlemen. I’d like to welcome you since you’ve taken time out of your busy schedules, cough...”

The next second, the man suddenly spoke in a tired and raspy voice, “At the same time, I’m also very grateful for your presence because it means that you’re willing to listen to my explanation of all this and place such huge trust in me. In just a few moments, I’ll make you feel that this trip is worthwhile...”

“Alright, let’s not waste time on formalities.”

However, at this moment, the President of Northern America suddenly waved her hand impatiently with a somber expression. “Go straight to the point. I hope you can give us a reasonable explanation for the Los Angeles incident!”

“No problem.”

The man nodded and flashed a smile. Even so, thanks to his skeleton-like appearance, this smile seemed truly peculiar.

“First of all, I’d like to ask everyone, do you still remember when the Earth Federation was established?”

“When was it established?”

Hearing the man’s question, the president of the North Sea Continent twirled his pen, recalling. “It was around 1992. I still remember that year, I had just returned from East Germany and witnessed the disintegration of the Soviet Union. Even though more than 70% of the voters in the last referendum wanted to retain the Soviet Union, the upper-level rulers had already made up their minds at that time.”

“To be precise, it should be 20 April 1992.”

The president of the European Continent added. “I also remember that day when the upper-level government suddenly informed us of a resolution to integrate the world, strengthen the ties between various countries on the basis of the United Nations, and form a huge institution.”

Hearing the answers of the two, the man nodded and said slowly, “So, what happened back then? Why did the Earth Federation appear?”

“That is classified.”

The big boss sitting in front of Yu Qi reminded him kindly.

“I understand.”

The man immediately replied, “In fact, although I don’t know the details, I have a general idea. There was a person who united the many countries and created the Earth Federation and that person’s name...”

“It’s a blank!”

Hearing this, the faces of the leaders of all the continents changed. Among them, the president of the European Continent asked in shock, “This is top-secret. Fewer than five people in each continent know about this. How did you learn all this?”

“I don’t just know this, I also know that he left behind the quantum computer ‘Adam’, an instrument that can predict the future. With this instrument, the Earth Federation was able to survive to this day...”

The man coughed slightly and continued. “Am I right?”

Hearing this conversation amongst everyone, Yu Qi felt his world turn upside down. The Earth Federation was expedited by an unnamed person? Who on Earth had such immense power?

“You’re not wrong.”

At this moment, the president of the North Sea Continent suddenly spoke up. He chuckled and said, “I was stunned when I learned this and even used various secret powers to investigate the identity of that person, but the strange thing was that all traces of him seemed to have been erased throughout the world. The secret files of both the Earth Federation and the North Sea Continent had nothing on him.”

“That’s right, cough cough...”

The man nodded, wearing a serious expression. “If there’s anything that you’ve ever wondered about, I’m guessing that mysterious man’s disappearance into thin air must be number one, right?

“Not only his name disappeared but even his identity, family background, nationality, and all traces of daily life had disappeared. If it weren’t for the existence of the ‘Earth Federation’ as proof of his existence, I’m afraid that this disappearance wouldn’t even be noticed by anyone. Don’t you think that’s scary...”

“Do you have information about that person?”

The president of Northern America asked again, “Please stop keeping us in suspense, okay?”

The man nodded slowly when he heard these words. He gripped the podium, took a breath, and then continued. “I don’t know who that person is, but I know why he disappeared.”

“What!”

“What’s the reason?”

The presidents and the other high-level officials of various countries looked stupefied and they all sat up straight to pay closer attention.

“I believe you’ve received the information I shared before you came here? The information about ‘memes’...”

The man did not answer directly but kept them in suspense as he spoke slowly.

“The term ‘meme’ is based on the ancient Greek word ‘μ?μημα’ and evolved from the term ‘gene’ in biology. Dawkins wrote in the book The Selfish Gene that evolution doesn’t depend on the specific chemical basis of genetics. Rather, it relies only on the existence of self-replicating delivery units that are called memes.

“Maybe many people mistakenly believe that a meme is just a so-called Internet buzzword, akin to something like a joke. In fact, this is both right and wrong.”

The man coughed and continued to explain, “The meme defined by our human civilization is indeed a joke, but the meme I define here is something that includes jokes but is far more influential than a mere Internet buzzword...”

With that, the man waved his hand slightly and the word “meme” written in several languages ??appeared on the white screen behind him.

“A meme is a unit of information, not an entity. It’s a new branch of learning that can change human civilization. It’s also a subject that has never been revealed to humans before. We humans have never come across this, not because we failed to discover it, but because it didn’t exist on our Earth before.”

“What you said was ‘before’, right.”

Listening to what the man said, the elegant man in front of Yu Qi suddenly spoke, “In other words, ‘they’ have now appeared on Earth?”

“More or less, yes.”

The man nodded. “No one knows how memes are born, but I tend to think that the Internet has spawned them. As the online world becomes more developed, more and more Internet buzzwords have emerged. Some of these Internet buzzwords are only viral for a while, but some have been celebrated for a long time. I suspect that this unlimited possibility led to the birth of the first batch of memes...

“The angel statues from a few days ago are a meme that is extremely threatening to humans.”

At this point, a paragraph of text appeared on the screen behind the man again. This paragraph was densely packed with words. On the right side of the text, there was a cartoon of an angel statue standing in place with its eyes covered.

Everyone looked at the screen immediately and the text said—

[Memetic Name]: Weeping Angel.

[Special Containment Procedures]: The “Weeping Angel” should be placed in a location that cannot be observed by anyone. Direct or indirect observation is not allowed. Anyone who sees a full picture of a “Weeping Angel” must be permanently quarantined.

[Description]: The “Weeping Angel” is a pattern painted with blood. The pattern is composed of 13,236 “blood dots”, each “blood dot’ is between 0.3-0.5cm in diameter. The “blood dots” constitute a pattern of an angel statue covering its eyes.

The “Weeping Angel” is contagious, and the mode of transmission is visual transmission. Anyone who sees the full picture of the “Weeping Angel” will be infected and the infected person will be transformed into a strange memetic derivative within 90 minutes.

In one operation, four mercenaries inadvertently saw the full picture of “Weeping Angels”, so to prevent memetic infection, all four of them were destroyed in the steel furnace of the Blacklight Steel Mill in the industrial park of the Eco Science City, in Namibia, Africa. More than three months later, when the steel produced in the steelmaking furnace arrived in Los Angeles, Northern America, a memetic infection still occurred, resulting in a large number of derivatives. To distinguish the “Weeping Angel” from its derivatives, a distinction is made —

[Derivative Name]: Weeping Angel 1.

[Special Containment Procedures]: According to the size of “Weeping Angel 1”, it should be placed in a closed space with a height of more than two meters and a width of more than one meter. No particular containment material is required, but it should at least score a 4 on the Mohs scale or there is a risk “Weeping Angel 1” will escape.

When placing “Weeping Angel 1” in containment, the number of personnel must not be less than three at any time and the door must be locked upon entry. At least two people must maintain eye contact with the Weeping Angel throughout the entire process until all personnel have left and the containment chamber is locked again.

[Description]: “Weeping Angel 1” is an angel statue of a material similar to granite. Its usual posture is to cover its face with its hands, with the head slightly lowered at 15-25 degrees, and its wings slightly opened; it appears in the form of a stone statue when observed by human eyes, but once the human looks away, “Weeping Angel 1” will change its position and approach the human at a very fast speed. Its known method of killing is to wring the neck. No other killing method has been found.

It is known that “Weeping Angel 1”, a derivative of the “Weeping Angel”, has indestructible characteristics. Infection occurs through human observation of the “Weeping Angel”. At the same time, anything that bears the image of a “Weeping Angel 1”, including but not limited to photos and videos, has the same infectious power as a “Weeping Angel” itself. However, portraits are an exception...

Seeing this lengthy explanation, both Yu Qi and the others looked flabbergasted.

Did this mean that this was the cause of the Los Angeles incident and the Quantum Deity Laboratory incident?

chapters.title: Chapter 486: Verify
chapters.body:
Chapter 486: Verify

Chapter 486: Verify

At last, a high-ranking official from Northern America suddenly said, “In other words, is it because of this that there are so many angel statues in the Quantum Deity Laboratory?”

“Yes.”

The man nodded. “Presumably you must’ve experimented with them using death row inmates as well. What the quantum computer Adam predicted was none other than a video of the ‘Weeping Angel’. You don’t want to believe that Adam will betray you. But in fact, Adam didn’t betray you because the video isn’t the issue. The real problem isn’t the video but the meme...”

After hearing everything the man on the stage said, everyone went silent.

Although this incident happened in Northern America, all those present naturally had a wide range of information acquisition channels. They knew everything about what happened that day, except for why it happened. Now this man was explaining that it was something called a “meme”.

Yu Qi did not know what the others thought, but he was already a little convinced because earlier the Northern American representative in the United Front, Nancy, had said something similar and blamed Yu Qi.

Hold on...

Suddenly, Yu Qi came to realize that the man on the stage must be from Blacklight Biotechnology. No wonder he did not know who this man was. However, why was it not the founder of Blacklight Biotechnology, but instead a sickly patient sent in his place?

At this time, the man on the stage was still explaining, “Later, in our experiments, we found that the statue has a very strange nature. It has the ability to influence any monitoring equipment. This ability is ineffective when the statue is in its petrified state, but once no one is observing it, the Weeping Angel will come to life and the equipment responsible for monitoring it will immediately fail.

“In other words, no one can see what the Weeping Angel looks like when no one is watching and even the monitoring device itself cannot observe it.

“When we look at it, it’s a statue and even its hardness is comparable to that of a statue. It can be easily broken into pieces, but no matter where you throw these statue pieces or how small you break these pieces into, the moment you look away slightly, you’ll find that the statue is restored again...

“This is the indestructibility of the statue. As for why it is so, we tend to believe that it’s because the statue itself doesn’t exist. We cannot kill a meme because a meme is a concept and a concept is immortal.”

“So, do they have any flaws?”

Below the stage, a senior official sitting next to the president of the European group suddenly asked.

“Their flaw can be said to be simple, that is, a line of sight.”

The man coughed twice and continued. “I believe you have also noticed, why can’t the Weeping Angel break our necks directly, but has to play ‘Statues’ with us? It’s not actually that this meme has this particular hobby because memes have no thinking. They are like wind and rain, a natural phenomenon, a natural law. So this is the law of this meme, that as long as it’s within someone’s line of sight, it’ll never be able to harm humanity.”

Hearing this sentence, many people looked disappointed. This was not helpful at all.

Nonetheless, in the next second, the man suddenly said, “Besides, we found that the reason the Weeping Angels always cover their eyes isn’t that they like to cry, as their name suggests, but because their line of sight also creates an effect of observation. Once two or more Weeping Angels look at each other, then these Weeping Angels will never be able to move...”

Yu Qi’s eyes suddenly lit up upon hearing this. Although he had never seen a real Weeping Angel, he could still deduce now that if this principle was used well, it was indeed a trump card against Weeping Angels!

“Also, we discovered in our experiments that if we kill a transforming person in advance before they transform into a statue, it can also stop the transformation of a human to a Weeping Angel.”

The man continued to speak. He sighed and said ruefully, “This is all the research we have on Weeping Angels.”

After hearing all this, everyone subconsciously looked toward Northern America as if they wanted to hear these words verified by the people from Northern America.

“No errors or omissions have been found for the time being.”

Seeing this, Nancy from Northern America nodded reluctantly, but he suddenly frowned, and asked in a mildly accusing tone, “You made some good points, but if everything you said is true, then how should your Blackwatch Corporation compensate for the losses incurred in the Quantum Deity Laboratory and Los Angeles City? After all, all of this was caused by your Blackwatch Corporation!”

Hearing this, several high-ranking officials in the European seat also agreed.

“Moreover...”

Nancy continued to speak, aggressively saying, “In the middle of the night on 2 June, outside of the Herrank Warehouse District in Los Angeles, one of our army units suddenly lost contact with headquarters. When they were found, they had already been wiped out. Don’t tell me this was also thanks to the Weeping Angels...”

“We at Blackwatch Corporation don’t know about this.”

The man replied with a placid expression and without waiting for Nancy to continue asking, he changed the subject again. “Then, back to our previous topic. I think after this instance, you should understand why he disappeared — the mysterious person who spurred the United Nations to form the Earth Federation, who didn’t even have a name?”

Everyone’s expressions flickered when they heard this and Nancy swallowed the words he was about to say...

“You mean that the mysterious man who united the world and created the Earth Federation also disappeared because of memes?”

Suddenly, all the questions began.

“That’s right.”

The man wore a solemn expression. “But what I want to clarify is that he wasn’t relying on ordinary memes, but something heterogeneous to memes — the antimeme.”

“Antimeme?”

Everyone murmured in low voices, wishing to break up all these words, and analyze each word clearly...

“If a meme is a unit of information and its raison d’être is to copy, mutate, and spread, then an antimeme is the opposite.”

A dark glint flashed in the man’s eyes and he said softly, “Any antimeme would be unknowable, because its existence doesn’t give out any information, just like a black hole of data. All perceptions, when they come into touch with an antimeme, will be sucked into the black hole...”

“In other words, maybe that person was right by our side and we might even know him very well?”

The president of the North Sea Continent was almost laughing in exasperation. “But because of the antimeme, we forgot about him?”

“Yes.”

The man nodded. “However, the impact of the antimeme on memory isn’t only to make people unable to remember it but also to make people lose interest in it and eliminate all traces of itself in reality. This is the most terrifying thing... Ignorance is frightening, but if you’re ignorant and unable to acquire any knowledge, then that’s true despair.

“It’s through the use of an antimeme that this person can conceal himself and eliminate everything related to himself in the world!”

“If what you are saying is true, doesn’t it mean that our human civilization could use memetic technology more than 30 years ago?”

Secretary Lin frowned and suddenly asked, “But isn’t this contrary to your previous statement? You said that there were no memes on Earth before. Don’t these two statements contradict each other?”

“It’s not a contradiction, sir.”

Though a flaw was pointed out, the man did not show the slightest hint of embarrassment but instead flashed a peculiar smile. “Because I didn’t say that the antimeme came from Earth. Don’t twist my words...”

After hearing these words, Secretary Lin was startled. Everyone else also looked thoughtful.

The United Front representative, Nancy, was just about to say something, but at this moment, he suddenly saw the president of Northern America shoot him a look. He took a deep breath, slammed the table, and got to his feet!

“Enough!”

“I admit that if you’re a speaker, you’re indeed well qualified, but everything you’ve said should at least be based on evidence. Now, I only see a series of hypotheses, which aren’t enough to be believed!”

“Evidence?”

The man turned his head, rather mystified. He raised his handkerchief and coughed slightly. “I’m the evidence.”

“You?”

Nancy seemed to be indignant at being mocked. He was about to say something when suddenly there was a blur across his vision. In the next second, the man who was ten meters away suddenly flickered and appeared in front of him!

“Woah!”

Nancy was so frightened that he fell back. In less than half a second, the man who looked like a terminal patient had crossed a distance of more than ten meters. This strange speed shocked everyone. In an almost conditioned reflex, the three bodyguards from Northern America took out their guns at the same time and took aim at the man who had moved abruptly!

However, the man just flashed a weird smile. With a single wave of his hand, the guns in the hands of these bodyguards seemed to come alive. They spun out of the bodyguards’ hands and flew directly toward the man!

‘Oh no!’

Such a thought flashed through everyone’s minds.

At this, not only the high-level bodyguards from Northern America, but also the bodyguards and soldiers in the entire venue all pulled out their guns and a dozen guns were steadily aimed at the man in the center!

At once, the atmosphere turned chillingly tense!

“Chen Chen, what are you trying to do!”

Several high-ranking officials stood up and looked at the man in front of them in indescribable rage.

“Don’t be nervous, I’m just doing a demonstration.”

The man looked around. At this moment, he suddenly transformed from his previous sickly aura. He seemed to be a different person. His eyes were sharp and his whole body exuded a disturbing presence.

“Clack clack clack, click, jingle jingle...”

Following the man’s words, the three pistols were automatically disassembled in mid-air. It took just the blink of an eye. The three guns were completely disassembled into their most basic parts and they clinked against each other as they scattered to the floor like puzzle pieces...

Seeing this scene, everyone was stunned. They had watched the three guns automatically self-disintegrate as if they were alive. These people wanted to tell themselves it was just a magic trick, but what kind of magic could perform this?

“If this isn’t enough...”

Following that, the man spoke again. He spread his palms out to everyone and then walked directly to the podium.

Just when everyone was still shocked by the fact that he had previously dismantled the guns in thin air, he went straight to the wall at the end of the podium and even stepped onto the wall when he reached the end.

Then, an even more incredible scene appeared — like a helium balloon, the man directly broke free of gravity and his body walked across the wall vertically at 90 degrees. This was not over yet, he was still walking forward...

When he reached the top of the wall, he stepped on the ceiling again.

“Patter, patter, patter...”

After a few thudding footsteps, the man was standing on the ceiling. He spread his arms to face everyone and even his hair and the corners of his clothes had not shifted as if gravity did not exist...

At this moment, everyone looked up at this man and the whole meeting room was dead silent...

“Clatter!”

The President of the North Sea Continent was stunned, with mouth agape, by all this. He did not even notice that the pen he was twirling subconsciously had fallen to the ground. The heads and officials of the various countries were stupefied. Everyone seemed to have been turned to stone.

Still, this was not the end. In the next second, everyone suddenly felt their bodies becoming lighter and many people exclaimed reflexively. Then, as they cried out in surprise, everyone apart from the presidents of the four continents started to float up uncontrollably!

Yu Qi only felt that there seemed to be countless pairs of big invisible hands all over his body, beginning to tug at him. He struggled instinctively, but no matter how hard he struggled, he could not resist this dark unseen force. After that, his body flipped over and he floated toward the roof, upside down...

“Please forgive my manners, everyone. Do you still think all this is just a magic trick?”

The man on the stage looked calm and unruffled. At this time, his words had an incomparably persuasive force...

chapters.title: Chapter 487: Blacklight Foundation
chapters.body:
Chapter 487: Blacklight Foundation

Chapter 487: Blacklight Foundation

When everyone was indescribably shocked, Yu Qi felt his body suddenly loosened and he started to fall headfirst at free fall!

The ceiling of the venue was a full five or six meters above the ground. Once he dropped headfirst, he would die or at least become disabled. Yu Qi instantly let out a scream. It was not only him, but all the people who had been pressed to the ceiling also dropped down like dumplings. The entire venue was filled with one scream after another for a time.

Still, just as Yu Qi instinctively closed his eyes, when he was about to make close contact with the ground, the expected impact did not happen...

Yu Qi slowly opened his eyes, only to realize that he was frozen in mid-air again, no more than one meter away from the ground.

Immediately, an invisible force pushed him and Yu Qi’s body suddenly turned one hundred and eighty degrees and became upright once more. His feet stepped on the ground again.

“Phew...”

He returned to his seat, bewildered. Yu Qi was now totally pale. He looked around in cold sweat but saw that other people had regained their freedom of movement just like him. At this time, everyone looked at the man on the ceiling as if he was a ghost.

If they could explain what happened before as a magic trick, then everything they had experienced just now was no magic trick...

Hold on!

Suddenly, Yu Qi seemed to realize something. The group of people called out the man’s name just now, which meant that except for himself and a few others, everyone who came to the meeting knew who this man was.

His name was Chen Chen?

Huh?

Hearing this familiar name, Yu Qi pondered a little dazedly, then he seemed to recall something and quickly raised his head!

This man was Chen Chen!

At this moment, Yu Qi finally understood why the foremost leaders of the four continents had gathered here and why a non-politician appeared in such an important venue, and why he would stand here as the speaker...

There was only one reason and that was that he was the founder of Blacklight Biotechnology — a legend who could crush the Northern American government, carry out arms trade with the Earth Federation as an individual, and even use his own developed technology to affect the entire human society!

Chen Chen!

However, in his memory, Chen Chen was a young man, but now the person standing on the stage looked like a patient who was so thin that he was unrecognizable, that calling him terminally ill would not be an exaggeration?

At this moment, a famous online picture flashed in Yu Qi’s mind. In the picture, there was an old man with white beard and hair, and a few lines of text accompanying it:

‘A person who does scientific research is wise, contented, and knows how to live. The secret he has learned is, scientific research in the morning, scientific research at noon, and scientific research at night; you see, he is almost 30 years old now, and he is still in good spirits...’

Regardless of Yu Qi’s inner thoughts, at this moment the thin, unrecognizable man spread his arms, slowly floated down from the ceiling, and then stood firmly on the ground. “Everyone, now do you still think all this is a magic trick?”

Everyone wiped off their sweat silently and there was silence in the audience.

“The ‘Weeping Angel’ incident isn’t the first memetic incident that occurred on Earth.”

Seeing that no one spoke, Chen Chen continued to speak. His voice gradually became low and reverberated through the meeting room at a low pitch. “I believe all of you remember the ‘This Man’ incident that happened in Northern America and the ‘Three Statues’ incident two years ago. Those two were similarly memetic incidents, but were detected and calmly resolved by us Blacklight Biotechnology, which allowed our human civilization to avoid the corresponding crisis.”

“What!”

Several high-level officials in Northern America immediately slammed on the table. Among them, Nancy stood up angrily. “You were responsible for the three statues that disappeared two years ago?”

“Sorry.”

Chen Chen nodded slightly and smiled apologetically. “If human civilization needs a person who hides in the dark and allows progress by carrying heavy burdens, then all these sins will be borne by Blacklight Biotechnology.”

“You...”

Nancy wanted to say something but was stopped by the high-ranking official beside him.

“So, your Blackwatch Corporation has the technology to control memes?”

The president of Northern America looked at Chen Chen solemnly. “Please answer us truthfully on this.”

“Yes, but only superficially.”

Chen Chen stared at her and nodded calmly.

As he said this, the expressions of all the people present changed even more and the eyes of everyone looking at Chen Chen were instantly full of complexities.

Whether it was this angel statue incident or the last This Man incident, everyone understood that memes were far more dangerous and unstable than nuclear weapons. After all, nuclear weapons could only destroy human civilization at best, but memes could even destroy the foundation of what they knew...

Therefore, Chen Chen, who claimed to have acquired memetic technology, was instantly upgraded from being an ordinary person to being on an equal footing with the four continents.

“Memetic technology is a long way from being thoroughly studied by human civilization.”

At this time, Chen Chen said frankly, “And the reason I know this is because I’m only a successor. I inherited the legacy of the man who disappeared from this world, so I was able to build the famous Blacklight Biotechnology and finally now stand in front of you.”

Hearing this sentence, everyone’s expressions flickered and they considered the truthfulness of Chen Chen’s words.

“I believe that with your intelligence, you should’ve already noticed that something was wrong. What is the purpose of the Earth Federation’s existence? Isn’t it to unite the power of all mankind? But why should we unite when there’s no foreign enemy?”

Chen Chen stood on the stage and spoke slowly, “Also, the quantum computer Adam is the legacy left by ‘that person’ to us. What’s his purpose and why did he leave this machine that can predict the future?

“If these two things are no big deal, then the existence of the Earth Federation United Front is even more suspicious...”

Hearing Chen Chen’s rhetorical questions, Yu Qi could not help recalling the United Front’s classification for various incidents. One of them was the so-called “abnormal event”.

It turned out that the Earth Federation United Front and the quantum computer Adam existed for the sake of catastrophic disasters such as memes?

After Chen Chen said so many things that only truly high-level figures could know, Yu Qi’s heart was overcome with shock. All his previous suspicions had collapsed at this time and were completely exposed under the light of day...

At this moment, the eyes of the leaders of the four continents also flashed distractedly. Since Chen Chen was able to speak of the secret of the “blank” and at the same time had learned all this from nothing, in addition to his current achievements, everyone had already believed at least half of his words.

“If you’re the successor to the ‘non-existent person’, what are you going to do now?”

At this moment, the president of the North Sea Continent suddenly spoke. He took a deep look at Chen Chen. “And what do you want by inviting us here today?”

“I don’t need anything because it’s not me who is in need, but human civilization.”

Chen Chen said with a faint smile, “I think the reason you didn’t dissolve the Earth Federation is that you’ve found some clues. I have to say that the situation of humanity is very precarious now...

“‘That person’ told me that this world is far crueler than we thought. In the vast universe, the earth is like an island in the ocean and human civilization is a castle built by children with fine sand. It’s exquisite but fragile. If any big wave hits, this civilization that mankind is so proud of will completely collapse with barely a splash.

“In addition, he also mentioned a theory called the ‘Great Filter of Civilization’ — if the planet is said to be a whole, then a planet with life is a human being with a soul. But we just don’t get that the emergence of civilization isn’t a pleasant surprise for the planet, but is like cancer cells instead. Civilization develops at the cost of harming the planet itself...

“So every civilization that develops from a corresponding ecosystem needs to withstand this resistance from the planet’s immune system. As long as the civilization isn’t destroyed, this kind of resistance will continue...”

Chen Chen continued to speak solemnly. “Our human perceptions have always been wrong. From the time we experienced World War II until now, almost no large-scale disaster has occurred. This gives us the illusion that the world is very safe, but if you look at history, you’ll find that this kind of ‘security’ is just an accidental phenomenon. The history of human civilization has always been based on countless natural and man-made disasters. So ‘that person’ united all humankind to create the Earth Federation for responding to crises that originate from the planet or even the depths of the universe...

“This was what ‘he’ told me.”

As he spoke, Chen Chen stood in front of everyone, spreading his arms. “As the successor of ‘that person’, I must inherit his will. Therefore, I hope that the entire human race can unite or at least that everyone here can join forces and work together, to deal with any crisis that may arise at any time in the future. This is the purpose of my trip...”

A true “union”?

Hearing Chen Chen’s words, everyone did not reply immediately but bowed their heads in thought.

“If we agree, what do we have to contribute, and what can we get from it?”

The President of Northern America suddenly asked.

“I’ll share the legacy I received from ‘that person’.”

Chen Chen replied without hesitation, “In this legacy, in addition to the technologies that I’ve released by now, there are more secret technologies that haven’t been announced.”

With that, Chen Chen suddenly waved his hand and dozens of paper sheets flew toward the leaders of the continents. These people picked up the papers in their hands and looked at them. Everyone looked astounded...

In this material, although there were only a few catalogs, it contained all the things that humans had dreamed of, but were hard to obtain!

Super AI technology, normal-temperature superconductor, stellarator reactor, super tokamak reactor, mini hadron collider, nuclear-powered spacecraft, fully automatic medical device...

The densely packed catalog dazzled all of them because everyone present knew that every item in here was enough to trigger a new industrial revolution!

At this moment, everyone’s world was turned upside down!

“In addition to the commercial use of these technologies, I can take them out and share them with everyone.”

Chen Chen spread out his hands and faced the crowd with an enticing tone. “The fully automatic medical device can cure all human diseases so far, and at the same time, greatly extend the human lifespan by more than three hundred years...

“The normal-temperature superconductor discards the need for low-temperature superconductors to maintain ultra-low temperatures of negative one hundred degrees. It can maintain superconductivity at room temperature, which is enough to overturn the entire industrial system...

“A mature super tokamak reactor has a maximum Q-value of over 10,000. Once completed, human civilization will enter the age of unlimited energy and humans will formally evolve into a trans-planetary species...

“There are more inheritances that I can share with all of your continents.”

Chen Chen continued. “And what you need to contribute is very simple and that’s to protect all humankind with me wholeheartedly. I hope that everyone can work with me to establish a special organization dedicated to protecting human civilization. This organization will have the highest authority, becoming a legal armed department independent of the Federation’s governments. Plus, it’ll have the same privileges as the Earth Federation United Army. It can operate in any region of the world without being obstructed by the law...”

When they heard Chen Chen’s request, everyone came back to their senses. Among them, the elegant man beside Yu Qi thought for a while and then chuckled slightly. “If you can lead the entire human civilization to resist the damage caused by the planet, what’s wrong with giving you permission?”

“On the condition that you cannot interfere in the internal affairs of all continents, let alone use this organization for war.”

The President of Northern America added.

“Of course.”

Chen Chen nodded and solemnly said, “Please be rest assured, my ambition isn’t in authority, but in immortality, which should be evident from my first technology.”

“In that case, what is the structure of this organization?”

The president of the North Sea Continent was a little curious and said, “And how do you plan to establish this institution, and what is its function?”

“For the structure of the organization, I’ll allow each of you to send one person to join the highest council of the organization. One, they’ll be responsible for controlling the organization and two, they’ll be in charge of supervision. Moreover, the identity of this person should be top secret.”

Chen Chen said without having to think, “The decision-makers of this supreme council have the right to vote and can effectively make suggestions to the organization.”

“As for the name of this institution, we can call it — Blacklight Foundation.”

chapters.title: Chapter 488: South Pole
chapters.body:
Chapter 488: South Pole

Chapter 488: South Pole

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

Several hours later, Chen Chen stepped out of the black sphere teleportation room, his body appeared extremely malnourished.

With the completion of the third reactor with a measured power of 10 million kilowatts, the USB drive was generating energy faster. During this time, he had instantiated several additional Gantz black spheres and had hidden one of them near the Earth Federation Headquarters to allow himself to freely navigate to and from there.

Chen Chen’s cheeks were pale and sunken in. There was hardly any strand of hair left on his body. Anybody who saw this haggard display would mistake him for an elderly man approaching his death.

This was after two days of rest. His condition was slightly worse right when his transformation had ended.

With all that said, this was only temporary. Using the Black Knight’s recovery progress as a reference, it should only take no more than a week for Chen Chen to return to his previous form.

Chen Chen, who just emerged from the teleportation room, appeared to be in a joyous mood. He paced around energetically despite his sickly appearance. It seemed like the conference he had just ended turned out very well.

“Sir Godfather, it looks like the meeting went well.”

As soon as Chen Chen returned to the Spire Experimental Base, he heard Little X’s voice beside him.

Chen Chen turned to his side and saw Little X sneaking up on him. She tried to play a prank on Chen Chen by sneaking up and jumping at him like how a little girl would.

“Not bad, it went pretty well.”

Chen Chen mentioned with a nod, “I promised to give them several pieces of technology that can multiply overall society productivity and their military strength.”

Chen Chen’s lips curled into a smirk as he mentioned this, “Additionally, I’ve also promised the four major continents that I’ll provide one ‘Blacklight Fully-automated Medical Platform’ to each one of them. They stared at me wide-eyed when I extended this offer. After all, the technology contained in that device greatly surpasses all contemporary technology. How could they ever refuse this excellent offer?”

“We still need to be cautious and keep our guard up in case they betray us.”

Upon hearing Chen Chen’s remark, Little X raised a point, which she seldom did. “After all, we’re dealing with four governmental bodies. Once they start to gain a better understanding of our organization...”

“Don’t worry, I’m well aware that we have conflicting interests with them.”

Chen Chen smiled faintly before continuing. “Which is why I haven’t provided them any details concerning our memetic research nor did I give them anything such as the G-virus or black goo that is capable of destroying mankind. These are assets that we have to hold on to since they’re the equivalent of nuclear threats. We don’t gain anything by sharing these assets, we only need them to know that we’re capable of unleashing these on them...”

“What’s the purpose of us establishing this multi-party secret agency?”

Little X tilted her head to the side and was slightly confused. “Is it just as the previous owner of the USB drive Zhao Shanhe said, to serve as a countermeasure from whatever galactic crisis that may occur?”

“That’s not the only reason.”

Chen Chen answered almost immediately, “The reason I established the Blacklight Foundation to drag them into this goes beyond serving as a countermeasure to whatever universe crisis that may occur. Another far more vital agenda is to deal with that mysterious person lurking in the darkness.

“That person who is constantly laying traps and plotting my death, I don’t care if this person is Wangxi, Zhao Shanhe or somebody else... If this person wants to wage war against me, then I need to be prepared to retaliate. This institution is essentially my solution to strapping the entire Earth Federation into my battalion...”

Chen Chen suddenly smiled upon noting this. “From today on, this new institution will establish a legally armed faction that’s independent from any governmental bodies in the world and has more authority than any other army in the Earth Federation. We’ll be able to operate in any part of the world without any legal restriction. Meanwhile, I can also temporarily employ hundreds of aerospace experts from them which will serve as additions to our aerospace center and raise our overall production level.

“Why is Musk so obsessed with space travel? Because the mastery of aerospace technology is equivalent to grasping the future of mankind!”

Having said this, Chen Chen suddenly turned to look at the girl who was staring at him blankly at this point. At the same time, a discrete smile that seemed to camouflage his intentions surfaced on his lips.

“Are you ready for the new chapter of Blacklight Biotechnology? From today on, we’ll be ushering the aerospace era...”

...

On the fifth of June in 2024, the four leading continents of the Earth Federation which were the Central, North Sea, Europe, and Northern America made a grand gesture out of nowhere. They unequivocally agreed to an unprecedented preferential policy that would allow Blacklight Biotechnology to establish branch companies in each of these regions.

At the same time, the intense negotiations on Xia Yin’s part that lasted more than five months had finally come to a close.

The news broke out on the Internet and immediately went viral. Countless people were baffled by such exciting revelation and in a near-instant, the stocks of all the major pharmaceutical and electronic companies all over the world drove off a cliff. In a matter of seconds, the world was in an uproar!

A lot of people in the pharmaceutical and electronic sector started “crying wolf” as they contacted their respective governments to look into the details of the recently agreed-upon policy. There was certain merit to the saying that “ignorance is bliss” because the results of their inquiry would give them a greater shock since the document was signed by the highest governmental bodies of the major continents...

That was when everyone realized that nobody in the Earth Federation could ever hope to contain Blackwatch any longer.

This was merely the beginning. Just as Blacklight Biotechnology announced their cooperation with the four major continents, they simultaneously developed a drug that could completely cure Alzheimer’s disease and immediately launched clinical trials in the four continents.

This would be the 139th clinical trial that Blacklight Biotechnology would conduct on an international scale. The previous 138 clinical trials were all related to cancer-targeted drugs.

The same thing was on everybody’s mind: Blacklight Biotechnology was on a killing spree...

This new drug variant was projected to completely cure mild to moderate cases of Alzheimer’s disease. In severe cases of Alzheimer’s disease, the drug could serve as a cure but would not be able to restore the previous intelligence level of the patient.

This limitation was due to the inherent characteristic of Alzheimer’s disease which was a disease that killed large quantities of brain cells. The damage caused by the disease was irreversible, therefore a decrease in intelligence level was a common effect. There was only one solution to remedy its damage – neural stem cell transplantation.

There were several approaches which included ventricular puncture transplantation, lumbar puncture subarachnoid injection transplantation or intravenous injection transplantation. Neural stem cells were transplanted into the brain to replace the necrotic regions and generate new brain cells. This was the only method to regain a portion of lost intelligence.

The human brain was the most mysterious part of the human body. Therefore, it would be more apt to describe this approach as a “modification” as opposed to a “cure” since new brain cells were not the same thing as the original brain cells but completely separate cells instead. This meant that a significant change in memory and personality of the patient was all but inevitable.

Of course, in this situation, Chen Chen could also use the brain repair chip in “Robocop” to alleviate this issue, just like what he did for the Father of DNA. However, this stem of technology would not be widely applicable for at least five more decades, which meant it was not a viable option for ordinary people...

However, this was merely a teaser for the main event. With the momentum still going strong and the topic still trending, the Central Continent along with the other major continents of the Earth Federation announced once again that they would be cooperating with Blacklight Technology to jointly develop a large-scale project akin to “Artificial Sun”, which was what was also known as controlled nuclear fusion.

The project was branded as the “CUERB Project”. The Northern American and European Continent even went out on a limb and publicly declared that within three years of the project announcement, the controlled nuclear fusion research would be completed. After that, mankind shall gain complete mastery over controllable nuclear fusion and put an end to one of the toughest endeavors in the history of mankind!

Unsurprisingly, news of this announcement tossed the entire world into an uproar. Countless countries and their governments started prying for intel like hounds sniffing blood in the air. It came off as a surprise to them to see the four major continents holding a collective stance, which was refusing to yield any information no matter what.

It may appear that just like with nuclear weapons in the past, the four major continents were ready to seize this controlled nuclear fusion technology...

As one would expect, the remaining continents felt bitter about the way things had turned out, but there was nothing they could do about it. Another thing that had them guessing was why would the major continents be directly associated with a private company like Blacklight Biotechnology?

Could it be...

A bone-chilling idea came to these speculators – perhaps certain central technology revolving around controlled fusion technology had something to do with Blacklight Biotechnology.

For now, we would leave the speculations conjured by the various parties all over the world regarding the CUERB Project aside. Several days later, a small tunnel boring machine with a measured diameter of about five meters was assembled in the Spire Experimental Base and drilled into the layer of rock formations based on the pre-designated plans and blueprints.

With that, a brand new secret project was conducted in the Spire Experimental Base.

Chen Chen planned to construct a ring of particle accelerators at a depth of more than seven hundred meters around the Spire laboratory.

This particle accelerator ring did not need to be obscenely large. Its circumference would only total to approximately three kilometers, which was one-tenth the size of the largest particle accelerator in the world. Once completed, it would surround the entire Spire Experimental Base and put the Spire at its very center.

Some of the core devices for this implementation were already acquired by Chen Chen in Europe and Northern America.

This project was originally assigned to Xia Yin, but things had taken an unexpected turn with the leak of memetic technology. Chen Chen was forced to expose his cards to the Earth Federation earlier than he planned to, which led to the situation developing to what it was now.

This particle accelerator did not need to be constructed any larger because even at this size, it was already far more powerful than the world’s largest particle accelerator in Geneva, Switzerland. The superconductor temperature regulatory technology alone reduced its size to at least two-thirds of the one in Geneva.

In addition, Blacklight Biotechnology obtained its technology from Elysium. Compared with the actual particle accelerators built in Europe, it was undoubtedly much more advanced and its power nearly hundreds of times higher. It was unnecessary to make it any larger than what was needed.

In addition to studying high-energy physics with this particle accelerator, what Chen Chen strived for was to research the so-called “high deflection armor”. Once they could replicate what was known as the “hardest matter in the solar system”, Chen Chen would have the leverage to further strengthen his arsenal and further pull apart the gap between himself and the Earth Federation!

The Blacklight Biotechnology particle accelerator project had officially commenced. The crew of scientists in the Spire Experimental Base was excited about its development...

This was merely one of Blacklight Biotechnology’s many projects. While this was happening, on the other end of the world at the South Pole, the Amundsen–Scott South Pole Station that was once here had been completely disbanded. The only evidence of their past stay here was the empty barracks and the airport with a 4270 meters runway.

At this moment, several military transport planes of different models and different stamped logos were parked at the airport. The curious sight was that there was a mix of different models from different countries. There was the C-17 transport aircraft with the Northern American flag painted on top, there was also the An-70 transport aircraft of the North Sea Continent, not to forget Europe’s A400M and the Yun-20 of the Central Continent...

The military transport aircraft of all four major continents were scattered all across the abandoned airport...

Somewhere nearby the barracks were several large transport helicopters parked atop the ice shelves. On the southern end of the South Pole, a huge drill with a diameter of more than three meters was slowly being erected into the air with iron scaffolding...

In accordance with the “Antarctic Treaty” signed by the continents of the Earth Federation, the use of Antarctica was strictly restricted to peaceful purposes and all military, nuclear or radioactive waste disposal activities were prohibited. However, the four continents were now blatantly violating the agreement and moving their military forces into Antarctica.

Their goal here was none other than to construct the first main base of the Blacklight Foundation underneath the ice sheet at the South Pole.

Based on Chen Chen’s plans, the base was to be built two kilometers below the Antarctic ice sheet. Upon completion, it should be a safe space from any calamities that should occur in the world above it.

Unless the earth was to detonate or stray off track of its orbit and swerve into the edges of the galaxy, the base should remain standing regardless of whatever happened.

As a gratuitous display for the commencement of the first four-continental joint military project in human history, Chen Chen had invested 10 billion U.S. dollars on his own accord for the construction of this base. The other four continents also invested tens of billions of U.S. dollars of their own while dispatching military forces to oversee the project.

This grand project was completely secluded from the outside world. Its nature was even more secretive than the Quantum Deity Laboratory. All satellites passing over Antarctica were ordered to be temporarily deactivated to prevent the secret from being exposed. Any other continents that attempt to pry into what was happening would receive stern warnings from the four major continents to not step over the red line or else they would receive joint sanctions.

Such a grand display was undoubtedly bewildering to anyone who had the chance to witness it. Nobody would ever foresee such a day where the four strongest continents in the Earth Federation united their efforts together...

chapters.title: Chapter 489: Three Years Later
chapters.body:
Chapter 489: Three Years Later

Chapter 489: Three Years Later

Blacklight Biotechnology, full name Blacklight Biotechnology Limited Company or also known as Blacklight Corporation. Its industry sectors included biopharmaceuticals, bionic medical, electronic technology, security, military aerospace, mineral resources, and other industries. It was a company wholly dedicated to diversified development especially in emerging industries.

Blacklight Biotechnology had nearly a hundred branch companies under its wing. The central ones among them were: Blacklight Biotechnology Central Continent Head Company, Blacklight Biotechnology African Branch Company, Blacklight Biotechnology Northern American Branch Company, Blacklight Biotechnology North Sea Branch Company, Blacklight Biotechnology European branch companies, and so on.

All of these branch companies had further subsidiaries in their respective region to handle the company’s operations for them. The shadow of Blacklight Biotechnology loomed over nearly every major city in the world.

In addition, Blacklight Biotechnology also had a large number of subsidiary companies which included: Los Angeles Osmond Biotechnology Limited Company, Preston Biotechnology Limited Company in Switzerland, Tara Strategic Resources Limited Company in Africa, and so on...

Blacklight Biotechnology was what legends were made of, starting from a whisper to a slowly garnering international attention, and then till the Project Xingtian that launched it into international stardom. This was soon followed by renowned cancer and Alzheimer’s disease targeted drugs, all of this only within a few years...

Over the last few years, Blacklight Biotechnology also expanded its reach into electronic technology, military, and aerospace sectors. The results of their endeavors were exactly as one might expect. Around the middle of 2025, the astounding CUERB Project was launched and that was when the world came to realize that Blacklight Biotechnology had taken a massive stride and ascended itself to a technological giant at the forefront of the world.

...

On a certain day three years later.

A haggard-looking young man with an unshaven beard on a wheelchair arrived at the gate of Blacklight Biotechnology Northern America headquarters in the Reston neighborhood of New York City.

His arms were fully intact and there were no signs of a urinary catheter attached to his body so he could not have been suffering from paralysis. However, the sections of his body were cut off below the knee.

Pedro Lane was a young man who had recently lost his legs.

He sat inside his wheelchair, disregarding the strange looks that passersby threw at him as he gazed upward at the enormous skyscraper several hundred meters tall.

This building was a modernist level 5-A office building that stood roughly three hundred meters tall. There were a total of one hundred and one floors in the building. The building shape took on a unique polygonal structure that made it double as a landmark in the Reston district.

Sitting atop his lap was a filled-out registration form with the Blackwatch logo stamped on top of it.

At the time, the gigantic LED panel on the exterior building was playing all kinds of news footage concerning Blacklight Biotechnology.

After the previous news clip ended, the figure of three people appeared on screen, their appearance was accompanied by passionate jazz music.

The three dark figures stood tall amid the darkness and flames and as the camera panned closer, their appearance was finally put on display.

Among the three of them, two were white men while the other was a black man. They had their arms across their chest as they faced the camera. They appeared confident and unyielding, evoking a powerful sense of justice with their posture.

The member at the center of the group suddenly proclaimed, “Severely crack down on any form of crime and resolutely defend the rights and interests of every citizen!”

After that, another man who appeared slightly younger also proclaimed, “Every second count, for every second wasted could mean another innocent citizen’s life lost!”

At last, the black man followed. “Justice may be late, but it’s never absent!”

“Courage!”

“Honor!”

“Sacrifice!”

The three proclaimed in unison and stretched their arms outward to stack their hands together. They looked together at the camera. “We’re the BHB Combative Police Force. Join us to preserve world peace and protect the right of public citizens. Blackwatch is always waiting for you to enlist!”

At last, their figures faded out from the view of the camera as a sentence appeared on the screen. “Blackwatch is recruiting combative police, anyone is welcomed to the trial training.”

When Pedro Lane saw the world-renowned legendary figures appear on the screen, especially the captain leading the trio, an icy and hateful glare gleamed in Lane’s eyes.

The image on the display switched again within seconds. A fair-looking girl with a somewhat stiff expression appeared on the screen. She was facing the camera and held her arms out in front of the camera enthusiastically and said:

“Xingtian series home Androids will soon be inducted into thousands of households. I’m your family nanny Xiao Ai, take me into your home!”

“Bang!”

Before the commercial finished, a beer bottle was suddenly thrown onto the display. It shattered on the ten-meter tall, gigantic display and left a very visible hole in the middle of the LED screen...

Lane was caught off guard by this and immediately snapped his neck around to investigate what was happening. He saw dozens of poorly dressed middle-aged people holding banners with written slogans on them. They stood in a formation behind him and started shouting at the Blacklight Biotechnology building.

Various quotes were written in a poor font using a red marker on the boards they held. “Androids stole the work of the poor”, “Androids are a manifestation of sin”, “God created mankind, but did not create Androids”, “The purity of mankind should not be desecrated” and so on.

So, it was the protestors that boycotted Blackwatch’s Androids...

Pedro Lane shook his head at the sight of this. He turned around again and saw eight security guards wearing bulletproof vests with the Blacklight Biotechnology logo printed on it. They swarmed out of the building with batons and explosion-proof shields and charged past Lane as they went after the group of protestors dressed shabbily in common clothes...

The following scene went just about as one would expect.

Lane paid no further mind to the commotion. He took a deep breath and made sure he looked presentable before he wheeled the wheelchair through the main entrance of the Blackwatch headquarters.

Upon entry, he saw the enormous lobby that was unlike the lobby of any other common office buildings. A stupendous amount of effort clearly went into the renovation of the lobby. The floors were clad with black marble tiles, the furnishing was mostly silver color and looked highly futuristic, the table and chairs were clean, transparent, and made of glass. The atmosphere of the lobby gave a powerful postmodernism impression.

Lane became slightly nervous and took a large gulp. When he approached the counter, the staff at the counter greeted him with a smile that could wipe away a person’s worries. “Welcome, honored guest. I’m Android 3199 and will be your receptionist for the day, how can I help you?”

Lane realized the identity of this lady upon hearing this greeting. He shot a look of slight amusement at the receptionist and asked, “Hi, I’m here for the application to become a combative police.”

With that, he handed the application form over.

The Android lady raised both her hands and politely accepted Lane’s form.

After a quick round of observation, Lane quickly noticed that although the movements of this lady in front of him were smooth, it was not completely natural. Her movements were almost mechanical and did not contain the hint of fluidity in a regular person’s movements.

Despite this observation, what he was looking at was rather impressive already. Unless one were to pay extra attention, it would be hard to distinguish the difference between humans and Androids at first glance.

The lady receptionist only glanced at the form briefly before looking up again and informed with a warm smile, “Dear Mr. Lane, welcome to the application for Blackwatch’s Combative Police Force. We at Blackwatch are now collaborating with the Northern American District Police Department, I will now enter your information into the company’s intranet and the New York City Police Department database. This is your number. Please head to the second floor for registration, be sure to queue...”

With that, the lady stretched out her hand again and handed a silver electronic card to Leon.

“Alright, thank you...”

Lane nodded and accepted the electronic card. He unintentionally grazed against the slender fingers of the receptionist while receiving the card.

The lady receptionist’s skin was warm and had a tender texture one would come to expect of human skin. Lane had the faint impression that he was touching a real human...

Androids did not have any self-consciousness so the girl did not respond to Lane’s actions. It only smiled and watched as Lane wheeled off in a wheelchair.

Upon arriving at the second floor and exiting the elevator, Lane saw applicants sitting in rows upon rows of sofa lined along the corridor. Some of them wore bionic prosthetics while the less fortunate ones like himself who could not afford prosthetics had to be bound to a wheelchair.

These were the disabled members of society also here to apply to be a part of Blackwatch’s Combative Police Force.

All major cities in the world that had an established Blacklight Biotechnology branch company also had a branch of the combative police force that was tasked with maintaining the order of the city.

The combative police department was a part public and part private department. Any members enlisted into the department would take on two roles, one of which was a police officer of the city while the other was as an employee of Blackwatch.

This job was an excellent deal for the disabled, but unfortunately, not everyone could join the combative police department. Needless to be said, the first prerequisite was to be a disabled person, it was also ideal to come from a police or military background. It was not a deal-breaker to not come from such backgrounds, but unless you were very physically gifted or trained in combat, the chances of being selected were bleak.

Lane picked a corner and waited silently. He looked at the electronic display mounted to the wall. It was flashing with the notice “New York City Bionic Combative Police Audition” with a number below it. The display currently showed the number 21.

Lane took a look at the silver-plated card given to him by the receptionist, it read 39.

Hmm, looked like there were still eighteen people ahead of him, better wait patiently...

Lane put on earphones and immersed himself into his world.

After approximately half an hour had passed. Lane took a look at the number in the office again and saw that it now read 25. He frowned upon noting this. Fortunately, there was an Android waitress serving water and offering diligent service for the applicants waiting here.

Lane could not help but silently reflect on how the Androids developed by Blackwatch were the perfect choice to fill a position like a nanny that required one to be hardworking, attentive, and respectful. All that and no slacking off.

Whether it was by intention or not, one clear observation that could be made was that these Androids were designed to be very attractive. Any one of them could pose as a supermodel celebrity in real life. Apart from the occasional awkward movements that outed them as Androids, there was pretty much no other tell...

Lane had also heard the occasional remark from his Internet friends that these Androids had spectacular physical assets as well. They could easily pass as premium inflatable toys in bed...

After three hours had passed and it was finally Lane’s turn.

The first thing Lane saw upon pushing the door open was four people dressed in suits in a dimly lit office. One of them was wearing a bionic prosthesis while the other three were perfectly healthy and normal people. There was a plate in front of each one of them that read chief examiner.

“Hello examiners, my name is Pedro Lane. I was born in Rhode Island and I’m here to apply to become a Bionic Combative Police today.”

Lane bowed lightly on his wheelchair and announced softly.

“Disabled legs?”

The man sitting on the far right frowned briskly and asked without making an attempt to be sensitive about the matter, “Lane, how did your legs come to be disabled? Was it due to an injury inflicted by criminals?”

“No, I lost them in an accident.”

Before he could finish his sentence, the same man interrupted him. “Was your previous profession associated with the police or military force?”

“Neither.”

Lane shook his head and put on his smile. “I was a university student and I’ve taken several elective courses, I performed quite well.”

The four examiners exchanged glances among themselves when they heard this. The only man with bionic prosthetics suddenly asked, “Tell us about your understanding of ‘justice’, also, what are your opinions about bionic combative police as a profession?”

“I think the definition of justice is to crack down on all evil before they can hatch!”

Lane answered promptly, “My dream is to become a superhero. The way I see it, justice is straightforward and doesn’t come with any external factors or influences. Any justice motivated by other selfish desires cannot be called justice.

“As for my opinions on bionic combative police as a profession, I don’t see them as true heroes because true heroes don’t demand the attention and idolization of the public. I see them as a farce because true heroes are nobodies that...”

This remark caught the attention of the other two examiners. They raised their heads in unison and looked at Lane strangely. After that, they held a whispered discussion.

Before long, the man with bionic prosthetics turned to Lane and nodded. “Mr. Lane, thank you for your application. You may leave now. We’ll inform you by email if you’ve been shortlisted.”

“Alright...”

Lane smiled bitterly and nodded. He gave the examiners one last polite bow before leaving.

By the time Lane exited the Blackwatch building, the protest outside had long died down. The LED screen on the front of the building had been fixed as well. It was playing the BHB Combative Police Force commercial again.

Lane could not fight the urge to look back again. When he saw the man in the middle of the BHB team shown on the display, a faint wipe of what seemed to be hatred appeared in his eyes...

It was at this moment when a figure came to a stop in front of Lane. He turned around and saw a large, empowering man standing in front of him.

“Lane, did you get recruited?”

“No, I think... I failed.”

chapters.title: Chapter 490: Revenge Squad
chapters.body:
Chapter 490: Revenge Squad

Chapter 490: Revenge Squad

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

“This person has interesting ideas...”

After they watched Lane leave in a wheelchair, the muscular middle-aged man whose right arm was a bionic prosthetic commented suddenly.

“He doesn’t come from a police or military background and his degree of physical disability isn’t severe enough either.”

Another blonde-haired lady noted without hesitation. “People like him aren’t suitable candidates.”

“I think so too.”

The man who cut off Lane mid-sentence just now agreed with a firm nod. “The key factor is the ideas he has. He’s simply too rebellious for our company’s cause. I’d advise against putting him through the preliminary selection.”

“Seconded, let’s move on from this one.”

The final examiner agreed as well.

With that, three out of the four examiners had selected the FAILED input on their computers.

It was only the middle-aged man who spoke first left. He held his mouth open and darted a hesitant look at the other three examiners as he looked at the screen displayed in front of him.

Name: Pedro lane.

Age: 26.

Physical Condition Rating: C.

Political Mindset Rating: C.

Mental Health Rating: B.

Overview:

After some consideration, he proceeded to write down several sentences in Leon’s evaluation form.

“The applicant displays a strong sense of justice and personality as well as strong self-awareness. I believe he’s an ideal observation target, awaiting further review.”

After providing this evaluation, the middle-aged man gave Lane the only PASSED review among the four examiners and promptly closed the window.

The rest of the examiners did not raise an issue with the middle-aged man’s review. They were the staff in charge of the preliminary screening. The information would then be handed over to the higher-ups for another round of sampling review. There was a chance that the final ruling may be revised, though it was very unlikely.

It was very rare for candidates that received a FAILED score from three examiners to have their final ruling revised and invited to the next round of screening.

The blonde-haired lady gave the middle-aged man a gentle nod before pressing the button to summon the next candidate. Soon, another candidate afflicted with frostbite came into the room in a wheelchair.

The previous segment was nothing too notable. The screening process for the combative police force was still ongoing...

Unbeknownst to the examiners, after Lane’s information was submitted into the system’s back-end database, something strange happened to it. Perhaps due to a system error or some similar issue, it was transferred from the group labeled as “FAILED” to the group labeled as “PASSED”.

In addition, the “FAILED” grading marked in red that was issued by the three examiners was somehow modified and turned into a bright green “PASSED”...

...

“Bang!”

There was a loud and sudden slam. A large and muscular man wearing a west who was built like an Olympian burst through the metal gate with a relentless kick as he stomped into the dark and damp garage.

The interior of the garage was a mess. Bits and pieces of unknown engine parts and wire were scattered all over the floor. There was also an off-road vehicle parked in the corner of the garage.

On the other side of the garage, there was a cluster of five to six server machines. The heat they radiated made the garage feel stuffy while the low hum of the machines echoed across the garage endlessly.

A black young man who was tweaking a device suddenly snapped around upon hearing the loud bang and took out a gun which he aimed at the door. When he identified the person who came in through the entrance, he cursed out loud and went back to the device he was working on.

“Wildfire, does it kill you to open a door normally?”

Meanwhile, another figure stepped outside gracefully from a dark corner of the garage. It was a lady with pink-colored hair that draped over one side of her face. She was chewing gum and she spoke with a soft, sultry voice, “What pissed you off so much, hold on, let me guess...”

The lady narrowed her eyes as she fixed her gaze on the figure behind the man in the vest. It was Lane wheeling himself in his wheelchair. “Did Lamb screw up the application process into the Blackwatch Combative Police Force?”

“Right on the mark, Kraken!”

The man in the vest ranted with great irritation, “Lane never follows through with the plan that we devised for him, what a pain!”

The man in the vest turned and angrily jabbed a finger at Lane’s direction. “Do you have any idea how much we’ve done for you to take care of your wounds, how much time and effort went into it? You’d never believe it even if I told you!”

“I’m so sorry, Wildfire...”

Lane said with a bitter frown, “It won’t happen again.”

“Of course it won’t because there’s no more next time!”

Wildfire roared and darted into a room at the other end of the garage.
